# Sunday, July 13, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1787, Congress passed the [Northwest Ordinance](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO6yAQRb8Gd7ZgDHFSULzGXV67pYVhiGeFIYKJrfz9KvsL293bnCOdr_tyd5QoP_67HS3HsmQ8W0JmrJ_JrlKMzeVcXtljeG7VNVxAgunl1KtxCY7Se9FdsBERcOzQqsmAlkqB7rbPiVfUyoBWzly8uSgEGVzU3ntp1o7shyYnNSopjboNZl2lukU1uptcr2CElhiISx2-C2XeMKZ3ObAOvuxdshvzs4nxn4BZwHye5-Cq3-jANjzKIWDeKWHjkrEPxb92zNwEzLlU3k5s3JcaKLvssas2PE9KidplElo-dkfp18J_SnNY-AkAAP__h6x54Q), establishing governance for the Northwest Territory, setting a path for new states, and resolving state land claims to preserve unity under the Articles of Confederation. Researchers have determined that 30 days of [mindfulness meditation](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHO4yAQhJ8Gd45gATsuKK5xl2uvtNZmifeEIQKSKG__y3mFv_uK0Tea-XdbbsiR0_0vHuRayEuid43UGpUTGxYOoWJK-Zk28o-9YKUFJNhejr3Si0eOn8V03gUiIN2RU6MFI5UC0-1ulUYN4arIbnrESa9XD8OwoZ9QIljZsTttclRaSWnVdLHrKtUUlMZJrlewwkjy3HK5_M-c2k4hfvKLymXLRxfd3tqjCv1HwCxgTvQsuW5MaaNzyhkSMB-cfHjGRLX2x2nDxjn12BqlL8Fk1Chg7orzjzfHyHUYhZH3Azl-m9qv7nk5-AkAAP__LvR6TQ) at any stage of life improves reaction speed, focus, and resistance to distractions. Today's sponsor is [Raisin;](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkMmK6zAQRb9G3jlosDwstHgQvMvb9tLIVtmutAajISF_37gJ5AN6V9SBU3Xv1226abTot__agcprmDw8k4WcIZ5j1hHXNWnvQ_ELmGOPOsHEKZc17WomJqPRvqamMmoF4CAqUKyTvKGM8abaVd8PVIhhpmbpOwFdq4U03bqw1gBIaSpUp412TDBKJRsucp4pG1Ym9EDnnkvSUDCYQ7zcA_q8w2pf4QHxsgRXWbXnfCQi_hE-Ej5GjQn9iQgfwdclET6mI6Lf6jn4kuo5hJSJGEt2kwODxRFx_aQmvD1JCiUuQMT1c-9NFu0OjZsn4novHrh87zPE0zTbsHyzKipzPNFaTG1HGro5jfb34fynlh-K_wQAAP__d6WTmA) unlock their access to high-yield savings accounts and make your money work for you.
|
---
## Trump Places 30% Tariffs on EU, Mexico
President Trump announced on Saturday that he will [levy tariffs of 30%](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL-OszAQxJ8GOqLdxWBcuPiadPnaK5H_rINPBiPjJJe3P3G6N7huRiPN6Dcft_lmYorb_b9ZWdeQ541fR-JauZyymhJDOMy25cfm2O9LMQfPBDR0IDvsZ29ies-i9TowE_cta5QDCUAk0S5aCQ9ukGYUnlG5iRyYoKQUQEBoQxv12QYSewQYUF0GawFVwN4osBMNjQD2seZy-cxxqwuH9M5PLheX1zbppdb9aPp_DV0bupr9BDij05QaXeKGrrU81r37pen40a38FV3uxtGGSXglvfOjstZjQISJBdHUm-Dbov3-iinFY5SNgPtqYvoZrn9666npOwAA__8eDH0A) against the European Union and Mexico beginning August 1. In a letter to Mexico’s President Claudia Sheinbaum posted on social media, Trump acknowledged that the country has helped stem the flow of illegal migrants and fentanyl into the U.S. But he said Mexico has not done enough to stop North America from turning into a [“ _Narco-Trafficking Playground._ ”](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL_u3CAQhJ8Gd5yWxQRfQZGG7pc2pcUd6zMRBgvW9-ftI0fJE6QbaaRP883Pr_krpJzK40fYyPFS50KvnomZ2hk5tLQsPZRSj3KnuK8tdJoR0EiwUuk5hpQ_8zhEtxAh6YGcsgZHUArHYXVoLCnSIU5G06StHmGioKZRARkLcUjupIFVWgEYdb2Y2w3UdVE6XOE2oREjUExc2-VXTYVXWvKnPqld7nUbsluZ9y70d4FeoC-fvXY-K4H-BAv0YAV6hQL90eXpJ9BzO7Zd_vPqUoP86yqX2uRG73SvMpQo6ZCdQ-NUHrLQm-VWC68C_dBc3F8p59S_WTHCYwsp_xnF__Xk0-HvAAAA__9nQoaT) Mexico has been the [top trading partner](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOszAQhJ_GdI7WaxygcPE3dPnbK5ExS9iTsZFtksvbn3K6N7hupJFm9H0ft-nmOHC8_3c72bqmKdKzBKqV8jtWl3ldi4sxndHTcmzZFZoQ0EjopNLT4ji8prZZ7EqEpBuyqjPYglLYNpsFrVSnqCdCJOxm1Zree2O8J_B-uDZs32vQKa0AjBouZp5BDavSboC5RyNaoIVrypfPxLFutIZXelC--LQ3wW61HkXofwJHgWPdaOMQ3p3AcT4LRypF4Gj00MMwyCVFFxZZ87kf8hdP7vTFPkk6czrIRXlGTlFqkAdlT7EKHJtsl-PJIXC5dqKF--7456apf_L2sPgdAAD__8AZgic) with the U.S. this year. EU members Germany, Italy, France, and the Netherlands are among the top 15. With Saturday’s announcements, Trump has now issued tariff conditions on 24 countries and the 27-member EU.
## Swiatek Dominates Wimbledon Final
Poland’s Iga Swiatek earned [her first Wimbledon title](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFutDAQhJ_GdEb22vwchYu_obu0KdGCl2MTYyPsnHVvH3GvkG5GI40-fZ_36Y4cOD4-cCdX1jRFqjlQKXReseDJ65oxxvQTF_LHdmKmCRR0UvVSm8kjh9dkG-9WIiDTkNN9B1ZpDbbZnKKhv_nFL1abDoy6KaO1t_OgrKVF64bd9aZ6bbRSnR7abp6VHlZtcFDzDTphFXku6Wy_Esey0Rpe6Ulnu6S9CW4r5cjC_BcwChhrrS3lI16jgLFQjJwFjLmk8yVgnASM7AWMtuvBwqCv_kCZK2Ohb-nTzhELZYk7Ro8SI2fe0xNl5Sgr73Mgn6J8KyhcAjWn80flEDj_64VVjx05vOHKn4w-HfwGAAD__-qQiZE) and sixth major championship on Saturday, overwhelming American Amanda Anisimova 6-0, 6-0. It was the first women's final at the tournament in 114 years in which one player failed to claim a single game. The 24-year-old Swiatek is [6-0 in major title matches](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO2zAQRL9G6miQS_EYFyzSqLu0KYWVubQ2oUiD3Ji4vw_sX7higCkGg5n3-3P7RM5c7r_wpCCpboVGzyRC7WUFG6fUsZT6r9woPo6GnTbQ4JT2ytgtIuevbZljSERAdqZgvINFGwPLfATvKEIE0BaTJwLnwN7srvfkvTPRzBxebdoba7R25npx-67NNRmLV73_ADctmiJLbZc_lYsclPJXfVK73Oo553CIPPpkf06wTrCOMS5lv_VHbdJfiQlWoVK4T7C-vk2w8h1VH4xCf1WkRChd4YklosLCnc_6RPWh9FtS1eCiDmoqceuiBp97pliLEpZMcwvxMThn7h9-WvT9RM7vafItns8A_wMAAP__8T2LmA). She won 55 of Saturday’s 79 points, while Anisimova was shaky from the start and made 28 unforced errors. Reaching the final was a full-circle moment for Anisimova, who [took a mental health break](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DtuwzAQBNDTSJ2E5epDu2CRxp3TphRW0tJkwI9Abmz49oFyhXSvGMwA83Vf7uSDT49PimzE5iXxqwYW4XJSqHhrK6WUf9LG--EKVV4QcOpAd2pYdvLhvYztbiwz8tCyUXrCEZTCsXUG1YizBmu1BtT7wOtmx3mbL2iBNzW33pxtoNWgACZ17ad1BXW1aqArrBecmhF495JL_519Esc2vPOTS7_l2AbjRI7aDB8N3hq81SMXqf2bXM5noMEbFfFb4FOR0k4dJR99zU_qIieh0DmmIK6jyMVvlDrQA4DCWfdOYmiL2Y-XD8HXWTcjPCL58Dcu_3rsafA3AAD__9Wkf_o) from the tour two years ago after playing in tournaments became _"unbearable."_ She previously was a semifinalist at age 17 at the 2019 French Open.
## List: Ranking Recession-Proof States
Florida is ranked as the best-positioned state to thrive during a possible recession, [according to a CNBC study](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OhCAUhZ8GOwwX8YeCYhu72XZLg3pRdhEMlxkzb79xX2Gbk5Oc5MvJ9_WYHtYHH7dPe6ApLk0RLwpYCua7Fpu9c2RjTM-44Hru2RJOUsiWi55DM63Wh_ekqtU4RIlNhQb6VioBIFW1G1AKNKiuwUEAdA5QazmoYVDzrDttK29umuihASFa0HU7zwK0g8ZqMQ-yZUrg6kvK9Xfysezowju9MNdLOqpg9lJOYs0HkyOT43Vd9RLn5R6ZHG80k6PomRxB3iE4lZzihlT4kzguKabDI_GMCxL5FHlJJ6diCxJ3KfP5ST4iEc82_vi4Ub2XI1TZrOflQ_DU9UyJ7bA-_F0q__L4MvI3AAD__y_HhnQ). The study considered factors such as economic growth and state finances but also the potential risk each state faces from higher tariffs and lower federal spending. The Sunshine State boasts solid economic and job growth and remains a leader in new business formations. Texas, with a wealth of foreign direct investment, is ranked second. Among the states [most vulnerable to an economic downturn](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFutDAQhJ_GdEb2AsdP4eJv6C5tSmSb9eHE2Mi7d-jePiKvkGY00kifRt_nfbnbmGJ-fNgdDYeyZDwpITPWq7KtMQSyOZdn9rgeW7WECygYpBql7pbVxvRe-mY1ARGwa9DocYBeaQ19sxl9m6ahCxNqdN6rcfLWWd_pMCgIGFwTzUVTo-60UoOe2sE5paegOzsp9w8G0StcI5fafpWYecOQ3uWFtfVlb5LZmA8S3X8Bs4D5PM_WZ-evUcB8oQXMahQwa7hCyRPtNxLLJ0n0JZc9IsmKHoliyZLLIYktI8lQqnRPihmJ2o331FSzHmdMKdJtFL167Dam3x_8J3kvAz8BAAD__ztzg8M) are Alaska, Mississippi, and Kansas.

**Good Morning!** On this day in 1903, a proclamation was issued from the steps of the Texas Capitol offering a $50,000 reward for the discovery of a way to [rid Texas of the boll weevil](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2OqzAQAODTmC5o_EMIhYvX0OW1WyIbD8HKYEf2CCe3X-1eYS_wFd_Xfbm7SDE9_rsDLb-3vCRslZAZy9IQn5hC5VyO-ioY4soY1nwsCtRwgfEi9RJcpM9iumA9hEl2aOU4KAPG3K7dbiccRnW7uTFIbTwCahm08xrM5r0bTBftDwaj1FKCVNCvEgD1OmxKT5NzUhjAEDmXnndkfLu60SefWPo1Hx3ZnflVhf4n1CzU3Frrue4uJ4oJ-1weQs27S8Hn_BRqxsQlYhVq9pno0hDPSF2x4dUiUazXURh4HC7SL89_Szmt-g4AAP__ILF3mg), an invasive beetle native to Mexico that threatened the state’s cotton industry. ## Texas Braces for More Heavy Rainfall
Gov. Greg Abbott mobilized [state emergency teams on Friday](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLFuhDAQRL_GdD7ZxkAoXKShu7Qp0WIvYMVg5N077v4-Ir-QbqTRjGbe9328Q0xxX75gQ8evOY87npSQGct4Iv7gHohz2egoGKJnDD5vo1GmkaqTuh4DxPQebRXcpEKvK3S6a4xV1n601eqazs6zNn0deh9gauqmn6cWvLcttmiaKrqrTHW61lppo25eK4W1b2ZT9z2AFlZhiJzLjVdkfAHN6Z2fWG4-b1VyK_NBov4UZhBm8BPBgzjulyvMcN0RZkjZQxJmWK7gnouEacrMEjzHJzCSJAZGiRuWBXf_lgXpyDvhJfKjeCQJK0KQeZYnAq9YJK8FgakqLhxnTClS2wmrlg1i-lvH_2P6dOY3AAD__4grkFU) to counter severe storms and potential flash flooding threatening millions across Texas this weekend. Heavy rainfall [capable of causing flash flooding](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLFuwyAURb8Gb44eDxzbA0MXb-na0cLwiFGxieApTv6-atp-Qeere67u-bjMFxtT3K_vdiPDj5DnnY6aiJnKfBB90u4r57LVWyEfHZN3eZsRsGuhb6WavY3pOevGmwX8KBsysu9Qg9bDuVmNhGV0zmOvdKcDyMVrF2BQsFgYZDc00XzDoJdKSpAIJycBSLkuoBpHa6XQQD5yLideielha0jPfKdycnlrklmZb1WoN4GTwOkgy-tPJnAKuZCzlQVOha4x7zYJnO7RUxY4bblQW2zc25By9u0L3v6-bv-6TTH-dsSUYj33QsN1szG9tvl_xu4GvwIAAP__zwaBvA) has been forecast for northwest, west, and north Texas, the Big Country, Permian Basin, Concho Valley, and the Hill Country. Storms are expected to arrive between 1 p.m. Saturday and 1 p.m. Sunday. The Texas Division of Emergency Management has deployed swift water boat squads and medical support, with personnel ready to respond.
## Texas Ranked the 2nd Best for Business
Texas generated [$2.7 trillion in economic output](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2O6jAQxfGncbqg8VcSChe3Scdtt4yMPSGjdWxkzxJ4-xW8wpZH-utXnK_LcvGUKN_--x0dP9eyZDxaQmasy4H4jTk2LnVv94qRAmMMZV8UKNvD2Eu9RE_ptZguuivEs-zQydEqA8ZMQ7e5COo6wGT1NAazGqtXKQc_WbAjIkxrR-6NwSi1lCAVnIIEQB3sqvT57L0UBjASl3riDRmfvq3pVR5YT6HsXXIb870J_U-oWaj5-tMoY2uUP-m7EWo-tlf_2ULNGEouO4W-ccV8402ouasu3g9KidowCgO33VP6-Py3Vx5O_QYAAP__FCN3cw) last year but did not rank first in CNBC’s America’s Top States for Business. The Lone Star State ranked No. 2 in [a 50-state analysis](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2OpDAQBOCnMZlRu_kxBA4uIdtLL0T-aQZrDUZ277Lz9qeZV9i4pE9V9e9j_bAxxfPx1x5k-GfL60l3TcRMZb2JPukMlXM56lUoRM8UfD5WBBwkaKm6NdiYnmvfBOMgzKoho_SAPfT9NDa70WMPbsIhzHoGt2kM04CuczhNrtPj0ETzwkCrTilQCK1XANT5YcNunq1VogcKkXNpeSemH1u39MzfVFqfjyaZnfmqovsjcBG43Pfd-tP5VyhwedkCF9ACFwUCF86XrGyZqtxyke6rxpNqlfagEr2t8j2Nd5LbV0qy2PMzno_a7nykpphw3TGlWEctengcNqZ3C_7dd98G_wcAAP__pxWB8A) measuring 135 metrics across 10 competitiveness categories, trailing North Carolina and ahead of Florida, Virginia, and Ohio. Texas ranked first in workforce and second in economy, technology, and innovation, but ranked 26th in infrastructure and 49th in quality of life. You can see the full analysis [here](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1ugzAUxfGnMRvo-gOIBw9d2NK1IzL2JVgxNrJvQ_L2VfIKnY_0O9L_5zpfbYgh3b7tjoaea54TnjUiEZb5RLxj8pVy2etR0AdH6F3eZwGib2FsuZy9DfE1q8abBbzmDRo-9kKBUpeh2YzW2g1er4DYq-UyylUNOLhBLiNIHEQTzBuDkUvOgQvoHAdA6fpVSK2t5UwB-kC5dLQh4dPWNb7yA0vn8t5EsxEdlckvJiYmpvM8O5cW9x6ZmN42ExOMTEwcmJg-QEv5aCtZwtquubTLbw0Ja22LTfeQbt1Ge2yK8ccZYgx1GJmC225D_HzS_0o9jPgLAAD__6axfLQ).
## Heroic Stories Emerge from Flooding
A teenage girl who [gave her life to save her family](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDuO8yAUhVeDOyLuxY-4oPgbd_nbKS0CFxsNGAuYON79yNnCtOdI33l8PeaH9sFvy38dSdW3S_NGRwlUK-X5IPqmzZaacix7JutNJWtSnFFgx8XAQc5W-3DObWPVU9gRGlIwdNiKtr33zaok6N6NaEawg5MjiHvXAhore2vEU1Lj1QUTA0gAAShuBoQgaTqHchy1BtYKsr6mfKsrVXrr4sKZXpRvJsUmqLXWvTD5j-HEcNrOPZV6WQyni8xwEgPDCYDh9FP4tY_hVIk2vRBffA7cerK86JffFr5S5k5HH07ucoo86-WSP8HchZTsoSvli9FkZffDh-BLP7BWLFH78GlV__blS-FvAAAA__-8L4is) during last weekend's deadly flash flooding is one of many inspiring, courageous stories to emerge from the tragedy. Hammond was on her way to a Christian summer camp with her family at 5 a.m. on Saturday, when they drove off a flood-covered bridge, sweeping the family's van into the floodwaters near Marble Falls. Hammond bravely managed to open the door of the van, saving her parents and two younger siblings, but was swept away by the floodwaters and found two days later. "_As the eldest, Malaya set the standard. Ask anyone who knows her, and they 'll tell you: Malaya is one of the most gentle, compassionate young women they've ever met,_" said a family friend. On this day in 1903, a proclamation was issued from the steps of the Texas Capitol offering a $50,000 reward for the discovery of a way to , an invasive beetle native to Mexico that threatened the state’s cotton industry. One of country music’s biggest stars has announced a charity concert to support victims of last weekend’s floods, featuring several special guest performances. Tune in to The Flyover Podcast! New episodes drop daily—watch on YouTube or listen on Spotify and Apple Podcasts, all in under 15 minutes. | | Texas Braces for More Heavy Rainfall Gov. Greg Abbott mobilized to counter severe storms and potential flash flooding threatening millions across Texas this weekend. Heavy rainfall has been forecast for northwest, west, and north Texas, the Big Country, Permian Basin, Concho Valley, and the Hill Country. Storms are expected to arrive between 1 p.m. Saturday and 1 p.m. Sunday. The Texas Division of Emergency Management has deployed swift water boat squads and medical support, with personnel ready to respond. Texas Ranked the 2nd Best for Business Texas generated last year but did not rank first in CNBC’s America’s Top States for Business. The Lone Star State ranked No. 2 in measuring 135 metrics across 10 competitiveness categories, trailing North Carolina and ahead of Florida, Virginia, and Ohio. Texas ranked first in workforce and second in economy, technology, and innovation, but ranked 26th in infrastructure and 49th in quality of life. You can see the full analysis . Heroic Stories Emerge from Flooding A teenage girl who during last weekend's deadly flash flooding is one of many inspiring, courageous stories to emerge from the tragedy. Hammond was on her way to a Christian summer camp with her family at 5 a.m. on Saturday, when they drove off a flood-covered bridge, sweeping the family's van into the floodwaters near Marble Falls. Hammond bravely managed to open the door of the van, saving her parents and two younger siblings, but was swept away by the floodwaters and found two days later. "As the eldest, Malaya set the standard. Ask anyone who knows her, and they 'll tell you: Malaya is one of the most gentle, compassionate young women they've ever met," said a family friend. subject: Biden's Doctor Takes Fifth in Capitol Inquiry
body:
Hey folks, In a recent twist on Capitol Hill, Joe Biden’s doctor, Kevin O’Connor, decided not to spill the beans during a big meeting with Congress. He took what we call the Fifth Amendment, a fancy legal way of staying quiet to keep from getting into trouble. This meeting was all about figuring out if [...] | [](http://trk.faithandlibertymail._poDKDJLfXe45A)

name: Phil Izon
subject: Thank You, just getting started
body:
Please share with friends and family | | | [](https://v8k6.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=6873945fd9b4250ce0609dff&u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.tropictundra.com%2F%3Fss_source%3Dsscampaigns%26ss_campaign_id%3D68739415eb90c70f96bb5c76%26ss_email_id%3D6873945fd9b4250ce0609dff%26ss_campaign_name%3DThank%2BYou%252C%2Bjust%2Bgetting%2Bstarted%26ss_campaign_sent_date%3D2025-07-13T11%253A11%253A40Z&w=658ac75703428575b106784b&c=b_68739415eb90c70f96bb5c76&l=en-US&s=qY4nbk1Cr3mkODQBIgGDvIV1vYo%3D)
---
| When I first embarked on the effort to repeal Ranked Choice Voting (RCV) in Alaska, I must confess, I did not fully comprehend the intricate and relentless nature of the battle ahead. However, from the outset, one principle remained clear: I would not stand idly by and permit our electoral process to be contorted into a confusing, frustrating system that leaves voters feeling disempowered and uncertain about the efficacy of their participation.Comprehensive EngagementMy commitment did not stop in Alaska. I authored three books “Field Manual To RCV,” “manufactured Majority,” and “Multiple Choice Voting Systems” meticulously exposing the truth about RCV and have distributed thousands of copies across the United States, entirely free of charge. Furthermore, I produced a thirty-part animated video series, designed to illuminate the dangers of RCV in clear, accessible language, enabling voters nationwide to grasp the unfolding complexities. I also gave out tens of thousands of free videos to groups in 7 states. I have invested countless hours in Zoom calls, phone calls, and strategic planning sessions with grassroots leaders across the country. Indeed, entire organizations actively combating RCV today have publicly acknowledged my role in their foundational efforts.This fight is far from concluded. I have already extended these efforts to Michigan, Connecticut and other states now confronting the same threat. My websites,[ http://RCValert.org](https://v8k6.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=6873945fd9b4250ce0609dff&u=https%3A%2F%2Ft.co%2FEFfWGMvo3Y&w=658ac75703428575b106784b&c=b_68739415eb90c70f96bb5c76&l=en-US&s=YIpHmOAwuRsBpnJ3W0xleAvDDQ0%3D) and[ http://RemoveRCV.com](https://v8k6.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=6873945fd9b4250ce0609dff&u=https%3A%2F%2Ft.co%2F7UE3tPiQgU&w=658ac75703428575b106784b&c=b_68739415eb90c70f96bb5c76&l=en-US&s=Xh8J1hUJV7fRWwsicNep0pkxU6I%3D), are comprehensive repositories of years of research, invaluable resources, and interactive tools, including the RCV Election Simulators and Resource Guides. All of these resources are meticulously designed to demonstrate to the public the inherent dangers and confusions embedded within this farse of an electoral system (RCV).A Personal ImperativeFor me, this endeavor has never been driven by mere political ambition or personal gain. It has always been deeply personal, originating within my own family. My grandfather, a good, hardworking man and a small business owner found himself utterly unable to comprehend the ranked choice ballot. If a man of his extensive experience, profound wisdom, and inherent common sense (He will tell you all about it), was confounded by the process, I recognized that a fundamental flaw existed. That realization marked the pivotal moment I committed myself, drafting Alaska's repeal initiative from my own living room.And I would be remiss not to acknowledge the indispensable role of my greatest supporter and closest confidante, my wife. She stood by my side and was my copilot, enduring Alaskan blizzards and brutal winter storms (also saved us from hitting a moose on our way back from Fairbanks during the blizzard by being an amazing copilot), unwavering in her determination to see this through. Her unwavering dedication and profound strength were a constant source of inspiration and resolve. She was able to see me on the court stand while Kendall & Co. (Attorneys) grilled me for nearly two hours and 6 hours of deposition prior about my handling of the petition. She would smile and give me a break from the disrespectful and stressful tone of the day. Her smile carried me through the tough times.We did not possess the financial backing of billionaires. We were not directed by political parties or high-priced corporate consultants. What we possessed was unwavering determination, diligent hard work, and an unshakeable conviction that Alaskans deserve fair and transparent elections. More than 700 dedicated volunteers, everyday Alaskans, rallied with me. We canvassed neighborhoods, knocking on countless doors. We stood steadfastly in the bitter cold. At one particular event, we successfully gathered over one thousand signatures in a single day. Another event was so extraordinarily crowded that the parking lot overflowed, compelling some individuals to depart. One of the parts of the court challenge was that Phil Izon couldn’t gather 500+ signatures in a single day, well I did and I had video evidence of me collecting signatures with Gov. Sarah Palin ([@SarahPalinUSA](https://v8k6.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=6873945fd9b4250ce0609dff&u=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2FSarahPalinUSA&w=658ac75703428575b106784b&c=b_68739415eb90c70f96bb5c76&l=en-US&s=-F4Adxzdulw49pNe1G5gv8B2D4g%3D) ) at the Palmer Train depot.Ultimately, we collected over 42,000 signatures, significantly exceeding the required threshold, achieved entirely without assistance from political insiders or billionaires.A National ImperativeDuring the signature gathering, I also traveled across the country, consistently raising the alarm about the profound implications of Ranked Choice Voting. I steadfastly refused to remain on the sidelines while this complex, manipulative system quietly infiltrated state after state, meticulously funded by out-of-state billionaires and political operatives who fully understood the disruptive potential they were unleashing.My efforts have extended to states such as Idaho, Oregon, Arizona, Missouri, Hawaii, Florida, Montana, California, Connecticut, Colorado, California, Nevada, Rhode Island, and numerous others. I have presented at GOP conventions, multiple events in Oregon, Arizona, and Hawaii, and dedicated time at our booth at CPAC in Washington, D.C., giving away educational materials and issuing warnings regarding the impending electoral shifts.I went on to debate 3 Alaska Legislators, 3 Oregon Legislators, The “Father” of RCV in Oakland and San Francisco (Steve Chessin), and finally U.S. Presidential Candidate Chase Oliver. I do not think anyone has debated the topic of RCV at the levels that I have. Fun thing I also was able to meet Gov. Desantis ([@GovRonDeSantis](https://v8k6.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=6873945fd9b4250ce0609dff&u=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2FGovRonDeSantis&w=658ac75703428575b106784b&c=b_68739415eb90c70f96bb5c76&l=en-US&s=xwgFjANw20iqVtWUCuWsBIDWH-0%3D) ) when he was also running for president, I was able to thank him for being the first Governor to ban RCV in America. I also gave him a copy of my book.Confronting the OppositionAs anticipated, the political establishment employed every conceivable tactic to impede our progress. The very attorney responsible for introducing Ranked Choice Voting to Alaska, Scott Kendall, subsequently filed a lawsuit against the State in an attempt to prevent our repeal initiative from even appearing on the ballot. It is a remarkable irony that the individuals who engineered the system and used the citizen initiative process to bring RCV to Alaska sought to undermine the right of Alaskan citizens to petition their government.However, we did not quit. We vigorously pursued our case in court, and decisively, we prevailed. We achieved victory in the Alaska Superior Court. They appealed to Alaska Supreme Court. We achieved victory in the Alaska Supreme Court. And subsequent to these legal triumphs, they ultimately settled with us for attorney fees ($50,000). To be clear, the court did not issue an order for them to cover our legal fees, but as an integral component of the settlement, they agreed to do so. Subsequently, he requested a confidentiality agreement, hoping that we would remain silent about our victory. We declined. While we agreed to the settlement, we adamantly refused to relinquish our right to openly discuss our accomplishments and were willing to pursue the case further if necessary.They outspent us by a staggering margin of one hundred to one. Millions of dollars flowed in from external organizations, backed by millionaires and billionaires, originating from New York, California, Virginia, and Washington, D.C., all desperately endeavoring to maintain their control over Alaska's elections. Groups such as Unite America, Action Now Initiative, and the Sixteen Thirty Fund, supported by prominent donors like George Soros and Hansjorg Wyss (Foreign National Billionaire), expended nearly 15 Million Dollars defending that flawed system, having initially spent ($7 Million) seven million to force its adoption in 2020.The Ongoing StruggleWhen Election Day finally arrived, I was not merely hopeful; I was profoundly proud. Not only had I dedicated years of my life to combating RCV here in Alaska, but I had expanded that fight nationwide. On that same day, seven other states where I had actively engaged decisively rejected Ranked Choice Voting. Their ballots were straightforward, a clear choice between "yes" or "no." The people's voices were heard emphatically. And here is a fact they prefer you not know: those seven states rejecting RCV collectively cost the pro-RCV lobby nearly one hundred fifty million dollars between 2020 and 2024 and we knew the results on Election Night. One hundred fifty million dollars squandered on a system that the populace opposes.Meanwhile, here in Alaska, despite being one of the least populated states in the nation, it required nearly three weeks to ascertain our election results. This, precisely, is the detrimental outcome that RCV delivers: confusion, undue delay, and pervasive doubt.In the end, even though we were winning by over 4,000 votes on election day, our repeal effort came agonizingly close, falling short by a mere seven hundred forty-three (743 or 0.02%) votes after nearly 3 weeks of counting (320,000 Votes Total). However, my resolve remains unbroken.Safeguarding Integrity of Our RepublicMake no mistake, this fight transcends the boundaries of Alaska. It serves as a stark warning for the entire country, particularly for states that have a citizen initiative process. They will undoubtedly endeavor to introduce this confusing system to your communities as well, accompanied by external funding, political manipulation, and election results that leave citizens questioning the accuracy of their vote.It is imperative that we maintain the simplicity, clarity, and honesty of our elections. It demands Photo ID, End To Mail In Voting, And The Destruction of RCV and the RCV Lobby, because the sanctity of your vote merits protection equivalent to any other matter of significance. And it requires keeping out-of-state special interests and even worse foreign nationals (Hansjorg Wyss) out of our elections.I am immensely proud of our accomplishments here in Alaska. It was not an easy journey. But we demonstrated to the nation what ordinary, diligently working individuals can achieve when they stand firm, remain focused, and resolutely refuse to yield.The fight is not over. Not in Alaska. Not anywhere freedom-loving Americans call home.Let us stand together. Let us protect our elections. Let us accomplish this vital task, for Alaska and for America.Phil Izon aka[@907Honest](https://v8k6.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=6873945fd9b4250ce0609dff&u=https%3A%2F%2Fx.com%2F907Honest&w=658ac75703428575b106784b&c=b_68739415eb90c70f96bb5c76&l=en-US&s=5jSc-TOxKyOyLNIr9PCH_A0zztc%3D)

**A short explanation of the court win above, this case was decided last September 2024, but the Supreme Court ruling in our favor at the time, said the final order would come at a later time. That happened at the end of June. More to Come this week.**
---
### The Fight for Fair Elections: A Personal Mission
When I began the effort to repeal Ranked Choice Voting (RCV) in Alaska, I knew it would be a challenge. But I was determined not to let our electoral process be twisted into a confusing system that leaves voters disempowered.My commitment went beyond Alaska. I wrote **three books** and created a **30-part animated video series** to expose the truth about RCV, distributing thousands of copies and making the videos free nationwide. I've also spent countless hours collaborating with grassroots leaders across the country, helping establish organizations now fighting RCV. My websites, **RCValert.org** and **RemoveRCV.com** , offer years of research, resources, and tools like RCV Election Simulators.This fight is personal. My own grandfather, a wise and hardworking man, couldn't understand the ranked choice ballot. If someone with his common sense was confused, I knew there was a fundamental flaw. That moment spurred me to draft Alaska's repeal initiative.We didn't have billionaire funding or political party backing. What we had was unwavering determination and the support of over **700 dedicated Alaskan volunteers**. We knocked on doors, braved the cold, and gathered over **42,000 signatures** —far exceeding the requirement—all without help from political insiders.I've also traveled nationwide, warning states like Idaho, Oregon, Arizona, and Florida about RCV's manipulative nature, funded by out-of-state billionaires.As expected, the political establishment fought us. The attorney who brought RCV to Alaska even sued to prevent our repeal initiative from appearing on the ballot. But we prevailed in both the Alaska Superior Court and the Alaska Supreme Court. They outspent us by a staggering **one hundred to one** , with millions flowing in from out-of-state organizations like Unite America and the Sixteen Thirty Fund, supported by figures like George Soros and Hansjorg Wyss.On Election Day, I was immensely proud. Not only had I fought RCV in Alaska for years, but seven other states where I'd been active decisively rejected it. Those rejections alone cost the pro-RCV lobby nearly **$150 million** between 2020 and 2024. Here in Alaska, despite our small population, it took nearly three weeks to get election results—a clear example of RCV's confusion and delay.Our repeal effort in Alaska came agonizingly close, falling short by just 743 votes after nearly three weeks of counting. But my resolve is unbroken. This fight is a warning to the entire country. We must maintain simple, clear, and honest elections, demanding voter ID and keeping out-of-state special interests out.I am incredibly proud of what we achieved in Alaska. We showed the nation what ordinary, hardworking people can accomplish when they stand firm. The fight isn't over—not in Alaska, and not anywhere freedom-loving Americans call home.**Stand with us! Let's protect our elections for Alaska and for America.**

clean_body: Please share with friends and family | | | | When I first embarked on the effort to repeal Ranked Choice Voting (RCV) in Alaska, I must confess, I did not fully comprehend the intricate and relentless nature of the battle ahead. However, from the outset, one principle remained clear: I would not stand idly by and permit our electoral process to be contorted into a confusing, frustrating system that leaves voters feeling disempowered and uncertain about the efficacy of their participation.Comprehensive EngagementMy commitment did not stop in Alaska. I authored three books “Field Manual To RCV,” “manufactured Majority,” and “Multiple Choice Voting Systems” meticulously exposing the truth about RCV and have distributed thousands of copies across the United States, entirely free of charge. Furthermore, I produced a thirty-part animated video series, designed to illuminate the dangers of RCV in clear, accessible language, enabling voters nationwide to grasp the unfolding complexities. I also gave out tens of thousands of free videos to groups in 7 states. I have invested countless hours in Zoom calls, phone calls, and strategic planning sessions with grassroots leaders across the country. Indeed, entire organizations actively combating RCV today have publicly acknowledged my role in their foundational efforts.This fight is far from concluded. I have already extended these efforts to Michigan, Connecticut and other states now confronting the same threat. My websites, and, are comprehensive repositories of years of research, invaluable resources, and interactive tools, including the RCV Election Simulators and Resource Guides. All of these resources are meticulously designed to demonstrate to the public the inherent dangers and confusions embedded within this farse of an electoral system (RCV).A Personal ImperativeFor me, this endeavor has never been driven by mere political ambition or personal gain. It has always been deeply personal, originating within my own family. My grandfather, a good, hardworking man and a small business owner found himself utterly unable to comprehend the ranked choice ballot. If a man of his extensive experience, profound wisdom, and inherent common sense (He will tell you all about it), was confounded by the process, I recognized that a fundamental flaw existed. That realization marked the pivotal moment I committed myself, drafting Alaska's repeal initiative from my own living room.And I would be remiss not to acknowledge the indispensable role of my greatest supporter and closest confidante, my wife. She stood by my side and was my copilot, enduring Alaskan blizzards and brutal winter storms (also saved us from hitting a moose on our way back from Fairbanks during the blizzard by being an amazing copilot), unwavering in her determination to see this through. Her unwavering dedication and profound strength were a constant source of inspiration and resolve. She was able to see me on the court stand while Kendall & Co. (Attorneys) grilled me for nearly two hours and 6 hours of deposition prior about my handling of the petition. She would smile and give me a break from the disrespectful and stressful tone of the day. Her smile carried me through the tough times.We did not possess the financial backing of billionaires. We were not directed by political parties or high-priced corporate consultants. What we possessed was unwavering determination, diligent hard work, and an unshakeable conviction that Alaskans deserve fair and transparent elections. More than 700 dedicated volunteers, everyday Alaskans, rallied with me. We canvassed neighborhoods, knocking on countless doors. We stood steadfastly in the bitter cold. At one particular event, we successfully gathered over one thousand signatures in a single day. Another event was so extraordinarily crowded that the parking lot overflowed, compelling some individuals to depart. One of the parts of the court challenge was that Phil Izon couldn’t gather 500+ signatures in a single day, well I did and I had video evidence of me collecting signatures with Gov. Sarah Palin ( ) at the Palmer Train depot.Ultimately, we collected over 42,000 signatures, significantly exceeding the required threshold, achieved entirely without assistance from political insiders or billionaires.A National ImperativeDuring the signature gathering, I also traveled across the country, consistently raising the alarm about the profound implications of Ranked Choice Voting. I steadfastly refused to remain on the sidelines while this complex, manipulative system quietly infiltrated state after state, meticulously funded by out-of-state billionaires and political operatives who fully understood the disruptive potential they were unleashing.My efforts have extended to states such as Idaho, Oregon, Arizona, Missouri, Hawaii, Florida, Montana, California, Connecticut, Colorado, California, Nevada, Rhode Island, and numerous others. I have presented at GOP conventions, multiple events in Oregon, Arizona, and Hawaii, and dedicated time at our booth at CPAC in Washington, D.C., giving away educational materials and issuing warnings regarding the impending electoral shifts.I went on to debate 3 Alaska Legislators, 3 Oregon Legislators, The “Father” of RCV in Oakland and San Francisco (Steve Chessin), and finally U.S. Presidential Candidate Chase Oliver. I do not think anyone has debated the topic of RCV at the levels that I have. Fun thing I also was able to meet Gov. Desantis ( ) when he was also running for president, I was able to thank him for being the first Governor to ban RCV in America. I also gave him a copy of my book.Confronting the OppositionAs anticipated, the political establishment employed every conceivable tactic to impede our progress. The very attorney responsible for introducing Ranked Choice Voting to Alaska, Scott Kendall, subsequently filed a lawsuit against the State in an attempt to prevent our repeal initiative from even appearing on the ballot. It is a remarkable irony that the individuals who engineered the system and used the citizen initiative process to bring RCV to Alaska sought to undermine the right of Alaskan citizens to petition their government.However, we did not quit. We vigorously pursued our case in court, and decisively, we prevailed. We achieved victory in the Alaska Superior Court. They appealed to Alaska Supreme Court. We achieved victory in the Alaska Supreme Court. And subsequent to these legal triumphs, they ultimately settled with us for attorney fees ($50,000). To be clear, the court did not issue an order for them to cover our legal fees, but as an integral component of the settlement, they agreed to do so. Subsequently, he requested a confidentiality agreement, hoping that we would remain silent about our victory. We declined. While we agreed to the settlement, we adamantly refused to relinquish our right to openly discuss our accomplishments and were willing to pursue the case further if necessary.They outspent us by a staggering margin of one hundred to one. Millions of dollars flowed in from external organizations, backed by millionaires and billionaires, originating from New York, California, Virginia, and Washington, D.C., all desperately endeavoring to maintain their control over Alaska's elections. Groups such as Unite America, Action Now Initiative, and the Sixteen Thirty Fund, supported by prominent donors like George Soros and Hansjorg Wyss (Foreign National Billionaire), expended nearly 15 Million Dollars defending that flawed system, having initially spent ($7 Million) seven million to force its adoption in 2020.The Ongoing StruggleWhen Election Day finally arrived, I was not merely hopeful; I was profoundly proud. Not only had I dedicated years of my life to combating RCV here in Alaska, but I had expanded that fight nationwide. On that same day, seven other states where I had actively engaged decisively rejected Ranked Choice Voting. Their ballots were straightforward, a clear choice between "yes" or "no." The people's voices were heard emphatically. And here is a fact they prefer you not know: those seven states rejecting RCV collectively cost the pro-RCV lobby nearly one hundred fifty million dollars between 2020 and 2024 and we knew the results on Election Night. One hundred fifty million dollars squandered on a system that the populace opposes.Meanwhile, here in Alaska, despite being one of the least populated states in the nation, it required nearly three weeks to ascertain our election results. This, precisely, is the detrimental outcome that RCV delivers: confusion, undue delay, and pervasive doubt.In the end, even though we were winning by over 4,000 votes on election day, our repeal effort came agonizingly close, falling short by a mere seven hundred forty-three (743 or 0.02%) votes after nearly 3 weeks of counting (320,000 Votes Total). However, my resolve remains unbroken.Safeguarding Integrity of Our RepublicMake no mistake, this fight transcends the boundaries of Alaska. It serves as a stark warning for the entire country, particularly for states that have a citizen initiative process. They will undoubtedly endeavor to introduce this confusing system to your communities as well, accompanied by external funding, political manipulation, and election results that leave citizens questioning the accuracy of their vote.It is imperative that we maintain the simplicity, clarity, and honesty of our elections. It demands Photo ID, End To Mail In Voting, And The Destruction of RCV and the RCV Lobby, because the sanctity of your vote merits protection equivalent to any other matter of significance. And it requires keeping out-of-state special interests and even worse foreign nationals (Hansjorg Wyss) out of our elections.I am immensely proud of our accomplishments here in Alaska. It was not an easy journey. But we demonstrated to the nation what ordinary, diligently working individuals can achieve when they stand firm, remain focused, and resolutely refuse to yield.The fight is not over. Not in Alaska. Not anywhere freedom-loving Americans call home.Let us stand together. Let us protect our elections. Let us accomplish this vital task, for Alaska and for America.Phil Izon aka 907Honest = Tropic Tundra | Breaking: Court deals final blow to Kendall lawsuit over ranked-choice voting repeal petition | A short explanation of the court win above, this case was decided last September 2024, but the Supreme Court ruling in our favor at the time, said the final order would come at a later time. That happened at the end of June. More to Come this week. The Fight for Fair Elections: A Personal Mission When I began the effort to repeal Ranked Choice Voting (RCV) in Alaska, I knew it would be a challenge. But I was determined not to let our electoral process be twisted into a confusing system that leaves voters disempowered.My commitment went beyond Alaska. I wrote three books and created a 30-part animated video series to expose the truth about RCV, distributing thousands of copies and making the videos free nationwide. I've also spent countless hours collaborating with grassroots leaders across the country, helping establish organizations now fighting RCV. My websites, RCValert.org and RemoveRCV.com , offer years of research, resources, and tools like RCV Election Simulators.This fight is personal. My own grandfather, a wise and hardworking man, couldn't understand the ranked choice ballot. If someone with his common sense was confused, I knew there was a fundamental flaw. That moment spurred me to draft Alaska's repeal initiative.We didn't have billionaire funding or political party backing. What we had was unwavering determination and the support of over 700 dedicated Alaskan volunteers. We knocked on doors, braved the cold, and gathered over 42,000 signatures —far exceeding the requirement—all without help from political insiders.I've also traveled nationwide, warning states like Idaho, Oregon, Arizona, and Florida about RCV's manipulative nature, funded by out-of-state billionaires.As expected, the political establishment fought us. The attorney who brought RCV to Alaska even sued to prevent our repeal initiative from appearing on the ballot. But we prevailed in both the Alaska Superior Court and the Alaska Supreme Court. They outspent us by a staggering one hundred to one , with millions flowing in from out-of-state organizations like Unite America and the Sixteen Thirty Fund, supported by figures like George Soros and Hansjorg Wyss.On Election Day, I was immensely proud. Not only had I fought RCV in Alaska for years, but seven other states where I'd been active decisively rejected it. Those rejections alone cost the pro-RCV lobby nearly $150 million between 2020 and 2024. Here in Alaska, despite our small population, it took nearly three weeks to get election results—a clear example of RCV's confusion and delay.Our repeal effort in Alaska came agonizingly close, falling short by just 743 votes after nearly three weeks of counting. But my resolve is unbroken. This fight is a warning to the entire country. We must maintain simple, clear, and honest elections, demanding voter ID and keeping out-of-state special interests out.I am incredibly proud of what we achieved in Alaska. We showed the nation what ordinary, hardworking people can accomplish when they stand firm. The fight isn't over—not in Alaska, and not anywhere freedom-loving Americans call home.Stand with us! Let's protect our elections for Alaska and for America. More News | Exciting News, My team and I have been hired to manage and deliver the Eaglexit project for Eagle River and Chugiak. The objective is to break Eagle River and Chugiak into their own borough.The phase approach is:1\. Education / Volunteer Training / Building2\. Gather signatures for Petition / Education3\. GOTV (Get Out The Vote) / EducationOur team at, is proud to manage and deliver the full-scale rollout of Eaglexit for Eagle River and Chugiak. Let’s make history. | | Phil Izon 12110 Business Blvd Ste 6 PMB 113 Eagle River United States | ---|---

name: The Editors at Broad + Liberty
subject: Verdict is in on Shapiro's budget
disclaimer_text: paid for by one-time revenue sources
body:
News + editorial to start your week | Broad + Liberty Weekly Reads | | | |
---
| | | Good morning and welcome to _Broad + Liberty_ 's Weekly Reads.

Stay up to date on all of this and more when you[ subscribe to our daily email list ](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=34061a1d82&e=b7b3eddce2)and get the best of _Broad + Liberty_ delivered straight to your inbox.
---
|
---
|
### **1.[Shapiro’s unsustainable budget](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=85d548ff1e&e=b7b3eddce2)**
_From the Editors_
Democrats love to use the word “sustainable” lately, so they really ought to take their own advice and look at the commonwealth’s budget through that lens.

The 2019-2020 total budget was $33.9 billion. The current proposed budget for 2025-2026 from Governor Shapiro is $51.5 billion. That’s a 51 percent increase in a short six-year period, which we will not hesitate in calling it what it is: unsustainable.

Nearly as concerning is the fact that the current budget proposal calls for about $4.5 billion of spending from two sources of non-recurring revenue. That means almost nine percent of the proposed budget is being paid for by one-time revenue sources. Again — unsustainable, without a 32 percent increase in the income tax.

**Why It Matters**. All this spending might be easier to accept if it were paired with major reforms — like school choice, regulatory overhaul, or long-term pension fixes. But those ideas are nowhere in this proposal. Instead, taxpayers are footing the bill for a status quo that only grows more expensive without delivering any improvement in services — more money for the same product.

The two sources of non-recurring revenue, $1.6 billion from the rainy day fund and $2.9 billion from other reserves, make up 8.7 percent of the funding in Shapiro’s budget, and are set to run out, conveniently, after the governor’s reelection campaign.

What will happen then? Pennsylvania’s constitution requires a balanced budget, so the legislature’s only options at that point will be to cut their spending or to raise your taxes. **[Continue Reading](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=35938c094e&e=b7b3eddce2)**
---
|
---
|
### **2.[Bribing poor families to stay in schools that don’t work!](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=c62d8d0df4&e=b7b3eddce2)**

_By Guy Ciarrocchi_
Yes, the headline is correct. Another public school district is bribing poor families to transfer back to the very schools that they transferred the children from. It’s outrageous, unethical, immoral and ought to be illegal — if it isn’t already. The Westmont-Hilltop School District (WHSD) in Cambria County is[ paying $2000 per family](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=9da1c69d3b&e=b7b3eddce2) when students in their community leave a cyber charter school and return to the WHSD. Remember, by definition, all children enrolled in cyber schools are there because their parents or guardians chose for them to enroll there — because the local school district school is failing, or is unsafe, or is otherwise just not working for them. **Why It Matters.** Westmont-Hilltop is the latest to hit a new low: bribing parents. They’re using tax dollars to bribe parents to undo their school choice — to bribe them to leave the cyber school that they chose to return to the WHSD school they fled. The school that was not working for their children. In the WHSD, their own data states that 47 percent of their students are “economically disadvantaged” according to government classifications. This makes a bad policy even more unethical. My fear is that it will spread to Chester-Upland, Coatesville, Reading, or Philadelphia — home to the largest group of cyber students by far. They may prevail upon poor and working class families who need cash to pay the bills. **[Continue Reading](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=fe55d9cd4b&e=b7b3eddce2)**
---
|
---
|
# **3\. Lightning Round**
* **[Christine Flowers: My hipster heckler](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=c970c2c685&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Rep. Tim O’Neal: Breaking the zip code barrier and putting students first](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=307074d082&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Stu Bykofsky: What makes the DC33 strike a tough nut to crack](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=898c7025e7&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Thom Nickels: The trash police](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=e071296a10&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Paul Davis: PA Attorney General touts organized retail crime unit’s year of achievements](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=7f1fa8536e&e=b7b3eddce2)**

---
|
---
|
# **4\. What we're reading**
The Supreme Court’s term is over, but the hits just keep on coming. An [8-1 decision last week](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=aa8da86358&e=b7b3eddce2) allowed the Trump administration to continue to make plans to reduce the federal workforce, but also exposed a continuing trend of Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson going off the deep end. As [Charles C.W. Cooke wrote in _National Review_](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=fc89abe3ec&e=b7b3eddce2), “Jackson has managed to set herself apart within” the group of three liberal justices “by declining even to pretend that she understands the purpose of her job.” Even her usual companion in dissent, Justice Sonia Sotomayor, felt compelled to to call out Jackson’s nonsense in that 8-1 decision, noting that “the plans themselves are not before this Court, at this stage, and we thus have no occasion to consider whether they can and will be carried out consistent with the constraints of law.” Sotomayor had been the most outspoken and politically left-leaning member of the court, but even she understands that every challenge they hear to a government action is not a chance to debate whether they personally like a policy or not, but only to pronounce on whether the law allows it. If Justice Jackson can’t understand that, she should probably just quit the court and get a show on MSNBC.
---
|
---

clean_body: News + editorial to start your week | Broad + Liberty Weekly Reads | | | | | | | Good morning and welcome to Broad + Liberty 's Weekly Reads. Stay up to date on all of this and more when you get the best of Broad + Liberty delivered straight to your inbox. | | 1. From the Editors Democrats love to use the word “sustainable” lately, so they really ought to take their own advice and look at the commonwealth’s budget through that lens. The 2019-2020 total budget was $33.9 billion. The current proposed budget for 2025-2026 from Governor Shapiro is $51.5 billion. That’s a 51 percent increase in a short six-year period, which we will not hesitate in calling it what it is: unsustainable. Nearly as concerning is the fact that the current budget proposal calls for about $4.5 billion of spending from two sources of non-recurring revenue. That means almost nine percent of the proposed budget is being paid for by one-time revenue sources. Again — unsustainable, without a 32 percent increase in the income tax. Why It Matters. All this spending might be easier to accept if it were paired with major reforms — like school choice, regulatory overhaul, or long-term pension fixes. But those ideas are nowhere in this proposal. Instead, taxpayers are footing the bill for a status quo that only grows more expensive without delivering any improvement in services — more money for the same product. The two sources of non-recurring revenue, $1.6 billion from the rainy day fund and $2.9 billion from other reserves, make up 8.7 percent of the funding in Shapiro’s budget, and are set to run out, conveniently, after the governor’s reelection campaign. What will happen then? Pennsylvania’s constitution requires a balanced budget, so the legislature’s only options at that point will be to cut their spending or to raise your taxes. | | 2. By Guy Ciarrocchi Yes, the headline is correct. Another public school district is bribing poor families to transfer back to the very schools that they transferred the children from. It’s outrageous, unethical, immoral and ought to be illegal — if it isn’t already. The Westmont-Hilltop School District (WHSD) in Cambria County is when students in their community leave a cyber charter school and return to the WHSD. Remember, by definition, all children enrolled in cyber schools are there because their parents or guardians chose for them to enroll there — because the local school district school is failing, or is unsafe, or is otherwise just not working for them. Why It Matters. Westmont-Hilltop is the latest to hit a new low: bribing parents. They’re using tax dollars to bribe parents to undo their school choice — to bribe them to leave the cyber school that they chose to return to the WHSD school they fled. The school that was not working for their children. In the WHSD, their own data states that 47 percent of their students are “economically disadvantaged” according to government classifications. This makes a bad policy even more unethical. My fear is that it will spread to Chester-Upland, Coatesville, Reading, or Philadelphia — home to the largest group of cyber students by far. They may prevail upon poor and working class families who need cash to pay the bills. | | 3\. Lightning Round | | 4\. What we're reading The Supreme Court’s term is over, but the hits just keep on coming. An allowed the Trump administration to continue to make plans to reduce the federal workforce, but also exposed a continuing trend of Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson going off the deep end. As , “Jackson has managed to set herself apart within” the group of three liberal justices “by declining even to pretend that she understands the purpose of her job.” Even her usual companion in dissent, Justice Sonia Sotomayor, felt compelled to to call out Jackson’s nonsense in that 8-1 decision, noting that “the plans themselves are not before this Court, at this stage, and we thus have no occasion to consider whether they can and will be carried out consistent with the constraints of law.” Sotomayor had been the most outspoken and politically left-leaning member of the court, but even she understands that every challenge they hear to a government action is not a chance to debate whether they personally like a policy or not, but only to pronounce on whether the law allows it. If Justice Jackson can’t understand that, she should probably just quit the court and get a show on MSNBC. | | | Broad + Liberty is funded by readers like you. Your generous support ensures that Broad + Liberty can bring you stories and opinions that Pennsylvania’s mainstream media would rather leave unheard. Please consider making a contribution today. If you would like your gift to be used to help us cover a specific story or subject, please let us know in the contribution form or at mailto:editirs@broadandliberty.com. As always, gifts made to Broad + Liberty are 100% tax deductible! Thank you, dear reader, for your steadfast support of our independent, broad-minded brand of local journalism. We could not do this without you. 👀 **_Races to Watch:_ Iowa Democrats have an opportunity to break the Republican supermajority **
On August 26th, Democrats face a critical special election – winning this seat would break the Republican supermajority in the state Senate. Democrats are building key momentum in elections just like this one: Already this year, Democrats have overperformed in special elections by more than 10 points, a trend we hope remains in August. [Read more about the election here → ](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/u001.T4f1s0H8aL8dqpmq959YtNm97nbkRuH92YTEcmEYuWD7vdLEtAFmu9iyqVFVCqTsIImbx5uwQyMS7khlZ7pnVpUXsqNY686HN_6FbJbVJ-ij4gi9kgiHjitwBXVivcf8bA2qiK2j_0OJ12fqFpqV_Ezef043vUi2yI6mH9L92s9FbKQ1_s0MqcTyglxwgFgAjdZgn5qE_Z0_b7TV04V_UsBSdzkDvfOfI0f5PxjXUNzsfFZRkbK0j30oE24RjAbyURx3ncL6vZaE2GfCPRJxpXAxs51EmbXzsBOQkpY-Y1sLlueETv8EryfruS8LV_cAJHBu443X5ATxE-FmqbJTaj1-7wIlGJUMSZg80I4jFrlG5LMb5ChbRP4B9g9fwRfhVgOtdQ9sfTclF4DHvJK0N5i8HuahOxBVdRQzhnL246RPq3Np6oGMgc7SLT6TXY1YRhE_DV_WJqYLtBrmwsUJHLVdhDvU0cJo4eiGCPnwTBp5ULQXyZilXF-Kg0ODzGcBRB32sgJejpOhcVorjos3xQ5FkOhB6AtrCPKjenySxMwzSXiSdssIeb54X8VKp1OMCloqVqv6IRXSMBKLgSIl1ywBB2mM9jSmvsb1tWO5vuDy0csoK2WrXc_ZlGBtMvGOl7roWugZqhYE-1_T5uYIxg/4i5/RCpWejvTT5udbYWv-DhBcw/h1/h001.xnzB67LbnufGeHGmgNui6Wcwl9EsV8kMb4Dt7u3niAQ)
🚨 **_Reproductive Freedom Update:_ Michigan GOP lawmaker introduces legislation to classify abortion as homicide **
Although abortion has been protected in the state since 2022, a GOP lawmaker introduced legislation that seeks to extend legal personhood to fetuses and fertilized embryos and would allow for women who receive abortions to be charged with homicide. [This is Republicans’ latest cruel attack on reproductive health care and freedoms → ](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/u001.A3cd-kVlFz1CzUMSYeTvVdN_aM57JPckduAeAt3kOaE0xyEUfrck_HaG1KNIikMiYtdhB5gOC2p5GOIrokhalgaBTn1CI5RtZ02Lve4fr84xZhaAK2RpBy1FWRYkMJU99TdzRqLsy50rPyJjM9LR7cGpu2pil4MEWJSrxkCYvgQFhtEZO6leVnBz9IcSjcNojIi0CxO84cs99cAkHrDC8V5IFgs4h3hQJUFkFitdz-AFcwCiccJuDjSsXSXsM9x7dswBCN9QscMiZvaT_skeGlmYbWMhTinCYFQrtrBz-uEaf46Zvm7GvI6PzJpoStujABLtXbsL2LhIaMSnOKJ_9efFZFgRz5oxbX6A1UB_-UKVIbdHn3YDwMuxZPkHDGfU2UDrCR7MOtuC5Mhlf6JvTLQQlJxEg_E0cbtnLLsHAaEgT2uHzCLoGAZzy3feOaerbqYIJJ857hwhI1QGhOKhgYjWB9B7egxrkhja-4Ek_mlDSGU-hxlcM_vE9wdK86eSozzOV2rQb8Wh0JOenVm2NpKot2g1NipaYTTUKgU1XTyPwBcu0Lic6pJ2vGENexHEFiu6jrUBCjvmppbw05kPK2xbXZ75mfpK1-Q-4jcz3uT_OCjgLm-egjf0ZDZmCVR2u4Mp3w_Fu78TtIP7EzgismZpbNbUfsxcwYAxy11qO-k/4i5/RCpWejvTT5udbYWv-DhBcw/h2/h001.Wp_sp7Q40ok5L39mDBPx5Erir9hewtNbvNWBmXdy2aI)
🏛️ **_Court Watch:_ Wisconsin Supreme Court’s liberal majority strikes down 176-year-old abortion ban **
The Wisconsin Supreme Court’s liberal majority struck down the state’s 176-year-old abortion ban on Wednesday, ruling 4-3 that it was superseded by newer state laws regulating the procedure, including statutes that criminalize abortions only after a fetus can survive outside the womb. clean_body: Here's the latest edition of the DLCC's newsletter for Sunday, July 6th, 2025. | | | | | | 👀 Races to Watch: Iowa Democrats have an opportunity to break the Republican supermajority On August 26th, Democrats face a critical special election – winning this seat would break the Republican supermajority in the state Senate. Democrats are building key momentum in elections just like this one: Already this year, Democrats have overperformed in special elections by more than 10 points, a trend we hope remains in August. 🚨 Reproductive Freedom Update: Michigan GOP lawmaker introduces legislation to classify abortion as homicide Although abortion has been protected in the state since 2022, a GOP lawmaker introduced legislation that seeks to extend legal personhood to fetuses and fertilized embryos and would allow for women who receive abortions to be charged with homicide. 🏛️ Court Watch: Wisconsin Supreme Court’s liberal majority strikes down 176-year-old abortion ban The Wisconsin Supreme Court’s liberal majority struck down the state’s 176-year-old abortion ban on Wednesday, ruling 4-3 that it was superseded by newer state laws regulating the procedure, including statutes that criminalize abortions only after a fetus can survive outside the womb. 🔦 Spotlight: Sen. Ghazala Hashmi prepares for a consequential election ahead The DLCC announced Democratic nominee for Virginia Lieutenant Governor, Sen. Ghazala Hashmi, as our newest Spotlight Candidate. The race for Lieutenant Governor is both highly competitive and extremely consequential for the state legislature, as the winner will hold the power to break any tied votes in the narrowly divided Virginia Senate for the next four years. 🫏 Join our movement: Become an Official DLCC Member If you’re looking for a way to take back your agency against the GOP power surge and help chart the path forward, we hope you’ll join our team by becoming a DLCC member. 🗳️ Upcoming elections: Democrats work to build progressive power State Democrats are facing over a dozen elections in the coming months. ****** | | ---|---|--- | Prepared and paid for by DLCC Victory Fund, 1225 Eye Street NW Suite 1250, Washington, DC 20005. name: MarkKelly.com
subject: [SIGN ON] Add your name and personally endorse Mark Kelly
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
As a team, we want to make sure we never lose sight of how important you are to Mark’s work — and how grateful we are that you have his back.
#

Team —
As a team, we want to make sure we never lose sight of how important you are to Mark’s work — and how grateful we are that you have his back.
In 2020, hundreds of thousands of people signed on to say they were with Mark. Thousands more did the same in 2022.
It meant the world to us, and to Mark.
Now, he needs our help again. He’s got a new mission: _Holding the Trump Administration accountable, combatting Elon Musk’s illegal actions, and getting the next generation of leaders elected to the United States Senate._
**And today, we’re respectfully asking you to sign on and personally endorse Mark Kelly.**

When planes crash, the National Transportation Safety Board investigates the accident so we can learn, adapt, and innovate.
**I believe we need something similar in the U.S. for weather disasters in order to lower risk to life and property for the next “unfathomable” storm.**
Going forward, I have a bold new plan to ensure this tragedy doesn’t happen again.
But science, safety, and putting you first isn’t what Trump or his yes-men in Congress want. That’s why the national Republicans are poised to pour thousands of special interest dollars into this race to silence our voices.
**What happens here has national implications. Not only does winning here go a long way to flipping the House back, but we can actually rebuild a Congress that believes in science and your well-being.**
[**This is officially one of the most consequential races in the country for our future, which makes every single supporter make that much bigger of a difference. If you can, watch my interview then chip in a donation of $3 today.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111404588/557119479/896231865?refcode=em-7.13.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS85MzkwNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNzE5MGM4Ni1lZDVmLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wIwjZB_vCDsUfF-JpzrvdAQLneBmOPDkzLSk0Fib94Y=)

-Eric

name: Rebecca Cooke
subject: Will you formally endorse my campaign in WI-03, Peter?
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111405752/557123265/1706257898?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzUwMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NTAwODA1NC1lZjVmLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RiH0fr9egwr4vA7B34TNAhUi62c5N-mXTCPk38xfP7k=&emci=fee2a942-435f-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=65008054-ef5f-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=5944223)
Peter, Derrick Van Orden has his rich, corporate PAC buddies. **But that alone can’t win an election. People do.** This campaign is and will always be focused on the issues and the needs of folks across WI-03 – from Eau Claire to Platteville. Never the 1%. **That’s why YOUR support means so much to me: So we can build the broadest coalition possible.** We’ve always run this campaign differently than politics as usual, and we’re staying true to our values of not taking any voter for granted. That's how we've been doing things for generations in Wisconsin, and it's what you deserve. In that spirit, I’d love your support too: [**As we start another chapter of this campaign, will you add your name to say you personally endorse my campaign? We're building a broad coalition of health care workers, small business owners, educators, union members, farmers, and more behind our campaign. I'd love to have your name on our list!**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111405754/557123268/-1564520458?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzUwMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NTAwODA1NC1lZjVmLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RiH0fr9egwr4vA7B34TNAhUi62c5N-mXTCPk38xfP7k=&emci=fee2a942-435f-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=65008054-ef5f-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=5944223) | | **[Endorse Rebecca](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111405756/557123270/-1564520458?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzUwMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NTAwODA1NC1lZjVmLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RiH0fr9egwr4vA7B34TNAhUi62c5N-mXTCPk38xfP7k=&emci=fee2a942-435f-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=65008054-ef5f-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=5944223)**

At a time when the status quo in Washington is more interested in squeezing us dry to make billionaires richer, WI-03 can send a message that we want one of our own to lead us instead of unhinged yes-man Derrick Van Orden.
[**So please, let’s make sure DVO truly sees our strength by adding your personal endorsement of our campaign.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111405759/557123273/-1564520458?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzUwMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NTAwODA1NC1lZjVmLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RiH0fr9egwr4vA7B34TNAhUi62c5N-mXTCPk38xfP7k=&emci=fee2a942-435f-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=65008054-ef5f-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=5944223)

Rebecca

clean_body: | Peter, Derrick Van Orden has his rich, corporate PAC buddies. But that alone can’t win an election. People do. This campaign is and will always be focused on the issues and the needs of folks across WI-03 – from Eau Claire to Platteville. Never the 1%. That’s why YOUR support means so much to me: So we can build the broadest coalition possible. We’ve always run this campaign differently than politics as usual, and we’re staying true to our values of not taking any voter for granted. That's how we've been doing things for generations in Wisconsin, and it's what you deserve. In that spirit, I’d love your support too: | | At a time when the status quo in Washington is more interested in squeezing us dry to make billionaires richer, WI-03 can send a message that we want one of our own to lead us instead of unhinged yes-man Derrick Van Orden. I’m continually humbled by your support. Rebecca | = = Rebecca Cooke is a small business and nonprofit leader who has focused her career on building community and serving others. She was born and raised on an Eau Claire dairy farm and was appointed by Governor Tony Evers to the Wisconsin Economic Development Corporation. We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to . Cooke for Congress PO Box 1846 Eau Claire, WI 54702 United States

subject: Sorry to reach out so early

I’m the only Democrat representing Kansas in Congress, and we know for a fact that National Republicans are desperate to kick me out

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111406253/557124669/-240735101?refcode=email-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4OTIwOWJiYS1mMDVmLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8lmDiGonhgoLEGv8AHs6J3nvg8vvKr7A08rYI9FZ0ZI=) Hey, I’m sorry to email so early on a Sunday, but I needed to reach out to my top supporters and ask: [_**Can you chip in just $5 to help us hit our mid-month fundraising goal and defend KS-03?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111406256/557124673/-1340363074?refcode=email_071325_mmfr1&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4OTIwOWJiYS1mMDVmLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8lmDiGonhgoLEGv8AHs6J3nvg8vvKr7A08rYI9FZ0ZI=) Trust me, I know it can be frustrating when elected officials reach out all the time asking for donations. It’s an unfortunate consequence of all the court decisions that allowed unlimited sums of dark money to flood our elections. **But I wouldn’t ask if it wasn’t important.** Look, I’m the only Democrat representing Kansas in Congress, and we know for a fact that National Republicans are desperate to kick me out. That’s why Mike Johnson even personally wired money to my opponent last year. If we want to elect a Democratic majority, we have to defend this seat. And hitting each and every one of our fundraising goals is the best way to put this campaign on the path to victory. If everyone reading this email chipped in just $5, we’d close the gap on our goal within minutes — and send a message that grassroots supporters like you are ready to do whatever it takes to protect this seat. [_**So if you’re with me, can you chip in $5 or more right now? If we want to make 2026 an election to remember, we have to stand together.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111406259/557124676/-1340363074?refcode=email_071325_mmfr1&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4OTIwOWJiYS1mMDVmLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8lmDiGonhgoLEGv8AHs6J3nvg8vvKr7A08rYI9FZ0ZI=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | | [Donate $5 →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111406264/557124679/-1340363074?refcode=email_071325_mmfr1&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&amount=5&express_lane=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4OTIwOWJiYS1mMDVmLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8lmDiGonhgoLEGv8AHs6J3nvg8vvKr7A08rYI9FZ0ZI=)

But we must not lose hope. Instead, we must fight back with the truth. Hillsdale College has been engaging the Left on the battlefield of education from the beginning. That’s why we’ve turned our course, [**“The Great American Story: A Land of Hope”**](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=597256fafac54700bd0f4404b7e604c2&_e=bYQlqvAc3fbMvG50is3x5ojDrS_uFTY8WJApsx0OyGYCiXfFvQI_VsMdNxYS9vNbunpx4cViRvGrwjwoTSOKROPauMLWTdM8Ahfno5wvMSU15fcCT0vGTNJW8-5GLzdJFJaY-PnaAcMcHGzgOEbrp6yUU5UjpqCRESjQKKZgO9i1ZgAaNS0c62jNd3q1EmjT6czAb446ub7_o-l37AQL9uxRhVg3zo3swNZtNdPJQLR4-VWVEJ0Cug5kmXaajohTUZpq2go5qfsPxPOMuifmIvXANqRoXKq133nKG9sRz2hZnS1GJ8M79a-gKC2uMqOuDWraQdYw4IonFJHMK6OoNRy6BB7jte66He2F5mq8NpiTy0_CLyfiepIaau2t7-Y63FCzlT7dIttY3OuVupGNFcCC97P1sKOTWeONJumUiO68KE2mg9T1el78ct1MpVxMn99Y5CfMXp0j9zaAyr_VDI0X8zwzmg066gT4AClFt6XtiRXvcUzeLI6OGw-3kM5c6aIZZmPYxDy2AzVUpKs_nNQBrWCt0S7vjnIo_qMKu-PGuFva-JcRFPjHnEV2y_HfyCNeihtucEbcggttd7veHg2SJ1Wrzr0tb2SG2nY_2B2_U41LJSBjIC3bBBwkH-tnmGnX0y9AtXCL13GOwsKKIot7rzgyB_uLH7mdH-iuoXak6pLRdVQB-9SUo7wjh3-IU4z6fLPEibFfWPFmSobmQG4HGLdeXserh_TXuWIx2VY%3D) into a DVD set, and I’d like you to have a copy. Taught by historian Wilfred McClay and based on his recently published history of America, **“The Great American Story: A Land of Hope”** presents a full, unbiased account of America’s past. Hillsdale’s goal in producing this course is to **restore the kind of intelligent patriotism—especially among our young people—that is essential to preserving free government.** **“The Great American Story: A Land of Hope”** is a powerful counterweight to _The New York Times’_ “1619 Project,” which is injecting the idea that America is an essentially unjust nation into the curricula of America’s K-12 schools. Please consider getting [**“The Great American Story: A Land of Hope”**](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=597256fafac54700bd0f4404b7e604c2&_e=bYQlqvAc3fbMvG50is3x5ojDrS_uFTY8WJApsx0OyGYCiXfFvQI_VsMdNxYS9vNbunpx4cViRvGrwjwoTSOKROPauMLWTdM8Ahfno5wvMSU15fcCT0vGTNJW8-5GLzdJFJaY-PnaAcMcHGzgOEbrp6yUU5UjpqCRESjQKKZgO9i1ZgAaNS0c62jNd3q1EmjT6czAb446ub7_o-l37AQL9uxRhVg3zo3swNZtNdPJQLR4-VWVEJ0Cug5kmXaajohTUZpq2go5qfsPxPOMuifmIn4CqKJkB9AwqoZJbluclyUFExZCkMj1vkvfoG7BojRosBcgKpMrnXoVWEXSFr_72d0PUZVrEOD6f5PoSW0UqhuHb651WBv5F7LCGiiiwH9vifRqee-rq2gaUxIWkDx7D5U5qjH27F6zOU_uIPZFxk99XTzQXe55losuM8E4NLPVdtI7FscGkyXZ2WctGfMs-aHgGF_lFlOz1tIYmEdU2PPLFg_a9kEidoYtVllBVBnpeykt3Jqo2qbQkbjatwZal5mwVCOQDm-Fcuh17I01UYG0Yf4E0XhTChyMUo3kxgDvfL6lYz1VYb0Cq_qgIiVII1xs2Knona0tlPKJRvVV0pxMqnLVNobrVtFvhLiKuM6v7gETng1TxHBbkyB6EdgxYXHvMgGlDaS8W_1CFwMtC7jJ_22DpzZcWHcNgoTEzhdWqYdSdAjRYl9e0-JxoA4wORK4UkrHpMiuNvKVoO-8k3Y%3D) as a DVD set for your home library or to give as a gift. We will not accept this.

| | **The Trump administration is withholding $143 million in federal funding for New Jersey public schools.** That's money our schools were counting on for:
* Training our teachers
* Before- and after-school programs
* Support for English learners

**This funding was already approved by Congress, but the Trump administration is holding it back, putting politics ahead of our kids.** And if these cuts stay permanent, it's our students, educators, and families who will suffer most – especially in lower-income communities that depend on these programs. **Let me be clear: these are your taxpayer dollars.** You paid for this support, and now they want to take it away from your local schools. Trump doesn't care if your kids get it, they just want it for their billionaire tax cuts. We will not accept this. I hope we can all agree that every child deserves the best education and the chance to thrive – no matter where they live or how much money their parents make. [**They're betting that we won't notice. They're hoping we'll stay quiet. We won't. I'm calling on you to speak out now. Add your name to support our public schools:**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111407510/557126877/1750341419?contactdata=xDJZetfFiB7g+apA/u3YMNOQIyB/w77Q7aSGmkG4DqtKo+Hh3N2klqzCHLO4v3ktQxjnTsgoZWK8nzmZ896WVrzaZ3W6/2sck05ABRYH44jB2P3EQpGiOnJ9zvcBwFB+b4s6RYdMs1MGE2/CjPs0JQ==&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzk3MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYTgyMWViNC1mMTVmLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EdMur_efd8D3pDGozvxEOYFAoeAX0wfUV5RtJBlDt4Y=&emci=4d29cbaa-845e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=aa821eb4-f15f-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=26261389) [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111407511/557126878/1750341419?contactdata=xDJZetfFiB7g+apA/u3YMNOQIyB/w77Q7aSGmkG4DqtKo+Hh3N2klqzCHLO4v3ktQxjnTsgoZWK8nzmZ896WVrzaZ3W6/2sck05ABRYH44jB2P3EQpGiOnJ9zvcBwFB+b4s6RYdMs1MGE2/CjPs0JQ==&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzk3MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYTgyMWViNC1mMTVmLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EdMur_efd8D3pDGozvxEOYFAoeAX0wfUV5RtJBlDt4Y=&emci=4d29cbaa-845e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=aa821eb4-f15f-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=26261389) Thank you for standing up for our kids. Matt Quinn, who’s in charge of the Secret Service, talked [...] | Rubio's Secretive Talks Hint at New Peace Strategy Hey folks, it’s Wyatt here. Let’s chat about something big that happened recently. name: All On The Line Election Watch
subject: These races will be big for redistricting
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE NATIONAL REDISTRICTING ACTION FUND
body:
We need a surge of donations to help educate voters about what’s at stake
|
---

Peter,
Right now, All On The Line is gearing up to educate voters on a crucial slate of elections in 2025 and 2026, where fair maps could be on the line:
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111407745/557127710/-1219977196?refcode=don_aotl_tier1_email_Priorities_email_20250713-AOTL-Tier1-Priorities_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250713-AOTL-Tier1-Priorities_&utm_campaign=Priorities&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImFiODIxZWI0LWYxNWYtZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=_TkmCn7bhCOkmIJ_ai2_33nko2jyj6vt4qYVHrc_KZg=)
---
**The fight for democracy runs through each and every one of these elections, Peter.**
There is so much work to do to prepare for these fights, from energizing voters to make their voices heard, to pushing back against gerrymandering attempts, to educating voters about what’s on their ballots. We need to make sure voters know what’s at stake in elections like these.
But it won’t be easy. Anti-democracy forces will use every tool at their disposal to attack our rights, whether through the courts or through map manipulation. We must continue to fight for fair maps and fair representation — and these upcoming state-level races are a moment for each of us to double down on our commitment to defend our democracy.
[**So we’re asking: Will you make a donation to help educate voters about critical elections and fight for fair representation, fair maps, and a more just electoral system?**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111407746/557127716/-1219977196?refcode=don_aotl_tier1_email_Priorities_email_20250713-AOTL-Tier1-Priorities_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250713-AOTL-Tier1-Priorities_&utm_campaign=Priorities&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImFiODIxZWI0LWYxNWYtZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=_TkmCn7bhCOkmIJ_ai2_33nko2jyj6vt4qYVHrc_KZg=)

clean_body: We need a surge of donations to help educate voters about what’s at stake | Peter, Right now, All On The Line is gearing up to educate voters on a crucial slate of elections in 2025 and 2026, where fair maps could be on the line: = The fight for democracy runs through each and every one of these elections, Peter. There is so much work to do to prepare for these fights, from energizing voters to make their voices heard, to pushing back against gerrymandering attempts, to educating voters about what’s on their ballots. We need to make sure voters know what’s at stake in elections like these. But it won’t be easy. Anti-democracy forces will use every tool at their disposal to attack our rights, whether through the courts or through map manipulation. We must continue to fight for fair maps and fair representation — and these upcoming state-level races are a moment for each of us to double down on our commitment to defend our democracy. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = Thank you so much, All On The Line is the grassroots advocacy campaign supported by the National Redistricting Action Fund.
