name: Team Bell
subject: Mike Johnson just handed Trump the keys to our democracy.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BELL FOR MISSOURI
body:
Peter,

Instead of standing with the American people this July 4th, Speaker Mike Johnson chose to side with Donald Trump in plain sight.

In a shameless display of allegiance, Mike Johnson handed Donald Trump the very gavel he used to ram through one of the most reckless, unfair tax bills in our nation’s history. A symbol of Congressional power, placed right in Trump’s hands.

Let’s call this what it is: An open admission that Trump and his loyalists intend to rule by fear, not by the will of the people. Trump isn’t lurking behind closed doors anymore, his grip on the GOP is out in the open. And House Republicans like Mike Johnson are showing us they’ll follow his orders, **no matter how extreme, no matter how dangerous.**

That’s why what we do right now and as we look ahead to 2026 matters so much. We need leaders in Congress who will fight for justice, defend our democracy, and put people over politics. Leaders who remember they work for the people, **not for Donald Trump.** But to flip the House next November and stop this dangerous power grab, we need to keep this movement fighting strong all year round.

body:
Hi team,
Last week, Republicans in Congress passed a bill that will rip healthcare away from 16 million people, take food off the table for 40 million people, raise costs for working families, and eliminate jobs .
House Republicans are pushing to cut millions of Americans off of programs like Medicaid and food stamps, while New Dems are standing up for the people who need them most – working families, seniors, and children.
While House Republicans give more tax breaks to the ultra-rich, we’re fighting for a fair economy that works for every American.
Republicans are enabling Trump to undermine the rule of law to push a partisan agenda, New Dems are focused on uniting Americans around the common cause to defend our democracy.
This cruel bill only makes us more committed to fighting for working families, a strong economy, and safe communities.

clean_body: Hi team, Last week, Republicans in Congress passed a bill that will rip healthcare away from 16 million people, take food off the table for 40 million people, raise costs for working families, and eliminate jobs . House Republicans are pushing to cut millions of Americans off of programs like Medicaid and food stamps, while New Dems are standing up for the people who need them most – working families, seniors, and children. While House Republicans give more tax breaks to the ultra-rich, we’re fighting for a fair economy that works for every American. Republicans are enabling Trump to undermine the rule of law to push a partisan agenda, New Dems are focused on uniting Americans around the common cause to defend our democracy. This cruel bill only makes us more committed to fighting for working families, a strong economy, and safe communities. |

Look ↙↙** **[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111408594/557130350/1158394903?amount=10&refcode=DOTL_20250713_EMLTR_E1_T0_AC_RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNS8xLzk0MjI1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjFlYzgyOGFmLWYyNWYtZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=ocjsMHxvta0HPbOagfQL4vvS3Qtk9eV09Qk1lyNzl9E=)** So tomorrow at 9am sharp we’re going to send a letter to Governor Newsom to let him know: **Democrats across the country have his back.**

We only have **[48] open signature spots** left for grassroots Democrats, Peter. [Sign the letter before our midnight deadline »](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111408595/557130355/1158394903?amount=7&refcode=DOTL_20250713_EMLTR_E1_T0_AC_RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNS8xLzk0MjI1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjFlYzgyOGFmLWYyNWYtZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=ocjsMHxvta0HPbOagfQL4vvS3Qtk9eV09Qk1lyNzl9E=) | [SIGN THE “THANK YOU GAVIN NEWSOM” LETTER NOW »](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111408597/557130356/1158394903?amount=7&refcode=DOTL_20250713_EMLTR_E1_T0_AC_RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNS8xLzk0MjI1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjFlYzgyOGFmLWYyNWYtZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=ocjsMHxvta0HPbOagfQL4vvS3Qtk9eV09Qk1lyNzl9E=)

**TO: Gov. Gavin Newsom
FROM: A loyal Democrat
RE: Democrats stand with you ** Dear Governor Newsom,

**Thank you SO much for standing up for our democracy by suing the Trump Administration!**

Trump is clearly threatened by how you exposed him to his unconstitutional and authoritarian actions.

**His threat to jail you is not only baseless, it’s a power-grab and another attempt to launch us into a MAGA dictatorship.**

This marks a terrifying point in our nation’s history. That’s why I’m so grateful to have leaders like you who won’t back down in the face of these anti-democratic threats.

[So I’m signing this letter to stand with you and chipping in $7 to Democracy On the Line so we can elect more Democrats, break the Republican trifecta, and join you in the fight against Trump and rising authoritarianism »](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111408598/557130357/1158394903?amount=7&refcode=DOTL_20250713_EMLTR_E1_T0_AC_RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNS8xLzk0MjI1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjFlYzgyOGFmLWYyNWYtZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=ocjsMHxvta0HPbOagfQL4vvS3Qtk9eV09Qk1lyNzl9E=) | **[SIGN THE “THANK YOU GAVIN NEWSOM” LETTER NOW »](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111408601/557130359/1158394903?amount=7&refcode=DOTL_20250713_EMLTR_E1_T0_AC_RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNS8xLzk0MjI1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjFlYzgyOGFmLWYyNWYtZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=ocjsMHxvta0HPbOagfQL4vvS3Qtk9eV09Qk1lyNzl9E=)**

name: Scott Brown
subject: My truck. My story.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scott Brown for New Hampshire, Inc
body:
Friend, the first time I ran for U.S. Senate I had little more than a
message and my beat-up 2005 GMC Canyon.

That truck wasn’t fancy — it had rust, over 200,000 miles, and no
business leading a political revolution.

_But it carried me through snowstorms and late-night town halls,
because I believed everyday Americans deserved a voice in Washington._

#
[https://secure.winred.com/scott-brown-for-new-hampshire-inc/our-fight-amc/?recurring=true&utm_medium=pemail&utm_source=rs_amc&utm_campaign=20250709_our-fight_sb&utm_term=lrm-active]

AND WITH YOUR SUPPORT, WE SHOCKED THE POLITICAL WORLD. WE WON. And we
did it the hard way — by showing up, listening, and earning every
single vote.

#
[https://secure.winred.com/scott-brown-for-new-hampshire-inc/our-fight-amc/?recurring=true&utm_medium=pemail&utm_source=rs_amc&utm_campaign=20250709_our-fight_sb&utm_term=lrm-active]

NOW, I’M HITTING THE ROAD AGAIN WHERE I’M RUNNING TO BE YOUR NEXT
U.S. SENATE FROM NEW HAMPSHIRE.
[https://secure.winred.com/scott-brown-for-new-hampshire-inc/our-fight-amc/?recurring=true&utm_medium=pemail&utm_source=rs_amc&utm_campaign=20250709_our-fight_sb&utm_term=lrm-active]
Chip in now to join Team Brown »
[https://secure.winred.com/scott-brown-for-new-hampshire-inc/our-fight-amc/?recurring=true&utm_medium=pemail&utm_source=rs_amc&utm_campaign=20250709_our-fight_sb&utm_term=lrm-active]

MY WIFE, GAIL, AND I HAVE BUILT OUR LIFE HERE IN RYE. It’s where we
became grandparents, and found a community rooted in service, faith,
and good, old-fashioned hard work.

So when I see what’s happened under Democrat control... skyrocketing
inflation, open borders, politicians too cozy with special
interests... _I know I can’t sit in the driveway._

I’VE GOT TO GET BACK IN THE TRUCK.

We’re launching a people-powered campaign to take back a critical
U.S. Senate seat — one that’s been held for years by liberals like
Jeanne Shaheen and Maggie Hassan, who are out of touch with Granite
Staters.

This is a chance to flip the seat, flip the script on out-of-touch
career politicians and keep the Senate RED.

BUT I CAN’T DO IT WITHOUT YOU.
[https://secure.winred.com/scott-brown-for-new-hampshire-inc/our-fight-amc/?recurring=true&utm_medium=pemail&utm_source=rs_amc&utm_campaign=20250709_our-fight_sb&utm_term=lrm-active]

Just like that first campaign, I’m relying on grassroots supporters
— not lobbyists or insiders — to power this truck forward.

WILL YOU CHIP IN $25, $50, OR EVEN $5 TODAY TO HELP US GAS UP THE
CAMPAIGN AND HIT THE GROUND RUNNING?
[https://secure.winred.com/scott-brown-for-new-hampshire-inc/our-fight-amc/?recurring=true&utm_medium=pemail&utm_source=rs_amc&utm_campaign=20250709_our-fight_sb&utm_term=lrm-active]
RUSH A DONATION NOW
[https://secure.winred.com/scott-brown-for-new-hampshire-inc/our-fight-amc/?recurring=true&utm_medium=pemail&utm_source=rs_amc&utm_campaign=20250709_our-fight_sb&utm_term=lrm-active]

_Every dollar we raise will help:_

* Knock on doors and talk to voters face-to-face (I’ll drive there
myself!)

* Hire field staff and build our event infrastructure

* Share our message with folks who are ready for change

* Show the D.C. establishment that New Hampshire won’t be bought

I MAY NOT HAVE ALWAYS HAD THE FLASHIEST VEHICLE, BUT I’VE GOT GRIT,
A FULL TANK OF GAS, AND A DEEP LOVE FOR THIS COUNTRY — AND THAT’S
MORE THAN ENOUGH TO GET STARTED.
[https://secure.winred.com/scott-brown-for-new-hampshire-inc/our-fight-amc/?recurring=true&utm_medium=pemail&utm_source=rs_amc&utm_campaign=20250709_our-fight_sb&utm_term=lrm-active]

Let’s put in some more miles, and let’s win this thing —
together.

All my best,
SCOTT BROWN
Candidate for U.S. Senate – New Hampshire

-------------------------

JOIN SCOTT BROWN’S TEAM
#
[https://secure.winred.com/scott-brown-for-new-hampshire-inc/email-footer-launch/?recurring=true]

Scott Brown is a proud husband, father, grandfather, and lifelong
public servant.

He wore our nation’s uniform for over 30 years, served as a U.S.
Senator, and represented America on the world stage as President
Trump’s ambassador.

NOW, HE’S ANSWERING THE CALL TO SERVE AGAIN — RUNNING TO FLIP NEW
HAMPSHIRE’S SENATE SEAT RED AND BRING REAL LEADERSHIP BACK TO
WASHINGTON.

For nearly 20 years, this seat has been controlled by Democrats while
Washington has grown more dysfunctional, more divided, and more out of
touch with the American people.

SCOTT BROWN IS READY TO CHANGE THAT — TO RESTORE ACCOUNTABILITY,
DEFEND OUR VALUES, AND FIGHT FOR THE AMERICAN DREAM.

_But he can’t do it without a strong grassroots team behind him._

Will you step up and join the fight today with a donation of any
amount?
[https://secure.winred.com/scott-brown-for-new-hampshire-inc/email-footer-launch/?recurring=true]

DONATE NOW
[https://secure.winred.com/scott-brown-for-new-hampshire-inc/email-footer-launch/?recurring=true]

Thank you,

Team Scott Brown
-------------------------

Paid for by Scott Brown for New Hampshire, Inc.

Scott Brown is a retired member of the U.S. Army. Use of his military
rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply
endorsement by the Army or the Department of Defense.

PO Box 606
Rye, NH 03870

Privacy Policy [https://scottbrown.com/?page_id=3178]

Unsubscribe: https://app.vbout.com/index.php?r=Email/OptoutConfirmationForm&messageid=301989&trkid=775432098&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com

215 N Payne Street, Alexandria, VA 22314

name: Adam Schwarze, former Navy SEAL
subject: Have you answered your call to duty?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Schwarze for Senate
body:
This isn’t about politics. It’s about the survival of common sense. |

---
Peter,

I’m Adam Schwarze—former Navy SEAL Officer, Marine Corps veteran, lifelong Minnesotan, and proud son of two working-class parents. I’ve deployed nine times to 70 countries to serve the country I love. I never expected to run for the U.S. Senate—but looking at what’s happening in our state and nation, I know I have to.

Minnesota is being led by two extreme voices: _**Governor Tim Walz and his lieutenant governor—my opponent—who is somehow even more radical than her boss.**_ While our cities burned in 2020, she led the charge against law enforcement while Walz hesitated. That’s not leadership. That’s chaos.

Meanwhile, crime has increased, small businesses have struggled, and our schools fail to teach the basics. The Minnesota I grew up in—where I learned to skate, hunt, and respect my neighbors—feels like it’s slipping away.

I joined the Marine Corps on my 18th birthday—October 2001— _**just weeks after 9/11.**_

_**I didn’t wait. I went.**_

And for the next 20+ years, I continued to serve. Not behind a desk or on cable news, but in the field—on missions that mattered, often in places you’ll never see on a map.

I don’t come from wealth or political pedigree. I come from hard work, long winters, and a deep desire to serve something bigger than myself. I earned degrees from Harvard, Johns Hopkins, and my MBA—not because I wanted to impress anyone, but because I wanted to serve better and lead smarter.

Now, I’m taking that same spirit to this campaign.
_**

[**HELP ME PROTECT THE AMERICA WE LOVE
CHIP IN $10 TODAY**](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=cb261f5bb9104f168ddd50b733b1d78f&_e=4SqQIdtTlN2sMagLSuI0yq6-EPquliARVjztQ2SAcqqrKZ2UCAHgj6wefe_KKLaKEPeHXxpt_rk_kt7Xx58a2suisi0jS5DmBZ-2-PUaRGUBzZCiGXWBFggITsjg1B5N_9TshsFzMpSfxpAC2J71HjU8Pn_vjwf_axAt7LU5aEh5eUtEhskJEk-b7Odwipkbt5llVrwT18S-VCi1fXUYXb_sCQBg6fpRJBbBLJJOvicM-rx_XX_NsCES8hIV7OoTUDf3b5bsEMmckUa7B_wzUcJQ1rFrFC-9F98CF8CqmRlzoRbHq0C3IKo9M-Z1TdvF_CB2AZZsZyCtWTFlU7bwbpdihzbt6uqQKBzH62-9p4m0ldAnyK3u2MS1eS9mXPPkQUjAGz4dMWOwrB2xNG20NTQgjbPiY2WdLOdD6LZMnq1WuGks9lB9d4T5QcUD0QKcwqeRsyxVQirrpX9a7bOUqyXuAynSn0U7KtevYsFdUTA_jOgPxq71CpXGsTCCPm4Z1kdGlGyH5JEbud9JbEysyhJPCqE-tEXU5ukqfhK2qaLooIxSBjzu5HzRE5fIx-dIswC3S3Lou4qfPHAg0YOkTcDDZfE6vzMzXbMY2SpaqwGr-L9VAjJbfrbh60MmBc1QKLQOxWEr85kHoFBXbG2ByjZGH9sYZAMqxIgLi8sOdn9x5BXHYhVdiUrQnTvuY91qKMQUIoY6cFMQuriLKJ_X7ZQtMpZrQ_JPb0meFkdItiKkAYAPZaaslaZJ4I_WCzw29c0oFU4VlGSNLUrcoVYSa0NdUOe2iJrfoM5o9gJespk%3D)
**_ **_Every dollar helps build a campaign ready to fight and win._**
_

This won’t be easy. Minnesota hasn’t voted Republican in a presidential race since 1976, and, unfortunately, hasn’t had Republican representation in the Senate since 2009. **But I’ve always thrived in harsh environments and never backed down from a challenge worth fighting for.**

And truthfully, I’m not running because I dreamed of holding office. I’m running because every time I came home from deployment, things seemed worse—more divided, less free, and further removed from the Minnesota and America I was willing to risk my life for.

We need a new kind of leadership rooted in service, not self-interest.

**If you believe in what I’m fighting for,[even $5 goes a long way.](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=cb261f5bb9104f168ddd50b733b1d78f&_e=4SqQIdtTlN2sMagLSuI0yq6-EPquliARVjztQ2SAcqqrKZ2UCAHgj6wefe_KKLaKEPeHXxpt_rk_kt7Xx58a2suisi0jS5DmBZ-2-PUaRGUBzZCiGXWBFggITsjg1B5N_9TshsFzMpSfxpAC2J71HjU8Pn_vjwf_axAt7LU5aEh5eUtEhskJEk-b7Odwipkbt5llVrwT18S-VCi1fXUYXb_sCQBg6fpRJBbBLJJOvicM-rx_XX_NsCES8hIV7OoTUDf3b5bsEMmckUa7B_wzUcJQ1rFrFC-9F98CF8CqmRlB6DGzl9UFsJBHgZe-B2URHcM2PHppt2Ku60vVgMklpLE8hDOLzPGZUGycEpPl8zfu4gGhWJLXqgOz9GAGN5f_i_5qgxa5lWWVp4QpFDD-aPLVcFKT-P-ub_wsgfH74aDn9wJIwmGsp21fuE_S6mBChjnW_6g2w6544xWsEK6YPUWtu9pt87HfKbv4CA6GItDYF9QJHnF5H7vKeDOEukpvNp0NwZSCFPK3Ah9lylmcxKI9zJUbR4Usvyh0BmGKGxcahjesCQvRJE_Q5Ox1VD6enBmh2L6xwCkgdM86WbABdmdEmude_lSqAcLewVKaCjJu6i9Ofw7jhvukiTRESBJQ9fq0teUOI3blJmgmVaHMI_ecM0xxwKgYTQAL4XC5UvL4YCIBO8--HTToyIgn8lAJ-EhYFMRwND_a0tdTB2pjkfZSBaHSmGcAq5UysUKhLwEMwEofosyGuvfwVP-xhU2LK2Xzl_Be3jRVUrXBCwusyE_V5liGSexfwxCjcfqmY98%3D)**

If you’re not ready to donate today, that’s okay. I just want to earn your trust in the weeks and months ahead. But if you believe—like I do—that Minnesota and America deserve better, please chip in now.

Let’s take this fight to the Senate. And let’s do it together.

**You’ve done your part for this country.[Let me do mine—will you donate $15 to help?](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=cb261f5bb9104f168ddd50b733b1d78f&_e=4SqQIdtTlN2sMagLSuI0yq6-EPquliARVjztQ2SAcqqrKZ2UCAHgj6wefe_KKLaKEPeHXxpt_rk_kt7Xx58a2suisi0jS5DmBZ-2-PUaRGUBzZCiGXWBFggITsjg1B5N_9TshsFzMpSfxpAC2J71HjU8Pn_vjwf_axAt7LU5aEh5eUtEhskJEk-b7Odwipkbt5llVrwT18S-VCi1fXUYXb_sCQBg6fpRJBbBLJJOvicM-rx_XX_NsCES8hIV7OoTUDf3b5bsEMmckUa7B_wzUcJQ1rFrFC-9F98CF8CqmRmWPt88ufaiZihHSAgH7mgHEqrZI2ZM2Ov8S__0cnnWVsCtFo0cRt4yrjPIFxFZg50t52XoNeCDu6apuMDmmwReUoCtEV6Mt8qqNEC4pnT09TIdvsI96naW4whej4wcpGc3D4ERvfSs8Qqf_6ahb3pJNZP2TFKFtcbNp4W0pQHUfpK7z6-7LSjzdgrOTBLx9fIteUM-7MxgbHFifce1aPchcEbeit3QPvhvEE3ETXG6m2AV7Uke_-S_l3bAI4ciWV5uzf3Bm7UFhPftwCnItNSwEkA1OPrEpyWqY-j6IdKckSt9arqmbkzE3gIOQmUqigFmc2ZC8W7SBwSwsHvYXY9tfFxNNS0MWr6jYybKkyxCKtpl9sI3-4-zYcN_g6BMuh-ILFufj5EQDBja5D3cLuBvTi7tMmxqwMIxIozLWX-l8Llw1PH_r-QR-5w8YJKONioEEl2tJ23PPH_lbOP4GdOGKiQxZWcZMlty-eE_N3on4DmK_oT7d6ieJAkkgkg1f0U%3D)**

clean_body: This isn’t about politics. It’s about the survival of common sense. | Peter, I’m Adam Schwarze—former Navy SEAL Officer, Marine Corps veteran, lifelong Minnesotan, and proud son of two working-class parents. I’ve deployed nine times to 70 countries to serve the country I love. I never expected to run for the U.S. Senate—but looking at what’s happening in our state and nation, I know I have to. Minnesota is being led by two extreme voices: Governor Tim Walz and his lieutenant governor—my opponent—who is somehow even more radical than her boss. While our cities burned in 2020, she led the charge against law enforcement while Walz hesitated. That’s not leadership. That’s chaos. Meanwhile, crime has increased, small businesses have struggled, and our schools fail to teach the basics. The Minnesota I grew up in—where I learned to skate, hunt, and respect my neighbors—feels like it’s slipping away. I joined the Marine Corps on my 18th birthday—October 2001— just weeks after 9/11. I didn’t wait. I went. And for the next 20+ years, I continued to serve. Not behind a desk or on cable news, but in the field—on missions that mattered, often in places you’ll never see on a map. I don’t come from wealth or political pedigree. I come from hard work, long winters, and a deep desire to serve something bigger than myself. I earned degrees from Harvard, Johns Hopkins, and my MBA—not because I wanted to impress anyone, but because I wanted to serve better and lead smarter. Now, I’m taking that same spirit to this campaign. Every dollar helps build a campaign ready to fight and win. This won’t be easy. Minnesota hasn’t voted Republican in a presidential race since 1976, and, unfortunately, hasn’t had Republican representation in the Senate since 2009. But I’ve always thrived in harsh environments and never backed down from a challenge worth fighting for. And truthfully, I’m not running because I dreamed of holding office. I’m running because every time I came home from deployment, things seemed worse—more divided, less free, and further removed from the Minnesota and America I was willing to risk my life for. We need a new kind of leadership rooted in service, not self-interest. If you believe in what I’m fighting for, If you’re not ready to donate today, that’s okay. I just want to earn your trust in the weeks and months ahead. But if you believe—like I do—that Minnesota and America deserve better, please chip in now. Let’s take this fight to the Senate. And let’s do it together. You’ve done your part for this country. In liberty Adam Schwarze Retired Navy SEAL Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate - Minnesota Former Navy SEAL officer Marine Corps Veteran Proud Minnesotan FOLLOW ADAM ON SOCIAL MEDIA | | Paid for by Schwarze for Senate Adam Schwarze is a former Navy SEAL and Marine. Use of his military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.

subject: One year ago… a bullet nearly ended it all
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Burlison for Congress

What I’m about to say may not be easy to read. But it must be said.

[](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/fLfr30WLmNLyCHjCH2OCIA64seQrW5E_XuzV1hGoP6svEwBMRq1fJJHVCK66LQQzbO7nP6Gz4kBTXWKF9MTw0JySc2-GDDl_Ygt5qF2uu3kPKw7d6cvaB_fzBuXPzL2qlrSG0-61MIvlCUmPCCrKOhWjzz-AI5hsMz_2Gp-33L54TgmLjPP2Q56FqAMM8AHtwwyZZWEBS0oj1FomiSm067CUzSqLWANa0_1LKqcK7HwRWopWKGjA0SudTtih6mXTOCLTJ-DIobxeOBkL-7KQxzx3O1X0kgVdZHeQazMvvbn33dGfWWoQzLqL96fCHcMAqH-W1k6pVBXv1ZskuZnJJqmP-FQNxroYri9waOlrm93zZAHXEtb_01KdYo0jpGHXiJdVxcv-u6PjNSWWC3TaBYE4cbEc_uOmADW9xOZty1QFE38LLGygIDH4Ttr_kSokOVsexbBZ8gn7iNhwuVmRBkMuKti_W6vidVaZ2voBmfIK9_DlZCcUOnjoX3f1vyJFF-IS4Hhy86PHDnsIpLjjTZ0h3JwhQVNpylg74HIhL9ftrdVikd-Q9J_Tp0JfeZAcynPLLzMOhx6XL5-ofi6voj_QgPCY3CpQzpTl68Z6kpSnzd-m96GqLfDLpdNoQ0kan7ma9qNb1VNoA6zVZOksW8JY7EXpFR6gfUtUCxMJnV755rwffjz7R67RdQqNvx6nvIHP1ajUgCimdcyhUBVvMgq95-RR5m37nRbnc0wbEBbQTdsKyD80ssmz8Ywv25jTyOjH/UZJ6KvFNenqypXzrRfJNqTOJxCjj2zv6/10)

**Peter** ,

Congressman Burlison here.

I don't write messages like this often. _**But today is not an ordinary day.

**_
Exactly one year ago--President Donald J. Trump **_was nearly assassinated_** in Butler, Pennsylvania.

A mere inch made the difference between life and death.

A mere inch could have changed the course of history.

The image of President Trump--his ear bloodied, Secret Service agents swarming, supporters screaming--will **_never_** leave my mind.

[](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/a-AGzW3dAYMhMYZj7rbKUtiXB6XcXbO1lz2nkLhX84uHn5OOB26P6VqQsjPtw720Tz82ijfDQRDtLTirB_0r6uXpWKNV-UVjVEo3L6WpEu2FiW_ZJtskJusrx8C_Qo6Ahfwb3GGOKXcoOGY56mszwPxcIKXlrI9MI6E6Z9jxlYtiaeZOLqygvrr6rqnOLE3RKd884ZsFWRvvjV1mX_oLFtPPNXBRn7BEl6L1N0cTr7fej8gtLO8f2Pxx2JDqs_N1j1mo8YM-DOX8hkNrcjDkCTnwxl6ShQnNcec_4vJh4vqX6st7zjsfui37boD9P6TSjsS_CvtPwHoicz9LWOV2LNRXBGfqw7i7haqR7sWpnakujQbj9YggnCQ0RhyfWcPx0D9At3M9pnJtP-DjBr5bqjDNcL0uw-st_YcUO760-mS3_-D3uOu8vp_nrb4kuus3U8INdnez1926aLTRhPtm1I2sWT2CIOYVdVhQbRz93kPRr8k4qP7yMOv_sTcMwGzwhLkeZ6MuDDsj8J0gmnewFfhHWMXS7yaKmHig1gPDaSrW1mVoiuFu_7dXQHltEiXdVE3lYlV3Mbpvl21iY5vTzuc63KX5-DrF-z8m3sdnpJJPrCes8zaVZvKaht89soLd3KYSgEqBQD24Pf4iOKaLgt-_4g1oVMgZq1nA8_BmIzvn2BY702BGDHVUM0-agxaot4Qq-jGwx_rM9Z4g3Tx4mQNNKMW4jsB9RpECejPJYsU2MwTeT6J6AYs-1mkYl1a_Emwc/h0I9yQzcEwgdwWJRGR_HiZnO2aALcUKa/10)
And yet...

The media moved on.

The Left refused to condemn it.

And instead of cooling the temperature--Democrats doubled down, calling Trump a _**" threat to democracy"**_ and his supporters _**" extremists."

**_
Let me be clear:
👉 I stand with President Trump.
👉 I stand with the America First movement.
👉 And I will **NEVER** let the Radical Left silence our voice with violence.

But Patriot--this anniversary is a wake-up call.

Our majority in Congress is **hanging by a thread**.

The Radical Left is **bankrolling challengers** in swing seats.

And if they flip even a handful of districts--**it 's over.

**
We won't have the votes to secure the border.

We won't be able to protect your freedoms.

And we'll LOSE the firewall protecting President Trump from impeachment--_**again**.

_
That's why I need you now more than ever.

We have to fight like every seat matters--because it does.

If you stand with Trump, with me, and with the America First mission, chip in **ANY** amount today 👇

[RUSH A DONATION TO DEFEND OUR MAJORITY](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/EFYexYTzkVgjwPzEBE3pavR7n7cItrBAb6OYhosLDQfev2FifXKwffXbW2k1Bfq02v4Rbe3gmMsBtykxX5Xw2QcyHxiLKhNt4WliAKpteESnTEXP8uhC5oVI1O-c8I5v_ShCHASJvWY_uKS6LT8Yj27hlAs5C7sA8ygY6nxtdUV4VpvXDTZ-Oe6qER1myVUJc3qv0WG0_WLSHmNQ3OFPTw-P6_ok7-SYDlwOMxXtI4YujEk4pBsdMxjBEML2L1eg-fyy6Z3CE1LLdMddDeuPMp6KimbZdYsZGfCqecbmY8ORtays-42KAIvdpspVX6V5ZpYWLA3wg8tSiwoFCx1Nb0ZeWIYU5pdEiqlnLp791x4qlz7KzBs5H4-WW9cBYNTrx7jZxC8aJroKf6DC1pKOVqZAJU4d9Sh3MuA79MD1WJOYl1IaxWD11-foUEPXler-rhi01ITB3SPpPU_18l5r9WI19aHMX-enzlT5u6Qvs5Ll6JF6QRXNBNPur-JUWQQSvPjgGZmwvUBAhCOkg-BokH-yUlg1meHSo7ox7DW_O0xIGD7NzPViMvaMFdW-4268WCvLdrYr19j2XuRfxDpndGxB1DQRdRbBcqj4Yr8mjsZgGmvULAsPoqc5lT0MzDOJniS3GOqMmwmiTKvNkJc-10ajnS3IskBISOeZTODITLoa1FMz2JiVvm5gcoU1RozdB56XthX9rDI9BFpd31nZ-nwD1spPFdh_E8izpZI8gCezFvLbiDqHFJG4u4sU_QvjaK8i/W7wbyE6a9eOWWshEWt1HIMyt4llFWrWf/10)

| | |
Hi friend, it's Senator Kirsten Gillibrand. This is one of the longest emails I've sent as DSCC Chair -- **but it's also the most important.**

**I hope you'll read every word, but if you can't, here's the top-line info:** [**The DSCC has their mid-month deadline coming up -- and without your help, we could fall _short_ of the $125,000 goal we need to raise to flip the Senate. Will you chip in $5 to make sure we hit it? Your gift will be tripled, but only until the deadline.**](https://go.dscc.org/go/21885?amount=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20250713-KirstenGillibrand-GillibrandLongForm-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20250713-KirstenGillibrand-GillibrandLongForm-Pros-sl1&akid=20733%2E9716520%2EiiizIU)

| | [DONATE $5 (BECOMES $15)](https://go.dscc.org/go/21885?amount=5&t=6&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20250713-KirstenGillibrand-GillibrandLongForm-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20250713-KirstenGillibrand-GillibrandLongForm-Pros-sl1&akid=20733%2E9716520%2EiiizIU)
---

Here are three things you need to know right now:

**DONALD TRUMP IS HURTING -- NOT HELPING -- HARDWORKING AMERICANS.**

Trump's second term is already as bad as we've feared. Worse, even. He's packing our government with MAGA loyalists, undermining our democracy, and grabbing unprecedented power and wealth for himself -- but he's done _nothing_ to tackle the issues that matter most, like lowering costs and protecting Social Security.

**SENATE REPUBLICANS ARE PUSHING TRUMP'S DANGEROUS AGENDA.**

On issue after issue, the Republican majority has moved in lockstep with Trump -- no matter how far he pushes our country in the wrong direction. They're rubber-stamping his radical, unqualified nominees and actually defending his pardoning of hundreds of January 6th insurrectionists!

Fortunately, we have an alternative path forward:

**DEMOCRATS HAVE A _REAL CHANCE_ TO TAKE BACK THE SENATE.**

With multiple special elections in key battlegrounds on the map and some of the most vulnerable Republicans up for reelection, we have no shortage of chances to flip seats blue. But to win back a majority and deliver on the issues that matter most, I'll need Democrats like you on my side.

[**So today, with our critical mid-month deadline approaching, I'm asking you to make a difference. Will you chip in $5 (becomes $15) to fight for a Democratic Senate majority that will fight for you?**](https://go.dscc.org/go/21885?amount=5&t=7&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20250713-KirstenGillibrand-GillibrandLongForm-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20250713-KirstenGillibrand-GillibrandLongForm-Pros-sl1&akid=20733%2E9716520%2EiiizIU)

We need your help to fight for the Senate. If you've saved your payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately.

| [Donate $5 (Becomes $15)](https://go.dscc.org/go/21885?amount=5&t=8&amount=5&express_lane=true&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20250713-KirstenGillibrand-GillibrandLongForm-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20250713-KirstenGillibrand-GillibrandLongForm-Pros-sl1&akid=20733%2E9716520%2EiiizIU)

If Covid taught us anything, it's that we are arguably more brainwashed and controlled than ever before in the history of mankind...

# [Why I didn't vaccinate my newborn and have no regrets](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3072780&post_id=168208477&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MjA4NDc3LCJpYXQiOjE3NTI0MTg3MzIsImV4cCI6MTc1NTAxMDczMiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMwNzI3ODAiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.qeowQuo-mEHGEIjNvtNddH00zCVBY24J61oPNL3crAY)
### If Covid taught us anything, it's that we are arguably more brainwashed and controlled than ever before in the history of mankind...
| | [Richie McGinniss](https://substack.com/@richiemcginniss)
---
| Jul 13| | | ∙| | Guest post
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/@richiemcginniss)

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3072780&post_id=168208477&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MjA4NDc3LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1MjQxODczMiwiZXhwIjoxNzU1MDEwNzMyLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzA3Mjc4MCIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.gXIrGaMPM9RommpYCwksoaoQ9QWVjRAnggpiXxDKU7k&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3072780&post_id=168208477&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MjA4NDc3LCJpYXQiOjE3NTI0MTg3MzIsImV4cCI6MTc1NTAxMDczMiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMwNzI3ODAiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.qeowQuo-mEHGEIjNvtNddH00zCVBY24J61oPNL3crAY&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/politibrawl/p/why-i-didnt-vaccinate-my-newborn?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/30530d7f-b35c-488a-8e1c-29f190697195?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The year was 2023 and I was sitting in a doctor's office as the Quack implored me to allow--within minutes of my first-born child coming into this world--for the doctors to jab her with the Hepatitis B vaccine.
"It's very important to protect your infant from Hepatitis B."
"Okay how could she contract this terrible disease?"
"Well, if the mother has the virus, then this will protect your child and ensure she is Hep-B free!"
"Mom just got tested and she does not have the virus. And she doesn't plan on stepping on a syringe in a dark alley anytime between now and when the baby is born."
The doc did not look happy with my resistance. She pursed her lips but remained silent, waiting for my continued defense. A defense that she undoubtedly perceived to be dangerously stupid and recklessly anti-vaccine.
"Are there any other ways that my baby might be at risk?"
"Well, it's possible that she is injected with a dirty needle or that she steps on one on the playground."
"Well, in the near future the only thing she would be injected with is the vaccine that you are proposing, so let's cross that off the list. Can you point me to one documented instance of an infant stepping on a dirty syringe and contracting Hep B?"
She sat with her mouth agape, now making no effort to hide her disdain. But she couldn't cite an instance because there is no documented case of an infant contracting Hep B from a discarded needle.
**A message from our sponsor (piece continues below)**
* * *
**Together with Bonner Private Wines**
## **[Drink Wine That's NOT Made by Globalists](https://substack.com/redirect/58d4add7-a54b-4050-b3ae-64e1338e2d72?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/58d4add7-a54b-4050-b3ae-64e1338e2d72?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Want to know what's really in that $12 bottle at the store?
* Chemical dyes
* Fake oak flavoring
* Grape concentrate

These mass-market wines are made in factories owned by global corporations.
What they don't want you drinking? Wine made in tiny batches by honest, hardworking families -- deep in the Andes, or tucked away in the hills of Tuscany.
**[That's what we source at the Bonner Private Wine Partnership.](https://substack.com/redirect/58d4add7-a54b-4050-b3ae-64e1338e2d72?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
Every quarter, we ship 6 rare bottles -- from places so remote, most Americans will never even hear about them.
These wines aren't for sale in stores, and they'll never be featured on a celebrity's Instagram.
They're for people like you. Folks who still believe in hard work, tradition, and doing things the right way.
**[See What's Inside the Box - Join Now](https://substack.com/redirect/58d4add7-a54b-4050-b3ae-64e1338e2d72?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
You won't find these wines at Costco. And that's a good thing.
- Will Bonner
[Learn More](https://substack.com/redirect/58d4add7-a54b-4050-b3ae-64e1338e2d72?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
**(Piece continues)**
Yet, **[according to a recent interview between Tucker Carlson and RFK Jr.,](https://substack.com/redirect/4687fcfa-89e7-4e7f-bb17-ec9c09887339?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** there was significant evidence of negative side effects derived from injecting newborns with the vaccine, though because of the money that big pharma stood to lose from these findings, it was covered up. "The CDC did that study in 1999," RFK explained during the interview. "They looked at children who had received the vaccine within the first 30 days of life and compared those children to children who had received the vaccine later or not at all. And they found an 1135 percent elevated risk of autism in the vaccinated children. It shocked them and they kept the study secret."
This is an instance where piles of money trumped the scientific method, something that is all too common in the pay-for-care medical system in the United States. Anyone who watches cable news is aware that the media is largely funded by ads for drugs. What they may not know is the US is one of two developed countries where advertising drugs on television is legal. Year over year, the pharmaceutical lobby **[spends more](https://substack.com/redirect/81041116-a483-41c2-86a1-13404e611d6b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** than any other industry to peddle their influence in DC. If one wants to call me "anti-vax" for begging even one question about the dozens of injections on the vaccine schedule that nets hundreds of billions for massive multinationals that care more about money than my baby, then so be it.
I was called as much when I had questions about a different injection, this one being a brand new vaccine for Covid 19. I recall in late 2021, I posted a clip in which Tucker Carlson was discussing the merits and possible drawbacks of a new and experimental vaccine. The clip earned our Daily Caller page a strike on Meta's Facebook, a strike that threatened to destroy the video news business I had labored to build over the previous four years. Facebook/Meta CEO **[Mark Zuckerburg admitted that Biden's White House had pressured his company](https://substack.com/redirect/bc50d23f-73e9-47b5-b706-e32e3a637a83?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** to remove content questioning the Covid-19 vaccine**.** That's when I realized that our digital discourse is even more censorious and corporately captured than the medium of cable news.
When I left NBC/MSNBC in 2014, I turned towards digital media and never looked back. I felt like I was leaving a candle factory and enlisting with Tesla to bring free energy and light to the whole world. How hopelessly naive I had been.
If America doesn't have a town square through which we can freely discuss difficult topics, then our system is doomed. The last five years has shown that these platforms are more keen to appease these companies than allow an open conversation about the merits and drawbacks of the products that they put out.
If Covid taught us anything, it's that we are arguably more brainwashed and controlled than ever before in the history of mankind.
A cloud of computer servers hosted by some of the biggest companies in the world saves our messages to our loved ones, it oversees our conversations in public and private, and now with AI it even censors our discourse without human oversight.
Consider me a luddite but, in my opinion, the more tech we get, the dumber we become. With all the technological advances of the last 20 years, our conversations have only become more callous and less concerned with critical thought. Meanwhile, our scientific community is more captured by dollar signs for grants than positive outcomes for our kids.
230 million Americans took the Covid vaccine, presumably because politicians and media personalities--in close coordination with their friends in the pharmaceutical industry--implored them to believe that the vaccine was "safe and effective." As it turns out, the experimental MRNA vax was not necessarily safe and did little to "stop the spread."
We were told to put on masks and remain indoors, yet the best natural protection against Covid was sunlight, exercise, and fresh air.
I was one of the 20 percent of Americans who were skeptical of this unassailable push to get everyone onboard with the jab. It reminded me of the media blitz that proclaimed we had to "support the troops" and the invasion of Iraq. I know people who had to use fake vaccine cards to sneak into bars and even countries like Colombia and Indonesia. Close family friends disinvited me to Thanksgiving because I refused to comply with Fauci and the faux scientists who insisted that the vaccine would prevent transmission.
In the wake of this monumental, world-wide lie, I am left with a bitter taste in my mouth. I have the right to ask questions about what is injected into myself and my child, and if a doctor has a problem with that then they can go straight to hell.
* * *

name: The Democratic Party
subject: Good morning, Derek
body:
Please don’t scroll past this short read.
---
Good morning, Derek — we hope you won’t scroll past this short read. It’s Sunday, and we’re sure your inbox is crowded, so we’ll get straight to the point: [**We’re asking you to make a $3 contribution to the DNC ahead of our mid-month fundraising deadline on Tuesday.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-july-2025?amount=3&refcode=em_20250713_3944_fr&refcode2=3944_22930333_NKySPV&akid=3944%2E22930333%2ENKySPV) This is our first fundraising deadline since congressional Republicans voted to rip health care away from 17 million people. Which means it’s more important now than ever that we show strong opposition to their disastrous agenda. Here at the DNC, we’re fighting back: Investing in every single state party. Launching our “Organizing Summer” initiative to register voters _now._ Taking the Trump administration to court. This work is essential, but it takes a huge number of resources to keep running. If everyone reading this email pitched in $3 to help fund it, we’d not only exceed our mid-month fundraising goal — but we’d have the resources we need to power all of the work we’re doing. And that’s important — because Republicans are going to spend millions of dollars trying to drown our opposition out. Unfortunately, not everyone gives. And the scale of the work we are doing makes our goals that much higher. So we’re relying on people who understand how important these early contributions are to chip in to support the DNC today. Our average online contribution this month is $27. Some give more, some give less. All that matters is you’re choosing to chip in ahead of our mid-month deadline on Tuesday. [**If fighting back against Trump and electing Democrats up and down the ballot is worth $3 to you, that’s the contribution we’re asking you to make today, if you can:**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-july-2025?amount=3&refcode=em_20250713_3944_fr&refcode2=3944_22930333_NKySPV&akid=3944%2E22930333%2ENKySPV) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $3](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-july-2025?express_lane=true&amount=3&refcode=em_20250713_3944_fr&refcode2=3944_22930333_NKySPV&akid=3944%2E22930333%2ENKySPV)**

clean_body: Please don’t scroll past this short read. Good morning, Derek — we hope you won’t scroll past this short read. It’s Sunday, and we’re sure your inbox is crowded, so we’ll get straight to the point: This is our first fundraising deadline since congressional Republicans voted to rip health care away from 17 million people. Which means it’s more important now than ever that we show strong opposition to their disastrous agenda. Here at the DNC, we’re fighting back: Investing in every single state party. Launching our “Organizing Summer” initiative to register voters now. Taking the Trump administration to court. This work is essential, but it takes a huge number of resources to keep running. If everyone reading this email pitched in $3 to help fund it, we’d not only exceed our mid-month fundraising goal — but we’d have the resources we need to power all of the work we’re doing. And that’s important — because Republicans are going to spend millions of dollars trying to drown our opposition out. Unfortunately, not everyone gives. And the scale of the work we are doing makes our goals that much higher. So we’re relying on people who understand how important these early contributions are to chip in to support the DNC today. Our average online contribution this month is $27. Some give more, some give less. All that matters is you’re choosing to chip in ahead of our mid-month deadline on Tuesday. If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly. | | | ** | | | | | | | ** Thank you for standing with us. The Democrats If you gave recently via check or mail, our system may not have yet processed your gift. We are appreciative and thank you greatly for your support._

name: The Antimonopolist
subject: Why we need to talk about crypto
body:
Hi there. As I wrote about last month in The Nation, it's really important to think about the cloud and AI in physical terms. The etherealization of tech hides real, material presence of data centers, as well as the power structures behind them. The cloud or "virtual currency" is not some immaterial set of ideas, but millions of transactions powered on electricity. Today, I want to talk more about crypto and power. Not metaphoric power, but literal power: the rate at which energy is transferre | | | | [](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/49ee41fe?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)
---
[The Antimonopolist](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/bb1a8ae0?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)
| [Why we need to talk about crypto](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/52ba5933?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)
---
| By Zephyr Teachout • 13 Jul 2025 | [View in browser](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/13629c65?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)
---|---
[View in browser](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/ff751ec7?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)
Seneca lake, getting boiled and battered by bitcoin. Credit: Andre Carrotflower, CC via Wikimedia Commons.
| | | Hi there. As I wrote about last month in [_The Nation_](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/f8d70937?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21), it's really important to think about the cloud and AI in physical terms. The etherealization of tech hides real, material presence of data centers, as well as the power structures behind them. The cloud or "virtual currency" is not some immaterial set of ideas, but millions of transactions powered on electricity.Today, I want to talk more about crypto and power. Not metaphoric power, but literal power: the rate at which energy is transferred.I was inspired to write this because of a disaster that happened earlier this month. A Bitcoin mining plant [dumped oil](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/7a618678?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21) into a protected wetland near Seneca Lake in upstate New York. The spill came from a retrofitted fossil fuel power plant which draws over 100 million gallons of water a day from the lake to cool its operations and dumps it back as hot as 108°F, fueling toxic algae blooms. You could see it coming. [Five years ago](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/0ab1f375?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21), locals reported that Seneca lake was "so warm you feel like you're in a hot tub," and have been protesting to shut it down. Clearly, we need to talk about crypto's energy use. As of now, cryptocurrency mining uses about 2.3 percent of all U.S. electricity. That is more than the total electricity used for all residential lighting in America. One Bitcoin transaction has been estimated to generate greenhouse gas emissions equivalent to driving a mid-sized car [1,000 miles](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/154e9a35?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21). Some places are beginning to ban local mining, but crypto operations are constantly on the hunt for deregulated zones with cheap energy. New York's spill wasn't an aberration. It was the predictable result of that hunt. In New York, new mining is temporarily banned, but older operations were grandfathered in.The issue here is structural. Bitcoin and similar cryptocurrencies rely on "Proof of Work" systems, which reward miners with tokens for completing complex computational problems. These systems do not accidentally consume energy. They are _built_ to reward energy consumption. If you wanted to set up a system designed to maximally burn fuel, you might design crypto. I found this post placing cryptocurriencies in the history of currency helpful: [Cryptocurrencies I](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/f3b63368?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21).
> Bitcoin isn't validated by its code, but by vast amounts of compute power required to create, transfer, and verify transactions. .. Humanity has deemed shells, metals, paper, and electronic ledgers as currency.. now the Tech boys advocate we use an almost innumerable opening and closing of transistors.
Costello [explains](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/389db964?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21) how crypto replaces trust with electricity and authority with computation. The result is a monetary system backed not by law or labor, but by the brute fact of burned energy.So with crypto, we are not burning carbon to light a child's homework or heat a kitchen. We are burning it to build "trust" and attempt to create a new form of money. What I hear in so many of my crypto enthusiast friends is not love of burning the planet, but a desire for coin not controlled by the state, and buried within that, a post-state utopia. That desire is not trivial. It is a core part of contemporary politics, and it's worth understanding. A few years ago, Matt Stoller [wrote a great explanation](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/a2417f28?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21) of the urge to embrace visions of new currencies, arguing that crypto takes real discontent and channels it into speculative finance and faux-antimonopoly tech libertarianism. Highly recommend. If you're a progressive focused on food deserts or UnitedHealthcare, crypto might feel like a distraction. The left has not been immune to the etherealization of tech discussions, thinking of it as not as real as the price of eggs or gig work contracts. But we need to care about crypto because it leads to toxic spills, a hotter planet, and it is driving up utility bills. And its definitely shaping the big political debates today. If you've been following the "abundance agenda" promoted by figures like [Marc Andreessen](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/594f808c?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21) and the network around the venture-backed Abundance Institute, you'll notice a core focus is energy. Specifically, nuclear and permitting reform. That push is not because we are naturally turning on more lights. It is because of the energy demands of crypto and AI. [Support this newsletter](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/d8aeacd1?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21). So when you hear a lot about the revival of nuclear, think crypto and AI. When you hear about permitting reform pushed by the Abundance agenda, think crypto and AI. This is not my daily bread. Mostly, when I talk about crypto, it is because I study anti-corruption laws. In the corruption world, crypto has all kinds of scammy uses. Donald Trump is currently violating the Constitution's Emoluments Clause by accepting foreign government money through his crypto ventures, at a scale that makes the hotel grift of his first presidency look quaint. And that was not quaint. Saudi money appears to have directly shaped U.S. foreign policy.Crypto is not immaterial. It is oil, heat, infrastructure, and emissions. And Trump corruption, as a cherry on top. If we are serious about water and oil and protecting democracy from big tech, we have to start seeing it in physical terms.More reading:
* How crypto is [eating renewable power ](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/9c6efdde?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)
* The [power concentration problem behind the green crypto alternatives](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/581bee62?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21).
* Earthjustice on the [unknown amounts of energy subsidies for crypto](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/428ef8a2?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21).

Remember to save a swear for Bitcoin next time you see your energy bill.More soon, Zephyr PS: I turned on the comments! Hoping you join in the conversation and tell me where you disagree. PPS: [Support this newsletter](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/972e9422?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21). I have to pay the data bills!
---
| [ Comment ](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/b5fcd58d?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)
---

The cloud or "virtual currency" is not some immaterial set of ideas, but millions of transactions powered on electricity. Today, I want to talk more about crypto and power. Not metaphoric power, but literal power: the rate at which energy is transferre | | | | | | By Zephyr Teachout • 13 Jul 2025 | ---|--- Seneca lake, getting boiled and battered by bitcoin. Credit: Andre Carrotflower, CC via Wikimedia Commons. | | | Hi there. As I wrote about last month in , it's really important to think about the cloud and AI in physical terms. The etherealization of tech hides real, material presence of data centers, as well as the power structures behind them. The cloud or "virtual currency" is not some immaterial set of ideas, but millions of transactions powered on electricity.Today, I want to talk more about crypto and power. Not metaphoric power, but literal power: the rate at which energy is transferred.I was inspired to write this because of a disaster that happened earlier this month. A Bitcoin mining plant into a protected wetland near Seneca Lake in upstate New York. The spill came from a retrofitted fossil fuel power plant which draws over 100 million gallons of water a day from the lake to cool its operations and dumps it back as hot as 108°F, fueling toxic algae blooms. You could see it coming. , locals reported that Seneca lake was "so warm you feel like you're in a hot tub," and have been protesting to shut it down. Clearly, we need to talk about crypto's energy use. As of now, cryptocurrency mining uses about 2.3 percent of all U.S. electricity. That is more than the total electricity used for all residential lighting in America. One Bitcoin transaction has been estimated to generate greenhouse gas emissions equivalent to driving a mid-sized car . Some places are beginning to ban local mining, but crypto operations are constantly on the hunt for deregulated zones with cheap energy. New York's spill wasn't an aberration. It was the predictable result of that hunt. In New York, new mining is temporarily banned, but older operations were grandfathered in.The issue here is structural. Bitcoin and similar cryptocurrencies rely on "Proof of Work" systems, which reward miners with tokens for completing complex computational problems. These systems do not accidentally consume energy. They are built to reward energy consumption. If you wanted to set up a system designed to maximally burn fuel, you might design crypto. I found this post placing cryptocurriencies in the history of currency helpful: . Bitcoin isn't validated by its code, but by vast amounts of compute power required to create, transfer, and verify transactions. .. Humanity has deemed shells, metals, paper, and electronic ledgers as currency.. now the Tech boys advocate we use an almost innumerable opening and closing of transistors. Costello how crypto replaces trust with electricity and authority with computation. The result is a monetary system backed not by law or labor, but by the brute fact of burned energy.So with crypto, we are not burning carbon to light a child's homework or heat a kitchen. We are burning it to build "trust" and attempt to create a new form of money. What I hear in so many of my crypto enthusiast friends is not love of burning the planet, but a desire for coin not controlled by the state, and buried within that, a post-state utopia. That desire is not trivial. It is a core part of contemporary politics, and it's worth understanding. A few years ago, Matt Stoller of the urge to embrace visions of new currencies, arguing that crypto takes real discontent and channels it into speculative finance and faux-antimonopoly tech libertarianism. Highly recommend. If you're a progressive focused on food deserts or UnitedHealthcare, crypto might feel like a distraction. The left has not been immune to the etherealization of tech discussions, thinking of it as not as real as the price of eggs or gig work contracts. But we need to care about crypto because it leads to toxic spills, a hotter planet, and it is driving up utility bills. And its definitely shaping the big political debates today. If you've been following the "abundance agenda" promoted by figures like and the network around the venture-backed Abundance Institute, you'll notice a core focus is energy. Specifically, nuclear and permitting reform. That push is not because we are naturally turning on more lights. It is because of the energy demands of crypto and AI. . So when you hear a lot about the revival of nuclear, think crypto and AI. When you hear about permitting reform pushed by the Abundance agenda, think crypto and AI. This is not my daily bread. Mostly, when I talk about crypto, it is because I study anti-corruption laws. In the corruption world, crypto has all kinds of scammy uses. Donald Trump is currently violating the Constitution's Emoluments Clause by accepting foreign government money through his crypto ventures, at a scale that makes the hotel grift of his first presidency look quaint. And that was not quaint. Saudi money appears to have directly shaped U.S. foreign policy.Crypto is not immaterial. It is oil, heat, infrastructure, and emissions. And Trump corruption, as a cherry on top. If we are serious about water and oil and protecting democracy from big tech, we have to start seeing it in physical terms.More reading: How crypto is The . * Earthjustice on the . Remember to save a swear for Bitcoin next time you see your energy bill.More soon, Zephyr PS: I turned on the comments! Hoping you join in the conversation and tell me where you disagree. PPS: . I have to pay the data bills! | | The Antimonopolist (C) 2025 -

A few weeks ago, the U.S. Supreme Court announced it would not hear _L.M. v. Town of Middleborough_ , a case where a Massachusetts school punished a student for wearing a t-shirt to school with the words "There are only two genders."

Liam's case was about much more than just a t-shirt. The school actively promotes its view about gender through posters and "Pride" events, and it encourages students to wear clothing with messages on the same topic--so long as that clothing expresses the school's preferred views on the subject.

This is a disappointing development--a setback for free speech.

By denying the case, the Supreme Court leaves in place a lower court ruling that denied Liam's clear constitutional right to free speech.

We believe public school officials cannot censor a 7th grader's speech by forcing him to remove a shirt that respectfully states scientific fact. This is a gross violation of the First Amendment.

Do you agree with Liam that there are only two genders?
##

name: Heartland Patriots HQ
subject: the so-called “Big Beautiful Bill”
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
What makes you most worried about this bill now that it has officially been signed into law?
#
Peter,
The GOP’s so-called “Big Beautiful Bill” is expected to have far-reaching impacts on everyday Americans — especially people and families who rely on critical public benefits like healthcare through Medicaid and food assistance through SNAP.
**So today, if you’re willing to share, we’re asking: What makes you most worried about the so-called “Big Beautiful Bill” now that it has officially been signed into law?**

name: Mamdani Poll Alert via Our Revolution
subject: Tell the Democratic Establishment: Stand With Us, Not Billionaires!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ stdin# ][IMG]

Peter Willis,

It's official – progressive champion Zohran Mamdani has a 10 point lead in
the latest poll in the race for NYC mayor, surging ahead thanks to
grassroots organizing and bold progressive values.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32811?source=email-20250713&t=1011&akid=9002%2E5885482%2Ec1m0UC ]Headline: Zohran Mamdani leads general election poll for NYC mayor

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32811?source=email-20250713&t=1012&akid=9002%2E5885482%2Ec1m0UC ]With the Democratic establishment lining up to defeat him, NOW is the
moment to double down and show overwhelming support. Can you urgently send
a clear message that the Democratic establishment MUST stand with us and
back Mamdani – not billionaires and special interests?

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32811?source=email-20250713&t=1013&akid=9002%2E5885482%2Ec1m0UC ]ADD YOUR NAME

While Mamdani leads the polls, current disgraced NYC Mayor Eric Adams is
busy raising massive amounts of cash to try to run as an independent and
take Mamdani down. He just held a swanky fundraiser packed with Wall
Street elite and billionaire donors determined to stop progressive change.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32811?source=email-20250713&t=1014&akid=9002%2E5885482%2Ec1m0UC ]Headline: Mayor Eric Adams gets ritzy Hamptons fundraiser as people
line up with 'oodles of cash' after Mamdani win: sources

Billionaires are lining up to defeat Mamdani, desperate to keep NYC
controlled by money – not grassroots movements. But Mamdani's top spot in
this latest poll shows us the path forward is clear: voters crave
transformative change and bold policies to help the working class.

This is a battle for the future of the Democratic party. And Our
Revolution is out there every day fighting to ensure that power lies with
the people, not big money donors.

If Mamdani can win New York City, it will inspire grassroots progressive
victories across the country – proving yet again that our strength lies in
people power, not billionaire fundraisers.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32811?source=email-20250713&t=1015&akid=9002%2E5885482%2Ec1m0UC ]Tell establishment Democrats: It's NOW or NEVER. Send a loud and
unequivocal message that the Democratic Party must support Zohran Mamdani
– and the communities he represents – at this critical moment.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32811?source=email-20250713&t=1016&akid=9002%2E5885482%2Ec1m0UC ]ADD YOUR NAME

When we organize, we win.

The Our Revolution Team

# [Trump Haters Reid Hoffman and Marc Elias Lead Democrats' Aggressive Financial Push for 2026 Midterm Election Victories](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1123743&post_id=168220803&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MjIwODAzLCJpYXQiOjE3NTI0MjE5NjYsImV4cCI6MTc1NTAxMzk2NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTExMjM3NDMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.yjHnUHgrwcHZCdvhV5hFEVQNwC_1gt1bdLf197rhpvw)

**FEC Filings Reveal Massive Democrat War Chest**
New Federal Election Commission (FEC) filings released in early July 2025 reveal that top Democrat donors are mobilizing significant financial resources to reclaim the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. Among these donors are LinkedIn co-founder, former Jeffrey Epstein associate and longtime Democrat financier Reid Hoffman, along with high-profile Democrat election attorney Marc Elias.
## **Hoffman Funnels Over $300,000 to DCCC**
On May 13, 2025, Reid Hoffman donated $310,100 to the Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee (DCCC). The donation reflects his continued alignment with House Democrats, despite widespread criticism over their increasingly extreme positions. The party's growing radical image has been shaped by several high-profile incidents involving its members, including assault, calls for violence, and support for illegal undocumented alien child rapists and HAMAS supporters.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..ufXn7vK6XHWxf3ELalhtZD1QRecfZmqB2r10-OUfMd8?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=168220803)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d8e91149-7f4c-4cd8-8ec3-16b212d99878?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Democrat Representative Jasmine Crockett has drawn national attention for issuing public threats of violence toward Republican senators, and she is on video physically assaulting _Loomer Unleashed_ DC Correspondent Charles Downs. Democrat Representative LaMonica McIver is under scrutiny for physically assaulting an ICE agent.
**CAUGHT ON CAMERA:[Rep. McIver Shifts Blame to ICE After Assaulting Agents, Then Attacks DHS Secretary During Homeland Security Hearing](https://substack.com/redirect/293b5c85-2709-452b-a2b3-8d9260f38145?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
Meanwhile, Representative Ilhan Omar refused to condemn a Hamas flag displayed by anti-Trump protesters outside the White House, and House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries has remained silent in the face of mounting violent rhetoric within his own caucus.
> **EXCLUSIVE VIDEO:[Rep. Ilhan Omar Declines To Condemn Hamas Flag Displayed Outside Congress During Anti-Trump Protests](https://substack.com/redirect/96fcd2ba-6416-4b2b-9055-a778c838d5c1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/75f3f57b-3507-45f0-bdb1-905441dc27d0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
## **LinkedIn Billionaire Co-Founders Back Radical House Democrats**
Evidence of Silicon Valley's support for far-left members of Congress extends beyond Hoffman. LinkedIn co-founder Allen Blue also donated to Rep. Jasmine Crockett after her highly publicized outbursts in spring 2025. This included threatening remarks directed at Senator Ted Cruz.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/274348f4-66f3-42f2-8d69-1fdb2a41e296?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Soon after, Crockett funneled $50,000 to the DCCC.
**READ THE STORY:[Jasmine Crockett Rakes In Campaign Cash From LinkedIn Co-Founder As She Funnels $50K to DCCC](https://substack.com/redirect/bbb14c25-ffd1-453f-a79f-cc096b90c3f3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/37a6eceb-14a6-4dc0-a378-56f5c3067425?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Rep. McIver similarly contributed $30,000 to the committee, despite ongoing controversy.
**VIOLENCE PAYS:[Freshman Rep. McIver Assaults ICE Agent, Recently Funneled $30K to DCCC](https://substack.com/redirect/3de0cf73-9092-4d84-90bc-aa001b162e1f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8db01fd3-cc01-4251-928b-ebbccf778094?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
## **DCCC Pays Marc Elias $618,000 for Legal Services**
Just three days after Hoffman's contribution, on May 16, 2025, the DCCC paid $618,144.39 to Elias Law Group LLP. The firm, led by longtime Democrat election lawyer Marc Elias, received payments for "Recount Legal Services" and general "Legal Services Rendered."
Elias has built a reputation for challenging election outcomes on behalf of Democrats. He played a central role in failed attempts to overturn Senator Bob Casey's loss in Pennsylvania in 2024. The Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee (DSCC) also paid over $500,000 to Elias's firm earlier this year for similar legal efforts.
> **GET THE SCOOP:[DSCC Funnels Nearly $700K to Elias Law Firm Ahead of 2026 Midterms](https://substack.com/redirect/b5358e90-4e7e-4f72-bec6-269c4874e502?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e3583913-4179-4a35-951f-afc1d12262ad?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
## **Elias Publishes 2026 Legal Strategy**
On May 20, 2025, Elias published a strategy [article](https://substack.com/redirect/9cf09905-d127-4787-baef-c06f7ebd2676?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on his website, Democracy Docket, signaling a coming wave of election lawsuits. He wrote:
"It also serves as a roadmap to potentially wipe out [Trump's] congressional majorities in 2026, exposing him as a fundamentally unpopular politician... Rather than searching for signs of decency among Republicans, we must honestly assess our willingness to fight."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/26612580-7e68-46d7-8aa2-ab1957849a85?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/40bbe542-0bde-4617-bc91-fccc412d0dbd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/9b70931c-a813-43a0-9671-06083dd83ea1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
## **Legal Spending Suggests Democrats Know Their Agenda Is Unpopular**
The aggressive legal spending and coordinated donations suggests Democrat Party leadership is aware that their radical agenda lacks broad support. Rather than moderate their policies, they appear focused on funding recount strategies, legal battles, and procedural maneuvers to retain or regain power. By heavily investing in election litigation well ahead of the 2026 vote, Democrats are signaling a clear intent to contest unfavorable outcomes and solidify control through the courts and a continued judicial coup--rather than by appealing to the majority of voters or toning down their extreme rhetoric and policies.

VIOLENCE PAYS: | | ---|---|--- DCCC Pays Marc Elias $618,000 for Legal Services Just three days after Hoffman's contribution, on May 16, 2025, the DCCC paid $618,144.39 to Elias Law Group LLP. The firm, led by longtime Democrat election lawyer Marc Elias, received payments for "Recount Legal Services" and general "Legal Services Rendered." Elias has built a reputation for challenging election outcomes on behalf of Democrats. He played a central role in failed attempts to overturn Senator Bob Casey's loss in Pennsylvania in 2024. The Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee (DSCC) also paid over $500,000 to Elias's firm earlier this year for similar legal efforts. GET THE SCOOP: | | ---|---|--- Elias Publishes 2026 Legal Strategy On May 20, 2025, Elias published a strategy on his website, Democracy Docket, signaling a coming wave of election lawsuits. He wrote: "It also serves as a roadmap to potentially wipe out [Trump's] congressional majorities in 2026, exposing him as a fundamentally unpopular politician... Rather than searching for signs of decency among Republicans, we must honestly assess our willingness to fight." | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- Legal Spending Suggests Democrats Know Their Agenda Is Unpopular The aggressive legal spending and coordinated donations suggests Democrat Party leadership is aware that their radical agenda lacks broad support. Rather than moderate their policies, they appear focused on funding recount strategies, legal battles, and procedural maneuvers to retain or regain power. By heavily investing in election litigation well ahead of the 2026 vote, Democrats are signaling a clear intent to contest unfavorable outcomes and solidify control through the courts and a continued judicial coup--rather than by appealing to the majority of voters or toning down their extreme rhetoric and policies. name: Rashida Tlaib via United for Democracy
subject: Shut down Guantánamo Now
disclaimer_text: Paid for by United for Democracy
body:
Trump must halt immigration detention at Guantánamo Bay, not expand it. | |
---
Peter, Trump and other Republicans are trying to spend billions of taxpayer dollars to expand mass incarceration, including proposing to deport and imprison thousands of immigrants in notorious overseas torture prisons. Recently ICE has been racially profiling U.S. residents and targeting people with tattoos, kidnapping them and sending some to Guantánamo Bay, a facility infamous for human rights abuses.1 Just last month the corrupt far-right members of the Supreme Court ruled to permit deportations of asylum-seekers and undocumented immigrants who’ve committed no crimes—not to their home countries but to random third countries where they lack community and legal status. They ruled that presidents can deport people, without meaningful review, to wherever a president designates a “safe country.” **I’m fighting back against these horrific governmental attacks on immigrants and on our constitutional rights.** I just led 15 of my colleagues in Congress in calling on the Trump administration to immediately end the use of Guantánamo Bay for migrant detention—and to permanently close the detention facilities there. [**Will you sign on today to join our letter demanding the immediate, permanent closure of Guantánamo Bay?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm38h9nrsCCiMen3CGd5zila9NcO5XPDx50DbF_L6A7b7rME-BP2JNuDHUjm_FUJYq7mFQelqY7D8aWQibp_1-_jHoU7t7HbLwQVVBlVKcZlSdQVXjqvGCGJ1j0LBFglwl6y1nGNfP-v4tM01KZ-zDeqI-OZNp7Z9ALczQOBxwnNJB0XxvhkCrsp7UYFTEbrwFicXEpvDYV5hru7mITAw9mWPSUB_XTGQocsdXcS58qX2FgV_xS2B5XoYCvqjELSexTdgeTmFx6rty8fa6a-RyPQl7DScwDpzp6lXzT_g723HZUEFxjemThg6Bhln8_prkoMrKbLCtGp4-EOjqGmauZQPLPaezwguTyK_uwj0sK33opoawb_5DGytXlK_tCJoS8l3cdw1XQBSbvL2thwOcvyn-FlHdda3uZJ7cRIpegOxhLG_4hWYKwBuCDI0ShKGKpEZoTLkaeW3rIam3ybU2OBXN5ViYrWqyHIfjcrHHilOLdRSR2KnDjHATdld2PmBw6DO60g7yG6jtiNKGYu52qEH66WfkweV8HTFp35Mqx5RCw/4i5/RiVmar5rQO2f4JFEke9YuA/h0/h001.J_RVePMXvVixFAyM95eK5jKQrM1GIM0-84RxlF7Xc0g) Fifty-five organizations endorsed our letter, which also demanded the administration _“ensure that all detained individuals are treated with dignity, due process, and the protections guaranteed under both U.S. and international law.”_ We must defend the rights of immigrants of all statuses _and_ U.S. citizens, who are also swept up in ICE’s raids and sometimes illegally deported.2 During Trump’s last term, ICE unconstitutionally deported at least 70 U.S. citizens, and that number is sure to rise in this term—unless we do something about it.3 I’ve worked as an immigration attorney, I’m a daughter of immigrants, and I’ve been to inhumane immigration detention facilities, so I’ve seen our broken and dehumanizing immigration system up close. I’ve held the Department of Homeland Security accountable for its violations of our shared rights, and I’ve introduced policies to protect immigrants’ rights and free people from ICE facilities. **It is my duty as a Congresswoman, and an American, to raise hell until immigrants seeking safety and a better life are treated with dignity and respect, and have real paths to citizenship.** We have urgent work to do, and it includes fighting back against the fascist attacks on immigrants, including demanding the end of mass immigrant detention and deportations. [**Will you add your name as a grassroots co-signer to our congressional letter demanding the Trump administration immediately halt immigrant detention at Guantánamo Bay, and to permanently close the detention facilities there?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm38h9nrsCCiMen3CGd5zila9NcO5XPDx50DbF_L6A7b7rME-BP2JNuDHUjm_FUJYq7mFQelqY7D8aWQibp_1-_jHoU7t7HbLwQVVBlVKcZlSdQVXjqvGCGJ1j0LBFglwl6y1nGNfP-v4tM01KZ-zDeqI-OZNp7Z9ALczQOBxwnNJB0XxvhkCrsp7UYFTEbrwFicXEpvDYV5hru7mITAw9mWPSUB_XTGQocsdXcS58qX2FgV_xS2B5XoYCvqjELSexTdgeTmFx6rty8fa6a-RyPQl7DScwDpzp6lXzT_g723HZUEFxjemThg6Bhln8_prkoMrKbLCtGp4-EOjqGmauZQPZG4sSTySKftlSJuvX17eX5ciXlvgY8eqCCF76q7g5VTMBNNVFVrbYCyaP3Wws5Yku_D00WArrSYOpRXQ7_oG1CZiBE3VzZ3ETnf8f8U820vyYVeLlr5e37lcdt-gVvXFiH0KOfPcoOxY1s-V34WW15lGvcEOQBArK9zqbsvxZ7HLObafT5NZ7XPCThl02eyZCXiT6BUTkH_NEe5tPKahVw/4i5/RiVmar5rQO2f4JFEke9YuA/h2/h001.ZHL-CdHvV5bGXdCXcDhQ-y-yXo8D-dIcsT7ofdmJu3U) Thank you for taking action. Together we’re demanding a government that invests in people’s needs rather than spending hundreds of billions of dollars on mass arrests, detentions, and deportations. clean_body: The Radical Left thought we were going to break...but they were wrong. | - Peter, That day in Butler, PA...the world held its breath. The Radical Left thought we were going to break...but they were wrong. That day didn’t stop our movement...it ignited it. From that day forward, we’ve only had one message—we will always FIGHT, FIGHT, FIGHT, for our movement and America. And one year later, we haven’t backed down. We cannot let that happen. Not after everything we’ve built together. | Butler was the spark of a political wildfire. And we’re only just getting started. Thank you, Royce White PAID FOR BY ROYCE WHITE FOR SENATE P.O. name: Jeff Merkley
subject: That’s what I’m fighting for
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
We believe in an America where every family has the opportunity to thrive, no matter their ZIP code, income, or background.
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111416507/557152099/150430950?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTMyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MDMxNmMyOC0wMzYwLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=n19q_lKzotR50TRrWB64OJrFr_xxwdL4BHC1nqs22ds=&emci=e098591d-a35e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=80316c28-0360-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=7731215)

| | Affordable housing. Health care that doesn't break the bank. Clean air and water for our kids. A "We the People" democracy that actually works for the people, not for billionaires and corporate polluters. **That's what this campaign is about. That's what I'm fighting for.** I launched my re-election campaign earlier this week because everything we care about is on the line. Donald Trump and his MAGA minions are on a mission to drag us backward — shredding our freedoms and funneling massive tax breaks to the ultra-wealthy while gutting Medicaid and vital services for working people. **Our vision is different.** We believe in an America where every family has the opportunity to thrive, no matter their ZIP code, income, or background. To make that vision a reality, we need to build a strong grassroots campaign from day one. As we close out our campaign launch week, I'm fired up, grateful for the support of this grassroots team, and ready to keep building this movement together. [**I'm asking for your help to finish this week strong. If you've been waiting for the right moment to chip in — this is it. **We're building a campaign powered by people, not corporate PACs.** That means we're counting on folks like you to help us reach our goals, stay competitive, and get our message out.
With Trump and his MAGA cronies deliberately pushing our country to the brink — we can't afford to wait.
**Now is the time to dig in. To stand up and say: We believe in an America where the rules aren't rigged for the rich and everyone gets a fair shot.**
[**So before the day ends, can you help us close out this first week with a big grassroots push?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111416541/557152136/-2122444374?refcode=2025-07-13-launch&recurring=&amount=3&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=80316c28-0360-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTMyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MDMxNmMyOC0wMzYwLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=n19q_lKzotR50TRrWB64OJrFr_xxwdL4BHC1nqs22ds=)

That's what this campaign is about. That's what I'm fighting for. I launched my re-election campaign earlier this week because everything we care about is on the line. Donald Trump and his MAGA minions are on a mission to drag us backward — shredding our freedoms and funneling massive tax breaks to the ultra-wealthy while gutting Medicaid and vital services for working people. Our vision is different. We believe in an America where every family has the opportunity to thrive, no matter their ZIP code, income, or background. To make that vision a reality, we need to build a strong grassroots campaign from day one.
