name: Sarah McBride
subject: I voted no
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McBride for Delaware
body:
This is a long email.
#
This is a long email, but it’s a deeply personal one. I hope you’ll take a moment to give it a read.
**Last week, I voted no on Trump’s big, ugly budget bill.** It was one of the most heartless pieces of legislation that Congress had considered in decades.
This cruel budget greenlights the largest cut to Medicaid in American history. As a result, seventeen million Americans are set to lose access to affordable care, and it’ll be harder for everyone to receive adequate care. Republicans passed it anyway.
**When I think about what these cuts mean, I think about Andy.**
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-20250713-1?refcode=em20250713-2176&amount=5&refcode2=2176_21716_cIZJoR&akid=2176%2E21716%2EcIZJoR "A photo of Andy and me sitting on the stairs.")
Andy was my husband. Just one year into our relationship, he was diagnosed with cancer. Overnight, we went from planning our future together to fighting for his life. I became his primary caregiver. During his battle with terminal cancer, I held his hand through appointments, treatments, and, ultimately, when he passed away.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-20250713-1?refcode=em20250713-2176&amount=5&refcode2=2176_21716_cIZJoR&akid=2176%2E21716%2EcIZJoR "A photo of Andy and me on our wedding day.")
But we were lucky. Andy had access to health care. It gave us precious time together. But I also saw firsthand how fragile access can be — and how devastating it is when people don’t have it.
**Not everyone is as lucky as we were**. Too many families are forced to make impossible choices — between seeing a doctor or keeping the lights on, between paying for a life-saving prescription, or putting food on the table for their children.
Health care is a human right. In the wealthiest country on earth, no one should have to make choices like these.
But instead of making health care more affordable and accessible, Donald Trump and Republicans in Congress just gutted Medicaid — which will leave millions more Americans uninsured, all to finance tax breaks for billionaires.
That’s why I voted no. It’s why I’m fighting tooth and nail to ensure Republicans feel the consequences. And it’s why I’m asking for your help.
[**Will you pitch in $5 to help me keep up the fight for affordable health care and build the grassroots power we need to take back the House from GOP control?** ](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-20250713-1?refcode=em20250713-2176&amount=5&refcode2=2176_21716_cIZJoR&akid=2176%2E21716%2EcIZJoR)
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-20250713-1?amount=5&refcode=em20250713-2176&refcode2=2176_21716_cIZJoR&akid=2176%2E21716%2EcIZJoR)**
When I think about the road ahead, I think of Andy. I think about every family that’s fighting to hold on to hope. I think about what it means to lead with conviction. And that’s what’s fueling my fight.
Thank you for reading,
Sarah McBride
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-footer?refcode=em20250713-1&t=1&refcode2=2176_21716_cIZJoR&akid=2176%2E21716%2EcIZJoR)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/signup/receive_fewer_emails/?t=2&akid=2176%2E21716%2EcIZJoR)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/signup/update_your_information/?t=3&akid=2176%2E21716%2EcIZJoR)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=2176%2E21716%2EcIZJoR)

Email us: [info@sarahmcbride.com](mailto:info@sarahmcbride.com)
Paid for by McBride for Delaware

clean_body: Peter, I’m about to let you in on what’s going on behind the scenes, so listen up: | | Peter, I’m about to let you in on what’s going on behind the scenes, so listen up: = Republicans raised a record-breaking = to keep control of the House. = They used that massive war chest to launch attacks against = swing-seat Democrats. = The latest polls show a = race for the House majority. = = | = = = Peter, I’m done watching extreme MAGA Republicans spread their dishonest lies. What we do – or fail to do – right now could decide the course of this entire election. That’s why I’m asking you to make a critical decision at this moment: – We can either sit back and let Republicans lie about my Democratic colleagues and buy their way into power again... = = | = = | = = | = = Keep the faith, Hakeem Hakeem Jeffries is the Democratic Leader of the U.S. House of Representatives. Like many of us, Hakeem was raised by a working-class family; His mom was a social worker and his dad was a substance abuse counselor. Now, as the top Democrat in the House, he’s fighting to stop MAGA extremism and reclaim the majority for Democrats. Hakeem is committed to electing a record number of Democrats in the next election and beyond. But he can’t compete with Republicans’ special interest cash without our support. Is there any way you could chip in to help? = We know you’re getting a lot of emails, but that’s because Democrats have a clear path to winning back the House – and Hakeem isn’t letting a single second go to waste. name: Kirsten Gillibrand
subject: Disinformation is trending. That should worry us.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand for Senate
body:
Fox News is dominating YouTube, flooding platforms with right-wing misinformation.
**Right now, Fox News is dominating YouTube, with viewership surging over 109%.** That’s not just a fluke — it’s the result of a deliberate strategy to game algorithms and flood online platforms with right-wing talking points. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111418205/557158475/76250001?refcode=LOC_EM_PET_2025.07.13_MSNBC_B1_F1_S1_C1_45AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0OGQ5MDg1Zi0wNzYwLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SQ_JPOgywzVEDoyl03zMv4i-YDSX_4KX2P1Z8NkqPBs=&emci=4cf9530c-995e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=48d9085f-0760-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=7659029) MSNBC? Falling behind. Trusted voices are being fired, and at a moment when truth should be loudest, disinformation is climbing the charts. As someone who’s spent years fighting for accountability — from protecting voting rights to defending access to education and health care — I know this fight isn’t just about politics. It’s about what people believe is true. That’s why I’m speaking up — and why I’m asking you to join me. **If you believe we need stronger voices in the media and real accountability for spreading disinformation, I’m asking you to speak up.** [**_Add your name to demand accountability from platforms and support for real journalism. _**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111418206/557158476/76250001?refcode=LOC_EM_PET_2025.07.13_MSNBC_B1_F1_S1_C1_45AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0OGQ5MDg1Zi0wNzYwLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SQ_JPOgywzVEDoyl03zMv4i-YDSX_4KX2P1Z8NkqPBs=&emci=4cf9530c-995e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=48d9085f-0760-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=7659029) | | [STAND WITH MSNBC >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111418207/557158477/76250001?refcode=LOC_EM_PET_2025.07.13_MSNBC_B1_F1_S1_C1_45AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0OGQ5MDg1Zi0wNzYwLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SQ_JPOgywzVEDoyl03zMv4i-YDSX_4KX2P1Z8NkqPBs=&emci=4cf9530c-995e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=48d9085f-0760-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=7659029)

clean_body: Fox News is dominating YouTube, flooding platforms with right-wing misinformation. Right now, Fox News is dominating YouTube, with viewership surging over 109%. That’s not just a fluke — it’s the result of a deliberate strategy to game algorithms and flood online platforms with right-wing talking points. MSNBC? Falling behind. Trusted voices are being fired, and at a moment when truth should be loudest, disinformation is climbing the charts. As someone who’s spent years fighting for accountability — from protecting voting rights to defending access to education and health care — I know this fight isn’t just about politics. It’s about what people believe is true. That’s why I’m speaking up — and why I’m asking you to join me. If you believe we need stronger voices in the media and real accountability for spreading disinformation, I’m asking you to speak up. | | Thanks for making your voice heard, Kirsten Gillibrand * =

name: Wes Moore
subject: We’re moving different
body:
Peter, this is what we’re still committed to.
# [](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/8?t=1&akid=298%2E1086043%2EsvbXWc)
Let’s talk about the difference a few miles makes, Peter.
Just down the road, Donald Trump signed his “Big Beautiful Bill” into law. This bill is a direct attack on the American people. Because of it, people will lose their healthcare, rural hospitals will be gutted, and families won’t be able to get SNAP benefits to keep food on the table.
But here in Maryland, we’re moving different. No state will be able to absorb the impact of this bill, and our people can’t either. **But we are committed to Leave No One Behind.**
We’re going to strengthen our connection with each other and plan a path forward. It will be tough work. But I know we can come out ahead.
If you’re with me in this effort, I would humbly ask if you can **[contribute to our reelection effort today so I can stay in the fight.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250713-1?refcode=em-20250713-1&refcode2=298_1086043_svbXWc&akid=298%2E1086043%2EsvbXWc)**
**[Donate $5 ››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250713-1?amount=5&refcode=em-20250713-1&refcode2=298_1086043_svbXWc&akid=298%2E1086043%2EsvbXWc)**
I’ll keep you posted as we chart the path ahead. Thank you for your support.
Elevate,
Wes

body:
Speak out before it’s too late >>

| Team – the fight to protect the USPS isn’t over, in fact, it’s heating up.

Donald Trump is trying to privatize the USPS, putting a critical public institution at risk. **And if we don’t speak out now, those plans could gain momentum.**

The Postal Service isn’t just a delivery system, **it’s a lifeline for millions of Americans.**

The moment we treat the USPS like a business instead of a public service, we jeopardize access, equality, and trust.

**New Dems are committed to stopping privatization,** but we need to show that Americans everywhere are ready to stand up for the Postal Service.

name: Bohannan For Congress
subject: *crickets*
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CHRISTINA BOHANNAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
👀👀👀👀

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111419656/557163423/-975735282?refcode=em_logoheader&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYjk5YjM2NS0wYjYwLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=D7vNjhQUG1A_PbA9aJZxvRmiut-7byJ8bRvd_LSfT28=)
Peter,

Back in April, Mariannette Miller-Meeks committed to holding in-person town halls.

So...has anyone seen that happen? 👀

_Yeah. We haven’t, either._ Here we are in July — with no date, no event, and no sign she ever planned to follow through. This is unacceptable. **[Chip in now to help Christina reach our mid-month goal and bring real accountability back to Iowa>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111419657/557163424/-1612730695?refcode=em25_250713_MM2_fnd_gq_act___B4C_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_250713_MM2_fnd_gq_act___B4C_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=fb99b365-0b60-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYjk5YjM2NS0wYjYwLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=D7vNjhQUG1A_PbA9aJZxvRmiut-7byJ8bRvd_LSfT28=)**

We can’t say exactly why she’s ducking her constituents, but we’ve got a few theories. Maybe it’s because she’d rather spend her time cozying up to corporate donors and party elites. Or maybe she’s not eager to answer for her latest vote — to take healthcare away from over 113,000 Iowans, hike home energy costs, close rural hospitals, blow a $3 trillion hole in the deficit, and hand billionaires a giant tax break in the process.

Whatever her reasoning, it’s simple: our elected officials should answer to the people who put them in office. And when they stop doing that, _the American people vote them out._

clean_body: 👀👀👀👀 | = Peter, Back in April, Mariannette Miller-Meeks committed to holding in-person town halls. So...has anyone seen that happen? 👀 Yeah. We haven’t, either. Here we are in July — with no date, no event, and no sign she ever planned to follow through. This is unacceptable. = We can’t say exactly why she’s ducking her constituents, but we’ve got a few theories. Maybe it’s because she’d rather spend her time cozying up to corporate donors and party elites. Or maybe she’s not eager to answer for her latest vote — to take healthcare away from over 113,000 Iowans, hike home energy costs, close rural hospitals, blow a $3 trillion hole in the deficit, and hand billionaires a giant tax break in the process. Whatever her reasoning, it’s simple: our elected officials should answer to the people who put them in office. And when they stop doing that, the American people vote them out. Christina is ready to show up and actually represent Iowans. She’s running to bring Iowa voices back to the table in Washington. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: subject: Today, we remember Butler, PA, and the Millimeter Miracle.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Team Imer PAC and is not authorized by any candidate or candidates committee
body:
Today on the one year anniversary of the attempted assassination of President Trump, we remember and honor Corey Comperatore, and all of the other victims who...
| | | | | [](https://ghwh6lbbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001I3vONXruC33n5PVLNRdoZTt3xH-u87TaEY-4n0bU9qZcRCo4-LYPicsX_SSlV-n0fNaYL4EYLWUkGD48sdbrue_bs1ABZLtTCYr54PxoJul3Gm0bnNZoTIX4BHIE5lBYDdIyrq1HTsftaWDbxbYVVgX28em3RKv5sLKWC-uARoCXNreTsH7Mw0uRjpxGJrtU&c=ZZFPm-QtyjqFqfHPdWNFubdjGyoOCgAM0eTTmh3cfJyH9VjNr-IofQ==&ch=wPJ5VkYjSYSZni5mrPGGrQR_FbV4lkm4-pi91sTLM2BuIp1AwuPamQ==)
---
---
| Patriot,
One year ago today, we witnessed the millimeter miracle.
We all remember where we were and what we were doing on July 13th, 2024.
One year ago in Butler, PA, President Trump was spared from tragedy as a would-be assassin took aim at him during a campaign rally.
After the shots were fired, President Trump rose with his fist in the air and called on us all to "Fight, Fight, Fight!".
President Trump showed strength over fear in this moment, even as bullets were flying towards him, the target of this act of terror.
Now, one year later, we remain steadfast and continue to answer his call to action from that stage in Butler.
---
| In case you forgot, in the hours after the attack, democrats rejoiced and equivocated.
This attempt on President Trump's life was a resounding wake-up call for civility in American politics, but instead, democrats like Colorado State Representative Steven Woodrow said this:
---
| [](https://ghwh6lbbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001I3vONXruC33n5PVLNRdoZTt3xH-u87TaEY-4n0bU9qZcRCo4-LYPicsX_SSlV-n0fNaYL4EYLWUkGD48sdbrue_bs1ABZLtTCYr54PxoJul3Gm0bnNZoTIX4BHIE5lBYDdIyrq1HTsftaWDbxbYVVgX28em3RKv5sLKWC-uARoD0N_JO5lsY7X6rFf6kYxlt&c=ZZFPm-QtyjqFqfHPdWNFubdjGyoOCgAM0eTTmh3cfJyH9VjNr-IofQ==&ch=wPJ5VkYjSYSZni5mrPGGrQR_FbV4lkm4-pi91sTLM2BuIp1AwuPamQ==)
---
| Rep. Woodrow won re-election in 2024...
Here at Team Imer, we will Never Forget Butler, PA. We will Never Forget Corey Comperatore, and we will never forget abhorrent comments like this one.
We will fight, fight, fight until every last democrat who applauded the attempted assassination of our Commander-in-Chief is out of office, and a Republican takes their place.
Will you help us fight back against the radical left, and Representatives like Steven Woodrow, and contribute any amount today?

clean_body: Today on the one year anniversary of the attempted assassination of President Trump, we remember and honor Corey Comperatore, and all of the other victims who... | | | | | == | Patriot, One year ago today, we witnessed the millimeter miracle. We all remember where we were and what we were doing on July 13th, 2024. One year ago in Butler, PA, President Trump was spared from tragedy as a would-be assassin took aim at him during a campaign rally. After the shots were fired, President Trump rose with his fist in the air and called on us all to "Fight, Fight, Fight!". President Trump showed strength over fear in this moment, even as bullets were flying towards him, the target of this act of terror. Now, one year later, we remain steadfast and continue to answer his call to action from that stage in Butler. | In case you forgot, in the hours after the attack, democrats rejoiced and equivocated. This attempt on President Trump's life was a resounding wake-up call for civility in American politics, but instead, democrats like Colorado State Representative Steven Woodrow said this: | == | Rep. Woodrow won re-election in 2024... Here at Team Imer, we will Never Forget Butler, PA. We will Never Forget Corey Comperatore, and we will never forget abhorrent comments like this one. We will fight, fight, fight until every last democrat who applauded the attempted assassination of our Commander-in-Chief is out of office, and a Republican takes their place. Will you help us fight back against the radical left, and Representatives like Steven Woodrow, and contribute any amount today? | | == | | == | | == | | == | | == | | == | | == | | == | | == | We will take back these seats, but only with your help. We humbly ask for your support today, as we remember and honor Corey Comperatore, President Trump's millimeter miracle, and all the other victims and families affected by that would be assassin in Butler, PA one year ago. Will you step up and help us honor them too? | Our Best, | Team Imer PAC | | == | | == | == == == == | == | This email was Paid for by the Team Imer PAC and is not authorized by any candidate or candidates committee. www.TeamImer.com This email was sent by Prodigy Consulting Group, LLC, with authorization and approval by Team Imer PAC. | | | | Team Imer PAC | 16606 W 9th Ave | Golden, CO 80401 US | | | | | | |

Peter,

I’m Adam Schwarze—former Navy SEAL Officer, Marine Corps veteran, lifelong Minnesotan, and proud son of two working-class parents. I’ve deployed nine times to 70 countries to serve the country I love. I never expected to run for the U.S. Senate—but looking at what’s happening in our state and nation, I know I have to.

Minnesota is being led by two extreme voices: _**Governor Tim Walz and his lieutenant governor—my opponent—who is somehow even more radical than her boss.**_ While our cities burned in 2020, she led the charge against law enforcement while Walz hesitated. That’s not leadership. That’s chaos.

Meanwhile, crime has increased, small businesses have struggled, and our schools fail to teach the basics. The Minnesota I grew up in—where I learned to skate, hunt, and respect my neighbors—feels like it’s slipping away.

I joined the Marine Corps on my 18th birthday—October 2001— _**just weeks after 9/11.**_

_**I didn’t wait. I went.**_

And for the next 20+ years, I continued to serve. Not behind a desk or on cable news, but in the field—on missions that mattered, often in places you’ll never see on a map.

I don’t come from wealth or political pedigree. I come from hard work, long winters, and a deep desire to serve something bigger than myself. I earned degrees from Harvard, Johns Hopkins, and my MBA—not because I wanted to impress anyone, but because I wanted to serve better and lead smarter.

Now, I’m taking that same spirit to this campaign.
_**

[**HELP ME PROTECT THE AMERICA WE LOVE
CHIP IN $10 TODAY**](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=57a789575c8a4f5fbb4b215f3ad1b6d7&_e=mqv6ICAN1bKJlQt5_qt2w8FGw_Sv0-DsNYBbUmYuPIYKCh8XGtkByKVWdXM_ik-xXzqS8XR7Drd8ki6evKWfVrKN2lwFP11an9qKoURBBNwpGuJtKjS3d_zAkF2QQ7zh7HN4wQk9SIO4uyENCeT7Sj1BjdCjrZX1P3YkbVikVFhcAfY_EOEAQt8oDcdFqBc6mbPmTauEa4QrDToigS1QxnPxk-9ZWMauHr3gyP7AgTyUdApvwGj9e9SYabWNo7ckTeBEUPfZ5AJtGe9zB00YyiuBFQ7FKa5RN8Ded-S5dp7z-H1IdGlWOrJ1vQVubUiaBVLrpYAKdwH4PExinHKpcGXpF09f0l1Umpil8oFvKXeWE1CuLI4tYwzDGuOJedzEqGlhuCp2J_jhxU8FHRiEM3U4i93jtfm2wRyRibjNJCgvrPi_qT4C23OeRyRe9Dv7NKo4JGBAYnVPaBv0A_u7PWN6Yepn_1R5NcJNdQSSdi-1LX2O1_jlkC5VBQZvivTrc1eR11-LI4DexmKhyxa_siOLwAvyIccBh7gftY5Gyvc86EIAzFBKnCuYoZwHhKxeBx2b9AZ5Gn1EkxQLe_MsgJ4XvSnTDnrIngsBOMiAeCNzgfZMl7xHrnUzJF2IzJpJUU7aak2swiNT9P08vAr35hDY3wRNHKdqR3Uwy1UrEpfwcFIsT6rnflCboglbmO_jpazV0BDtjPdrL2gDV7KCTeqWDuy4QM7PRvf00EKxF-fP1_0wJGyYHB_1_1tosg4TaNTpKgnjiZHPN3xY4HAzJSMIYbgJIZ7KHDyuv2q_ICzWytaI7gfvFwgABSYQZH8m)

This won’t be easy. Minnesota hasn’t voted Republican in a presidential race since 1976, and, unfortunately, hasn’t had Republican representation in the Senate since 2009. **But I’ve always thrived in harsh environments and never backed down from a challenge worth fighting for.**

And truthfully, I’m not running because I dreamed of holding office. I’m running because every time I came home from deployment, things seemed worse—more divided, less free, and further removed from the Minnesota and America I was willing to risk my life for.

We need a new kind of leadership rooted in service, not self-interest.

**If you believe in what I’m fighting for,[even $5 goes a long way.](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=57a789575c8a4f5fbb4b215f3ad1b6d7&_e=mqv6ICAN1bKJlQt5_qt2w8FGw_Sv0-DsNYBbUmYuPIYKCh8XGtkByKVWdXM_ik-xXzqS8XR7Drd8ki6evKWfVrKN2lwFP11an9qKoURBBNwpGuJtKjS3d_zAkF2QQ7zh7HN4wQk9SIO4uyENCeT7Sj1BjdCjrZX1P3YkbVikVFhcAfY_EOEAQt8oDcdFqBc6mbPmTauEa4QrDToigS1QxnPxk-9ZWMauHr3gyP7AgTyUdApvwGj9e9SYabWNo7ckTeBEUPfZ5AJtGe9zB00YyiuBFQ7FKa5RN8Ded-S5dp5arqaJqGHIqsq3MlpI-y9LCuzVNnkUwIwN_oGD42UWyynjEhTMTJ22jE5RJnErKBEGd26MEFmfA-wV09Rh5RUsO0RQUmHpPZAhjrgXDYQ9g2pvTSKvJnr6IsTNFYTawtfDOA0pWvViy4R1aCd3JhYtWFy4dWP1cjZz4jaR9ZDNkdqBSve0HAyFcEvoXVvIJdH5H40uXEaEQzBLLbaDz3LGVZDJ4aBxzMlJXVPS9CcPvuQ1r538yocHSjeErpFjUcm7LA_D-drSBw7mXveRFzdYAYFy5XDLnEqxKCSHO2ewKFl9-aFy00BHM6EbJt9KihO3pvNIOX-aL2MOgV22xA9suZYarw6uY6fZHxabCKDTi2Bm-vTrIwpPKzEm2T-wLGLtwdXs3VcoclRoklvmMoRmi3nwbQW9lnuHIrnLse_FBWUNo0Vgq3Vv7-UOtm7t0M2ld1ucs5mZf7bTJv6zmvbe0XhwKnUVWCpIX78zCJXzN6NWms5pSkBNFkt4TbU-Da5g4daXJjz4Fj5QHy6ZCLYH)**

If you’re not ready to donate today, that’s okay. I just want to earn your trust in the weeks and months ahead. But if you believe—like I do—that Minnesota and America deserve better, please chip in now.

Let’s take this fight to the Senate. And let’s do it together.

**You’ve done your part for this country.[Let me do mine—will you donate $15 to help?](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=57a789575c8a4f5fbb4b215f3ad1b6d7&_e=mqv6ICAN1bKJlQt5_qt2w8FGw_Sv0-DsNYBbUmYuPIYKCh8XGtkByKVWdXM_ik-xXzqS8XR7Drd8ki6evKWfVrKN2lwFP11an9qKoURBBNwpGuJtKjS3d_zAkF2QQ7zh7HN4wQk9SIO4uyENCeT7Sj1BjdCjrZX1P3YkbVikVFhcAfY_EOEAQt8oDcdFqBc6mbPmTauEa4QrDToigS1QxnPxk-9ZWMauHr3gyP7AgTyUdApvwGj9e9SYabWNo7ckTeBEUPfZ5AJtGe9zB00YyiuBFQ7FKa5RN8Ded-S5dp5-_1Aik2HhKZyiX-3zIAuaO9Kn5pIMx3AAYnVYRdWLAM1zAq42a4FMt9j-pHiWt8XKS7SKSlQ3RLwu5dXQ1xRcHQU9HskGrBicQMUH7GjngYHtlLuFpYEFCQXzoY-a5c39NdhuqcJgSBnY90mbM0OikeYykNekiDKn3KGNk8ySkTDx4iOw6I9gm0I93-kbBNowb6WsHSzcPBMFayPfzo06H9W_VT0v4GB0I-bOz17dSxc3Ykjm4hITcCgdO28d0ggjAhGKMfNaI3uTi0ww4OAYIuiyGbvBcE2xjAyjHcohe_4O6sNlA7CN_HfTPBSMoz1_SrezLPSr3gIgFb5cwRYunfdftPhCvj1DiWHDPFndLEoQiydlyG6d1tJbdYVW5DbTSkqch3SJCdTLhglUEfpRzfJYWWz6jIYiLbzU608TDUdq_T0H6imyGNd5WYUdSDbP4ueKBWWeLqEyO7jRQ_n4Siab3C4Qc2NyTlC6j-wt2GgaB_kUka9tv_kNCtEUJcB21JgVkqS31tGIAK3AzGp8)**

clean_body: I’m in this race, not because I want to impress anyone in particular, but because I believe in a life of service, and mine is just getting started. | Peter, I’m Adam Schwarze—former Navy SEAL Officer, Marine Corps veteran, lifelong Minnesotan, and proud son of two working-class parents. I’ve deployed nine times to 70 countries to serve the country I love. I never expected to run for the U.S. Senate—but looking at what’s happening in our state and nation, I know I have to. Minnesota is being led by two extreme voices: Governor Tim Walz and his lieutenant governor—my opponent—who is somehow even more radical than her boss. While our cities burned in 2020, she led the charge against law enforcement while Walz hesitated. That’s not leadership. That’s chaos. Meanwhile, crime has increased, small businesses have struggled, and our schools fail to teach the basics. The Minnesota I grew up in—where I learned to skate, hunt, and respect my neighbors—feels like it’s slipping away. I joined the Marine Corps on my 18th birthday—October 2001— just weeks after 9/11. I didn’t wait. I went. And for the next 20+ years, I continued to serve. Not behind a desk or on cable news, but in the field—on missions that mattered, often in places you’ll never see on a map. I don’t come from wealth or political pedigree. I come from hard work, long winters, and a deep desire to serve something bigger than myself. I earned degrees from Harvard, Johns Hopkins, and my MBA—not because I wanted to impress anyone, but because I wanted to serve better and lead smarter. Now, I’m taking that same spirit to this campaign. Every dollar helps build a campaign ready to fight and win. This won’t be easy. Minnesota hasn’t voted Republican in a presidential race since 1976, and, unfortunately, hasn’t had Republican representation in the Senate since 2009. But I’ve always thrived in harsh environments and never backed down from a challenge worth fighting for. And truthfully, I’m not running because I dreamed of holding office. I’m running because every time I came home from deployment, things seemed worse—more divided, less free, and further removed from the Minnesota and America I was willing to risk my life for. We need a new kind of leadership rooted in service, not self-interest. If you believe in what I’m fighting for, If you’re not ready to donate today, that’s okay. I just want to earn your trust in the weeks and months ahead. But if you believe—like I do—that Minnesota and America deserve better, please chip in now. Let’s take this fight to the Senate. And let’s do it together. You’ve done your part for this country. In liberty Adam Schwarze Retired Navy SEAL Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate - Minnesota Former Navy SEAL officer Marine Corps Veteran Proud Minnesotan FOLLOW ADAM ON SOCIAL MEDIA | | Paid for by Schwarze for Senate Adam Schwarze is a former Navy SEAL and Marine. Use of his military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.

Team, On July 3rd, Republicans in the House pushed through a budget bill that includes:
* The _largest cut to Medicaid_ in American history.

* The _biggest transfer of wealth_ to the rich in our nation’s history.

* The _most devastating reduction_ to food assistance we’ve ever seen.

This isn’t about fiscal responsibility. It’s a blatant display of cruelty, greed, and a total disregard for the people these Republicans were elected to serve. Just like their Senate counterparts, House Republicans made it clear they’re willing to gut health care and slash food assistance to reward billionaires — no matter the cost to working families, children, or seniors. clean_body: | | | Ahead of November’s Virginia elections, I need your help to print HUGE 4’ x 8’ road signs, like the banners we carried at last week’s July 4th Parade. We’re gearing up for a big road sign blitz ahead of November’s election These union-printed 4’x8’ signs across VA-HD22 that will boost our campaign’s name recognition, remind voters that an election is coming, and help us WIN! My toss-up, red-to-blue race is a TOP TARGET in this November’s nationally watched Virginia House of Delegates elections, an early indicator of our strength ahead of the 2026 midterms. Democrats control the VA House of Delegates by just one seat \- every vote counts! We need to know how many we can print before we place the order. Friend, can you help us expand our Big Sign Budget? If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. ** Thanks for your support, Elizabeth | Democrat is running for Delegate in Virginia's 22nd District. She came to America as a single mother, becoming a union leader and one of the first Latinas in the Virginia General Assembly. She passed stronger gun safety laws, expanded collective bargaining rights and paid sick leave, and protected reproductive rights. To donate by check, send to: Friends of Elizabeth Guzman PO Box 74, Nokesville VA, 20182 \- \- Paid for by Friends of Elizabeth Guzman. name: Team Katie Porter
subject: Katie doesn’t take the easy way out—and she never has.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=39893%2E3422442%2E7ZeLG8)
Peter,
Katie Porter doesn’t take the easy way out. She refuses money from corporations, lobbyists, and executives at Big Pharma, Big Oil, and Big Banks because Katie’s never been for sale.
**But here’s the no BS, honest truth** —grassroots fundraising typically slows down in the summer months. We get why! Folks want to save any extra dollars for summer fun.
→ To keep on pace with our July fundraising goals, we need to raise $20,000 from emails like this one by midnight on the 15th. We can’t rely on corporations writing huge checks to make up for any dips.
**[So we’ll ask directly: Can you pitch in $5 or any amount to help us make up for the summer slowdown?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_kp_20250713_1_ak?refcode=em20250713-39893&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=39893_3422442_7ZeLG8&akid=39893%2E3422442%2E7ZeLG8)**
[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_kp_20250713_1_ak?refcode=em20250713-39893&refcode2=39893_3422442_7ZeLG8&akid=39893%2E3422442%2E7ZeLG8)
We’re so grateful for every single person who is a part of this team. Thank you!
—Team Katie Porter

Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4g-em-footer?refcode=em20250713-39893&amounts=&t=3&refcode2=39893_3422442_7ZeLG8&akid=39893%2E3422442%2E7ZeLG8)**

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/receive-fewer-kp4g-emails?source=39893&t=4&akid=39893%2E3422442%2E7ZeLG8)

* [Unsubscribe](https://act.katieporter.com/unsubscribe/kp4g-unsubscribe?source=39893&t=5&akid=39893%2E3422442%2E7ZeLG8)

Katie Porter for Governor 2026

name: Mark Warner
subject: One thing I find particularly bizarre
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
|
---

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111420474/557166901/144714547?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_mayEOM4RS_250712_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkMGQ0YjI1MC0wZjYwLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RDGHdj1MaCXeBCIe5s2of-hUQ6qKyqxcAL8XZOZRMtM=) Derek, I know it’s Sunday and you’re probably out enjoying your weekend, so I’ll try and keep this quick.

I’ve been thinking: in my time in politics, there has never been anything as challenging, bizarre, and chaotic as the second Trump administration. Trump planning a $45 million military parade in his own honor... Trump ignoring court orders... Trump refusing to fire Pete Hegseth... Trump signing into law the largest cuts to Medicaid in history to fund tax breaks for the richest Americans.

**But do you know what I’m realizing is even more bizarre? How many of my Republican colleagues in the Senate stay silent through it all. How they put Trump above the courts, the Constitution, even above their voters.**

If my Republican colleagues can’t find their voice and speak out against this dangerous chaos, they prove that taking back the Senate for Democrats is the only way we can hold Trump accountable.

[_**That’s why I’m asking if you’ll chip in to my reelection campaign. I still need 312 donors to stay on track to reach our goal for the month, and I’m hoping I can count on you.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111420475/557166902/144714547?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_mayEOM4RS_250712_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkMGQ0YjI1MC0wZjYwLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RDGHdj1MaCXeBCIe5s2of-hUQ6qKyqxcAL8XZOZRMtM=)

The fight continues,

Mark Warner

clean_body: | | | = Derek, I know it’s Sunday and you’re probably out enjoying your weekend, so I’ll try and keep this quick. I’ve been thinking: in my time in politics, there has never been anything as challenging, bizarre, and chaotic as the second Trump administration. Trump planning a $45 million military parade in his own honor... Trump ignoring court orders... Trump refusing to fire Pete Hegseth... Trump signing into law the largest cuts to Medicaid in history to fund tax breaks for the richest Americans. But do you know what I’m realizing is even more bizarre? How many of my Republican colleagues in the Senate stay silent through it all. How they put Trump above the courts, the Constitution, even above their voters. If my Republican colleagues can’t find their voice and speak out against this dangerous chaos, they prove that taking back the Senate for Democrats is the only way we can hold Trump accountable. = The fight continues, Mark Warner **= | | Friends of Mark Warner 1490-5A Quarterpath Road #213 Williamsburg, VA 23185 United States | Click here to . | Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner

subject: The math ain’t mathing
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Ohio House Democratic Campaign Committee, 545 E Town St
body:
|
---
Folks, Donald Trump has spent the last six months tearing families apart and deporting people without due process. He doesn’t care about the human suffering he’s caused because he thinks it will never affect him or his billionaire buddies. **But I refuse to let him get off that easy — so I threw his own rhetoric back in his face and brought the fight directly to his family:** **[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111420521/557167093/-31074794?refcode=250713_Jasmine_Math&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDI2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNjA5ZWJiZi0wZjYwLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-j1Ap2_4EcvF9-jgTOWImJe42uViEKrg5zq3EpGZKpY=)** Melania Trump is only in this country because of what’s known as an “Einstein visa.” To receive one, you have to be an accomplished and distinguished figure who has achieved something notable — but when Melania came here in 2001, the only experience she had under her belt was a modeling career. _Oh — and she was Donald Trump’s girlfriend._ **It’s clear Donald Trump lives under a “rules for thee but not for me” mindset._And it’s time someone made him realize that the rules apply to him as well._** That’s why I raised this issue at a recent congressional committee hearing, and that’s why I spend every waking hour fighting back against his dictatorial administration. [**If you’d like to join me in that work, I’d be grateful if you could chip in any amount right now, split with Ohio House Dems. Together, we can show Donald Trump that the will of the people is more powerful than he’ll ever be — and we’ll make sure the rules apply to him just as much as anyone else.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111420522/557167094/273633830?refcode=250713_Jasmine_Math&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDI2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNjA5ZWJiZi0wZjYwLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-j1Ap2_4EcvF9-jgTOWImJe42uViEKrg5zq3EpGZKpY=) | | [**$10 »**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111420523/557167095/273633830?amount=10&refcode=250713_Jasmine_Math&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDI2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNjA5ZWJiZi0wZjYwLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-j1Ap2_4EcvF9-jgTOWImJe42uViEKrg5zq3EpGZKpY= "https://secure.actblue.com/")

name: John Fetterman
subject: Republicans will NEVER forget voting for this budget
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fetterman for PA
body:
We’re staring down our first fundraising deadline since the GOP rammed their Big, Ugly Bill through Congress
| | |
---
[](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/51346?refcode=jtk172-fr-fl&t=1&akid=10196%2E434096%2EzEZvkE)
Hey, Derek, it’s John.
We’re staring down our first fundraising deadline since the GOP rammed their Big, Ugly Bill through Congress and Trump signed it into law. [**Will you chip in $5 now to help hit our $30,000 mid-month goal and then let me explain why this budget is such a dumpster fire?**](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/50993?refcode=jtk172-fr-fl&t=2&akid=10196%2E434096%2EzEZvkE)
This budget:
👎 **Guts Medicaid.** Nearly 300,000 Pennsylvanians – cancer patients, seniors, kids – are about to lose coverage so Trump’s rich buddies get another tax break.
💰 **Hikes your energy bills.** Families in PA could see costs jump $1,200 a year. That’s money that should go to rent or groceries – not fat-cat oil executives.
**👷 ♂️ Hamstrings U.S. manufacturing. **They rolled back tax credits for American-made jobs, yanking $50 billion in investments. That means more jobs shipped overseas. Again.
This bill was so bad that it nearly collapsed. In the Senate, JD Vance had to cast a tiebreaking vote. In the House, it passed by four lousy votes.
Republicans knew this bill would screw over the folks who sent them to Washington. They passed it anyway. They do not care.
I’m furious. I’m heartbroken. But I’m not backing down.
**Now we’ve got to fight back. And that starts with hitting our $30,000 mid-month goal by Tuesday at midnight. Will you pitch in now to make sure Republicans NEVER forget their votes to pass this bullsh*t?**
_If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [DONATE $5 NOW](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/51343?refcode=jtk172-fr-fl&t=3&akid=10196%2E434096%2EzEZvkE)

| | [DONATE ANOTHER AMOUNT](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/51346?refcode=jtk172-fr-fl&t=6&akid=10196%2E434096%2EzEZvkE)
---
Thanks, Derek,

John ❤️
| |

subject: ICE budget SOARS — Trump’s fascist agenda is being funded with YOUR tax dollars
body:
Peter, they didn’t just pass a disastrous bill — they handed Trump BILLIONS MORE for ICE to hunt down immigrants, silence dissent, and expand his authoritarian machine.
This is an all-out assault on our freedoms — and it’s fueled by your tax dollars.
We can’t wait. We need to fund Democrats fighting back in the streets, in the courts, and at the ballot box. Chip in $15 or more right now to stop Trump’s fascist takeover before it’s too late. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-fightback?refcode=071325_2]
Peter, let’s not sugarcoat it: Trump is using the U.S. military to silence dissent — and threatening to arrest governors who stand in his way.
🔻 700 Marines

🔗 Mass arrests sweep L.A.
🚫 Protesters brutalized. Silenced. Shackled.

name: Christina Gagnier
subject: I couldn’t be more different.
body:
Peter,

I wanted to make sure you saw my opponent Young Kim’s latest betrayal.

**[Before I get into the details, will you chip in $3 to help me unseat her?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111421607/557171036/1631137775?refcode=em-7.13.25-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODczMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZjMxMTA2My0xMzYwLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WETYJU2p55g0cQ5d009_AlCQWhwC63RHMbaCFGPAG_o=)**

**Young Kim has made her allegiance clear: She has put the MAGA agenda ahead of the people she was elected to represent in CA-40.**

Last week, she voted for Trump’s so-called “Big Beautiful Bill” that stands to rip health care from over 17 million people. Her vote came after promising Californians that she would not vote for anything that cut Medicaid.

This is the final straw in a long list of bad faith deals that leaves Orange County and the Inland Empire out to dry. She must lose her job in next year’s midterm elections, and our fight starts now.

**I could not be more different. When I was on my school board, I did the right thing no matter the personal cost.**

Meanwhile, Young Kim knows what she did was wrong – but she only cares about herself. We need leaders in Congress who put their communities before themselves and do not turn their backs on working families for personal gain.

I am that candidate, and I am the only one in this race who has stood up to the MAGA agenda. **[Will you stand with me and chip in $3 to unseat Young Kim?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111421611/557171039/1631137775?refcode=em-7.13.25-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODczMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZjMxMTA2My0xMzYwLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WETYJU2p55g0cQ5d009_AlCQWhwC63RHMbaCFGPAG_o=)**

Thank you,

Christina
| [Donate](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111421614/557171075/1631137775?refcode=em-7.13.25-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODczMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZjMxMTA2My0xMzYwLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WETYJU2p55g0cQ5d009_AlCQWhwC63RHMbaCFGPAG_o=)

name: Pramila Jayapal
subject: A taste of Spain, from my kitchen to yours
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
Today, I want to share a little slice of my Andalusian summer with you.
#
Peter,
As summer heats up, I’ve found myself reminiscing about a trip I took to Spain many years ago. The scorching Andalusian sun, the vibrant streets, and most importantly, the food – it all came flooding back to me.
You know what stood out the most? _The gazpacho!_
Not the lumpy kind you typically find with lots of vegetables, but a simple, smooth, pure tomato and olive oil one. It was love at first sip, and has become one of my favorite soups to make — especially when there are fresh, ripe, homegrown tomatoes I can throw in the mix!
Today, I want to share a little slice of my Andalusian summer with you, Peter.
**For the next 24 hours, for every donation made to our campaign, I'll include my beloved Andalusian Gazpacho recipe in your ActBlue receipt. It’s the perfect way to savor these last warm days before the upcoming election!**
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20250713-1?amount=3&refcode=em20250713-5233&refcode2=5233_420207_VeNzxo&akid=5233%2E420207%2EVeNzxo)**
Your support is doing double the work here — it will help us reach our campaign’s mid-month fundraising goal, and it’ll unlock a slice of culinary heaven that’s been my go-to comfort food through so many campaigns.
[**Take a breather from the political heat. Chip in $3 or whatever feels right to you today, and you’ll receive the recipe in your emailed donation receipt.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20250713-1?refcode=em20250713-5233&amount=3&refcode2=5233_420207_VeNzxo&akid=5233%2E420207%2EVeNzxo)
With love,
Pramila Jayapal

---
[](https://www.facebook.com/pramila.jayapal) | [](https://twitter.com/pramilajayapal) | [](https://www.instagram.com/pramilajayapal/)
Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, and small-dollar contributions sustain our movement for transformative change. To contribute by check, please address to Pramila for Congress, PO Box 21912, Seattle, WA 98111.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/did-your-info-change/?t=1&akid=5233%2E420207%2EVeNzxo)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=2&akid=5233%2E420207%2EVeNzxo)

name: Team WFP
subject: Good Trouble Lives On: July 17th
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Working Families Party PAC (77 Sands St
body:
Day after day, we’ve shown up with one clear message. | | | Peter, Six months into this presidency, working people, our families, and our communities have been facing some of the most brazen assaults in a generation from Trump and Republicans in Congress:
* They've torn away health care from more than 17 million people and gutted services working people rely on,
* They’ve unleashed masked ICE agents to abduct people in the streets and targeted those who dare speak out,
* And they’ve attacked our civil rights and human rights from every angle, all to keep stealing from us so they can put more money in the pockets of billionaires.

**They may have hoped we would sit back and be quiet in the face of all these attacks. They were sorely mistaken.** Week after week, month after month, millions of us have been raising our voices and showing the world that the vast majority of us aren't standing for this. In big cities and small towns and suburbs, in every corner of the country, we’ve shown up with a clear message: **In America, the power lies with the people.** On Thursday, July 17, the fifth anniversary of Congressman John Lewis’ passing, people will again be taking to the streets for a national day of peaceful, non-violent action, co-sponsored by Working Families Power. **[RSVP here to join a Good Trouble Lives On event near you — and show the entire country that we are not spectators to history, we're the makers of it:](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMfIftN0qqHwVKQtyJddGeMHx5ychWIUK92BgZWN3hkpeYgdvCDneQox30PnT8koTdlxdWAE7bx-w7eGakdQBmJnVq_VfSpXoVbgW7oDsbrzGFfdP2V1SGJsCJFNbyZooBnS5gbdG-x0060iZ2Bk1OQ87Y5N_IX_g6uA2SbYwFik-1xRaKqUy4hi2yfBiY71Mwhh2nJICIFu_9U0fv2WamN28K1byNpld85hCxiwwA8XwK3eydKTX-x7UTeUrEe8OLg/4i5/ipATjotQSp-1SAI3gEvxmg/h0/h001.A-QmGelkcGw74imvgHdEay7TKeelY-7lHw0Sm4fcIik)** [](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMfIftN0qqHwVKQtyJddGeMHx5ychWIUK92BgZWN3hkpeYgdvCDneQox30PnT8koTdsz4essUjWz7Bvi7e7VS8hU_ajLFdUQhl8FWsSCNWNn8TKIuT8xkS498uBV38wtZ005ZMMiobpW615kGrmzjIJp9vS25S75qZMVBj5jcEZLNs18MXVOXcyPYtGNZj-yqTcRZc5wd1Vvu5nyM3klincV3ZDcp-rP-DjliBXWEYCcItjyK5N0I0nuaPrTGOyq0OQ/4i5/ipATjotQSp-1SAI3gEvxmg/h1/h001._4EtPxeopQEhuBLuxX1ufGImXsawe9rkyzKSKyhYn0E) **[Find an event and RSVP »](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMfIftN0qqHwVKQtyJddGeMHx5ychWIUK92BgZWN3hkpeYgdvCDneQox30PnT8koTdoGLhoPnvdBPI4nm1Mb35I6eHz_KZsnXmSAzkvypXAjnKjjouGrSosFfxPsQpoguSfeUgj0I2KDKq306IO_2tWKjoLIRHUfbCVakKZ7JQF6_y-155s3n3h5ZOBV_riulWzQK4HTSuc61GB2hGpc67a4mt2Dtz_7tXxG4WD0jbRhuonHDgS5jl1uzU5rnFCgo-Q/4i5/ipATjotQSp-1SAI3gEvxmg/h2/h001.rYgOWdqtalmVuseUK92lYrzeQg0YG4ByJLzX0YE8xAY)** 65 years ago, President Johnson deployed the National Guard to _protect_ civil rights marchers — like the late Congressman Lewis — in Selma, Alabama. In 2025, President Trump has been deploying the National Guard to _spread fear_ throughout our communities. That's because they want to rule by fear. They want us to sit on the sidelines — or check out entirely — as they set fire to civil rights, health care, freedom of speech, and make our lives more miserable. **Instead, working people have been responding with power, people, and purpose. clean_body: Day after day, we’ve shown up with one clear message. | | | Peter, Six months into this presidency, working people, our families, and our communities have been facing some of the most brazen assaults in a generation from Trump and Republicans in Congress: They've torn away health care from more than 17 million people and gutted services working people rely on, They’ve unleashed masked ICE agents to abduct people in the streets and targeted those who dare speak out, And they’ve attacked our civil rights and human rights from every angle, all to keep stealing from us so they can put more money in the pockets of billionaires. They may have hoped we would sit back and be quiet in the face of all these attacks. They were sorely mistaken. Week after week, month after month, millions of us have been raising our voices and showing the world that the vast majority of us aren't standing for this. In big cities and small towns and suburbs, in every corner of the country, we’ve shown up with a clear message: In America, the power lies with the people. On Thursday, July 17, the fifth anniversary of Congressman John Lewis’ passing, people will again be taking to the streets for a national day of peaceful, non-violent action, co-sponsored by Working Families Power. 65 years ago, President Johnson deployed the National Guard to protect civil rights marchers — like the late Congressman Lewis — in Selma, Alabama. In 2025, President Trump has been deploying the National Guard to spread fear throughout our communities. That's because they want to rule by fear. They want us to sit on the sidelines — or check out entirely — as they set fire to civil rights, health care, freedom of speech, and make our lives more miserable. Instead, working people have been responding with power, people, and purpose. Let's keep up the Good Trouble. (To learn more about the Good Trouble Lives On National Day of Action, .) In solidarity, Team WFP Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. Reply directly to this email and let us know. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you received this email as a forward and want to get the latest updates from the Working Families Party directly, The Working Families Party is the party of the multiracial working class, fighting for a nation that cares for all of us. Wanna learn more? And if you need to update your contact information, Other political groups have corporate donors. But we can pool our resources too. ** Paid for by Working Families Party PAC (77 Sands St. #6, Brooklyn, NY, 11201). | | Sent via . name: Eric Burlison
subject: One year ago… a bullet nearly ended it all
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Burlison for Congress
body:
What I’m about to say may not be easy to read. But it must be said.

[](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/Q6AnDGO1YKTuwKuJNKmS_DNlAXh__xF2PbSUjLki7LoNK8MhWTf455lWe77Ii7rTLuPSzt6xkGUIkJlHD3eV_QFytnH-FUuqMXo6mAJSbf79w6zqRZDq4uwEaRga9qWuYKM1CqAP1T3DPoyEIjVsGx7ir1WnjzJDFWsMknCjjjap6CbfqX83rrVLFZik8_9EvDCRlqlyDMANMyB-aJsJNGPX41nimY7dL2BRcesoGVgWXWBzmvXI3Jkvqk-uGQXqUbmZH-TRvn0keuR3ZbAWnx0324C_XO5a-W7-obawYsTpjr1-auWk_0Vj86EYaE-Koh5nCxwcnSWZW4WwCG3KeBM8cOIuX7hKsVoP1REUJ3RXqPW9j3FHdDH6lXAQYpdVMonmcwl6gcM2-EUjEXUK1A9IjQWHzhLPsBKM7kxm6iTaxRuFYiYko0wbM_x6Rutn9egxZo-rW8_N8G3NduGGHgrcD-FtE9tM4JEEQBNh7G-pXQbZ1EMge1tjKXfeVI1cRAorwKAJZLlus8VCbfcF1AgVtxEfti-eSVBAeaZOO_VXze6TqNqSLjcNJy1UUXBZfyfKvZvItLmVkWYgu26-d9QTUkvMby_QK6PQmgMkMOGOHe9m8umXpLf-YmMg81sKes4uBnoPDQfUULmE3_846oUQpaCt7FljJrzFypLTwuog_IoWny_C-eFLhJ83bO3-43WOBmDKPxmV4UCC00HqKbocEPuErMZuuw2ooX9fPoiWMPG1d4VCywCYh4h0HlwTNIDZ/co_E97K_hZdp_9bo0p8RzK2qX9DGgY3a/10)

**Peter** ,

Congressman Burlison here.

I don't write messages like this often. _**But today is not an ordinary day.

**_
Exactly one year ago--President Donald J. Trump **_was nearly assassinated_** in Butler, Pennsylvania.

A mere inch made the difference between life and death.

A mere inch could have changed the course of history.

The image of President Trump--his ear bloodied, Secret Service agents swarming, supporters screaming--will **_never_** leave my mind.

[](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/ACsSKDfdpOBBFtNzt_LDJiwCU-c2iKKBYGalKtZEuapyweC2ptEzOhoDAqfaBSf3CNANKjUs_BlZvtsclNUVj_EH_2GxRZN0LRDXxU993zIyLqe6QsgqDcJprMBbBLgkT9xPgWsdqUabWnPwwBy9rMe9Kz78tUA-t7AzLaPGBa9o6_wRHSgGjIZ05QMfv0q42glw-YC7T4P3LxbSMlMViDgau6OM6irEL8DtVXFTbM4X6EFnI2s9ygOMnNP-g1DZ1_G3o04Z_54WnVcRLZ1di43jfmfNfrxLAnkLL0aSZxpX63mEfj92pikLEGQJxMelEdqWFAZfoOk4EcqMyVlShT1se8bxxaf_pvBL5VHJ5Vf8zX0eDvPzM4-Thh9_AorCCq1rS_466FwxLvD2-DfTk6qpXkBZ-FbggmAadOTUnXDSUdCZ2opSLgMpaa_wdT8_5FXpMnOuUhTgHe01fz54ykG1eZYPmWn2Jhx725lxrgaG3eR-1ifNoprL5jQvZq-nuVdHmmBQzUHM1QOR61RBMZoZXt53ISvg10CMFhs_LegvPuNHGbQCkCbkFOaDQLPOcPw4-vUHkUDFwQKFcFfi1tAvcLBf5CPOU-YVG5EZLyzdk153A5EOWGtdXhys_m5qNYlmBwFgABFYkVUIefmyaBAhqFMf1WshL7YOZXNpG6plc-8qy_GGH-BQIU7eUTY04ynXRGjhUUQQpTc72qnnF5la8RHw9UZSEBCeYyc5u5p_vRO-6_PYY9n6_OkCVTVYS7dF/wwH8-JM2Dn_FVE7hFS2ebhfkhtpjY3z5/10)
And yet...

The media moved on.

The Left refused to condemn it.

And instead of cooling the temperature--Democrats doubled down, calling Trump a _**" threat to democracy"**_ and his supporters _**" extremists."

**_
Let me be clear:
👉 I stand with President Trump.
👉 I stand with the America First movement.
👉 And I will **NEVER** let the Radical Left silence our voice with violence.

But Patriot--this anniversary is a wake-up call.

Our majority in Congress is **hanging by a thread**.

The Radical Left is **bankrolling challengers** in swing seats.

And if they flip even a handful of districts--**it 's over.

**
We won't have the votes to secure the border.

We won't be able to protect your freedoms.

And we'll LOSE the firewall protecting President Trump from impeachment--_**again**.

_
That's why I need you now more than ever.

We have to fight like every seat matters--because it does.

If you stand with Trump, with me, and with the America First mission, chip in **ANY** amount today 👇

[RUSH A DONATION TO DEFEND OUR MAJORITY](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/taQEWfkqUaxePvWam_ZM8jp7qj8akatDTxLNbZdyqjkdFV1WgQpd4BkQv9I0M2naBfLN259bjTO_DITZloXZdmWselI_Hmkvp5pB53nk_I1ZXQG45GKOIWSFB8DbTE3c3mzKKspt_336Tw42-MBTYPGd_VoTCq6Nv2ZITESkhOsPy0lnA6s_C8YreXxEfc3rKZ1HUd9nqBID4mcrNzP7AMg1eeoJ-4tNktS0RUxbiAgN-SPFitxSTQJX25A6lvIbIVfgPirUBrfk4NuMWr4vdkUvzIlHBxcOBZWvJ8694M4ieCY5o-YWN5AgTb9Gft_L6Xw69Fav0FT1wXTZF2i1NeChBvnXr5EFGm39I0xB-NGt5CL7YM81t-zG3a31uqkSsnTUAhR8wz9kdiNKEfUVWn3cZf3XHP2728go3wOlhC11H7VIyVrY4Pvqyzih8liV1cG3ZwL_QFWQEIUhTttTTjFYDxI1Tnfi2CFM488-_kY6C6_nyTRhcYB8F0oa5sv-FP8F_Rbbtn3dOlQWogNTpt2Pf2LAaoI-vcjQjTbmOdphSRdiZSr5BXz3v_k4RoI9iZJePTwYcHJcZhBtobyfF4lEmKJXklkR08va1H93j_AnoKkeKNgKhWhM7yXW0VLAZrN5pBcO11M2pPuMjCa-WAPSoMboEcjgMRCCLFzJhj_iAqbF5_iElEchgyX1FqYvcosSreANYtuIFyZqELByhfWpXWFpi9aqg2iG-TsI89pxIY8GD_-IswPZcYfMy-yl0Yq-/Tu8Bk1UgPaS_Yh9KVMtvcKepZ2Ja9QMW/10)

name: Megan Jacobs, LCV Victory Fund
subject: Your invitation to join us for a virtual climate conversation on July 15th
disclaimer_text: Paid for by GiveGreen United Action, www
body:
To view this mail in a browser, copy https://links.messages.lcv.org/servlet/MailView?ms=MTI2OTQzOTQS1&r=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&j=MjI2MDEyMjA3MQS2&mt=1&rt=0 into your browser.
Peter,

We wanted to invite you to a GiveGreen virtual fundraiser on Tuesday, July 15th from 6:30pm - 7:30pm ET https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/6Lg6BP2_-jsx/RGw4enJZSElLQjRDRFFjUVdxNzM0TnlXbkF2SGFTQmpYWkROajRkNmUzcHdxWTJMZ2EyTTdKVnVnbFNwdUtTeG0vTnp6dDJKemJEYzc5LzVZcGtqR3hpd2hrVXR5emlIR25KcE1hckVidEk9S0/ in support of Abigail Spanberger, Ghazala Hashmi, and four incumbents from the Virginia House
of Delegates.

The event will feature special guest Senator Tim Kaine, as well as Representative Abigail Spanberger and State Senator Ghazala Hashmi, the Democratic nominees for Governor and Lieutenant Governor of Virginia. The
event will also support Delegates Nadarius Clark (HD-84), Joshua Cole (HD-65), Michael Feggans (HD-97), and Josh Thomas (HD-21). We will be discussing the state of the races in Virginia, and hear about the
impact of Virginia’s elections on climate policy and our political momentum
going into 2026. Join us on Tuesday, July 15th to hear more from these outstanding climate leaders as they discuss the state of the 2025 elections and why Virginia is so crucial to building momentum for climate progress across the country. RSVP here https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/X6bzs~gwdbQw/RGw4enJZSElLQjRDRFFjUVdxNzM0TnlXbkF2SGFTQmpYWkROajRkNmUzcHdxWTJMZ2EyTTdKVnVnbFNwdUtTeG0vTnp6dDJKemJEYzc5LzVZcGtqR3hpd2hrVXR5emlIR25KcE1hckVidEk9S0/ >> https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/7RYwsXpzv-CW/RGw4enJZSElLQjRDRFFjUVdxNzM0TnlXbkF2SGFTQmpYWkROajRkNmUzcHdxWTJMZ2EyTTdKVnVnbFNwdUtTeG0vTnp6dDJKemJEYzc5LzVZcGtqR3hpd2hrVXR5emlIR25KcE1hckVidEk9S0/ RSVP HERE https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/~NwYf4VWaAs7/RGw4enJZSElLQjRDRFFjUVdxNzM0TnlXbkF2SGFTQmpYWkROajRkNmUzcHdxWTJMZ2EyTTdKVnVnbFNwdUtTeG0vTnp6dDJKemJEYzc5LzVZcGtqR3hpd2hrVXR5emlIR25KcE1hckVidEk9S0/ Virginia is one of the biggest battleground states this year – and all six
champions will need the support of climate donors like you to win this November.
This event is a great opportunity to connect with other donors in the movement
and to hear more from some incredible speakers. Reserve your spot today by contributing directly through the event page and supporting the campaigns of our six Virginia climate champions https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/RZbkcR8p4zf4/RGw4enJZSElLQjRDRFFjUVdxNzM0TnlXbkF2SGFTQmpYWkROajRkNmUzcHdxWTJMZ2EyTTdKVnVnbFNwdUtTeG0vTnp6dDJKemJEYzc5LzVZcGtqR3hpd2hrVXR5emlIR25KcE1hckVidEk9S0/ >> https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/bxyvTVjYpZcV/RGw4enJZSElLQjRDRFFjUVdxNzM0TnlXbkF2SGFTQmpYWkROajRkNmUzcHdxWTJMZ2EyTTdKVnVnbFNwdUtTeG0vTnp6dDJKemJEYzc5LzVZcGtqR3hpd2hrVXR5emlIR25KcE1hckVidEk9S0/ Hope you can make it, Megan Jacobs
Vice President of Paid Communications & Elections
LCV Victory Fund

## MESSAGE FROM TRUMP:

Peter,

Still, the Big Bad Bill will hurt Rhode Island families, cutting health care, food benefits, and more. **And we need to win back control of Congress to reverse it.**

[The fight for a House majority in the midterms has already begun. ### The movie's parallels with today's human rights conditions are undeniable, and the lessons it presents are memorable
| | [Qasim Rashid, Esq.](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---
| Jul 13
---
| [](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)

[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=168171423&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MTcxNDIzLCJpYXQiOjE3NTI0MzUyMjQsImV4cCI6MTc1NTAyNzIyNCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.67e2mfenBp9wZa26uH3lt8h7KQOq12Vv24aB99MuKlU)
This weekend I watched the new Superman movie, direct by James Gunn, and it is a strong contender for the best Superman movie yet. The Superman franchise is not paying me to write this review, nor do I have any contact with anyone associated with the film. I'm writing this because I feel compelled to share a powerful work of art that speaks to the human rights issues I care so deeply about. Walking into the theatre I'd seen murmurings from MAGA pundits that the movie was "woke" (used as a pejorative of course) and even "anti-Israel." Not only are these claims unfounded, in reality, the film demonstrates the power of storytelling, the need for justice, and makes us wonder--who are the real superheroes in our world today?
**Let 's Address This.**
**Let's Address This with Qasim Rashid is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.**
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack..dyUYDtFRdMqmAqHTW1Quu1zHCY8zB9zgybwO81xEllA?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=168171423)
**NOTE: Spoilers below. If you have not yet seen Superman, you can stop here and avoid spoilers. Otherwise, please proceed!**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/6aa2a09c-3b73-4d75-9c8f-81464e9411e9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
## Is Superman 'Woke?'
The above 1950s era comic answers this question. Not only is being _Woke_ a good thing in terms of respecting human dignity and diversity, it is who Superman always was. This was Superman in Jim Crow America--an immigrant alien orphan raised by a childless couple and taught to accept all religions, races, and nationalities. Superman was the de facto anti-establishment anti-corruption anti-nationalist superhero. Those offended by that notion of inclusivity either never understood why Superman was created, or want to transform him into a right wing zealot to appease their own egos. Either way, with this bizarre alleged criticism of 'woke,' they reveal much about themselves. Now let's dive into the film.
## A Timely Commentary on Earth 2025
I assure you Let's Address This is not transforming into a movie review platform. Yet I feel compelled to write about Superman because its parallels with politics and human rights today cannot be ignored. Movies have often served as water carriers to glorify war and nationalism, e.g. American Sniper, Top Gun 1 and 2, and Air Force One, among many others. Superman does the opposite, and appeals to our inner and innate humanity to care for one another, to place public good above personal greed, and to recognize the need of heroes to speak up and step up for marginalized communities today. With that, let's dive into the plot.
## The Plot
Superman focuses on a conflict between two fictional neighboring nations, Boravia and Jarhanpur. Boravia is an American ally for decades, a nation who has received $80 billion in military aid from the United States through defense contractor Lex Luthor. A nation with a history of grotesque human rights violations, ruled by the imperialist President Ghurkos (more on him later), yet gets away with it because it is, after all, an American ally. And a nation who wants to conquer Jarhanpur to 'free it from its tyranically dictator,'--an excuse we soon learn is largely western propaganda. Jarhanpur, by comparison, is a poorer and underdeveloped nation with no real army. It's residents prepare to fight Boravia's massive military with sticks, stones, and pitchforks. And as they face destruction, Superman intervenes and forces back the Boravian military.
We quickly realize Boravia and Jarhanpur are metaphors for both Israel and Palestine as well as Russia and Ukraine. This is also why I find it fascinating that some claim the movie is "anti-Israel." In fact, the movie is anti-imperialism. And given the noted distinction that the Jarhanpurians are brown skinned and not physically dissimilar to Palestinians, the movie's resemblance to Israel's aggression against Palestine largely also applies to Russia's aggression against Ukraine. However, I've yet to hear anyone from the right claim the movie is "anti-Russia." Why? Because they recognize Russian aggression against Ukraine as unjust and illegal, yet refuse to apply the same rules to Israel's occupation of Palestinian lands and current invasion and occupation of Gaza. As the film progresses, the forces of evil seek to advance Boravia's war of aggression against Jarhanpur, all with US backing and support. Because, after all, horrific human rights violations notwithstanding, the United States stands by it's ally ~~Israel~~ Boravia.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..dyUYDtFRdMqmAqHTW1Quu1zHCY8zB9zgybwO81xEllA?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=168171423)
## The Players
Each of the main characters play a role that well corresponds with contemporary society. It isn't subtle, either. And that's what makes the film such an impactful experience. It doesn't offer whitewashed aspirations of right and wrong, but speaks truth to power unequivocally.
**Superman:** Played by David Corenswet, the film makes no secret of the fact that Clark Kent is an undocumented illegal alien orphan, raised by an anonymous childless couple. Despite facing torture to reveal the identity of his adopted parents, Superman resists, knowing full well Luthor will kill them if he finds out. In fact, the one martyr is an apparently Muslim food truck owner named Malik Mali Ali, who had earlier attempted to help Superman. This act of solidarity enraged Lex Luthor, who kidnaps Ali to use him as leverage against Superman. Luthor tortures Superman to reveal his parents' identity, and when he does not, Luthor executes Ali with a bullet to his head. Right now, anti-Muslim hate crimes are at record highs--most of which corporate media ignores. Right now, by some estimates the genocide in Gaza has killed more than 300,000 Palestinians--all of whom the United States and the Israeli government deems expendable. Thus, the symbolism of a portraying a Muslim American martyr as an expendable casualty did not go unnoticed to this Muslim American writer.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/26b5fc8d-6e17-421a-865e-29235aedbeda?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
As another stark similarity, a Department of Defense official makes clear to Superman that because he has no documented status, he therefore has no rights and due process of law in the United States. While in reality the United States Constitution affords due process to all people, documented or not, the film's commentary speaks to the fact that this due process is being unjustly denied. And thus, to further emphasize the point, just as the US Government is outsourcing immigrants to private contractors to lock into torture camps and gulags on foreign soil, in the movie the US Government outsources Superman's detention and torture to billionaire sociopath Lex Luthor, who traps Superman in a tiny torture chamber in a pocket universe and in a different dimension.
**Lex Luthor:** Played by Nicholas Hoult, the film leans in to the modern tech bro billionaire brand of a man who is allegedly a genius, hopelessly corrupt, and openly envious of everything he doesn't have. Luthor is the lead villain, with several villains under his direction. He is a man who demands total control and compliance, has every tech gizmo imaginable at his disposal, virtually unlimited wealth (yet somehow wants more), and commands a team of insecure minions who hang on his every word (DOGE employees, anyone?). There's even a moment where we see Luthor's army of online social media influencers--literally monkeys locked in cages trained to sh*t post rage bait against the film's hero, Superman. An astute commentary on how mindless bots drive online outrage.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/7221119c-ee78-4f6c-b2dd-d03de95a21f6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Luthor is a combination of Elon Musk and his reliance on government contracts and shady dealings with foreign governments, Peter Thiel and his eugenicist style obsession with using technology to 'perfect' humanity and make himself immortal, and Donald Trump and his obsession with power and wanting to be a king. It is revealed that Luthor is helping Boravian President Ghurkos invade Jahanpur in exchange for sovereign kingship over half of Jahanpur--again--a not so subtle jab to Jared Kushner boasting about "beachfront property" in Gaza or Trump's AI generated propaganda video of a Trump Gaza hotel.
**Lois Lane:** Played by Rachel Brosnahan, Lois Lane's story arc represents the hope of what journalism is supposed to be. She begins by grilling Superman for his uninvited intervention that stopped Boravia from invading Jahanpur, focused on whether he had the proper approvals to stop a war, rather than on the principle that Superman saved the lives of innocent people who would have otherwise been murdered. She admits that her and Superman are opposites, because she inherently questions everything and everyone, and Superman inherently trusts everyone and sees the good in them. It is only when she sees the injustices imposed on Superman that she decides to take meaningful action to stop billionaires and the US Government from colluding to deny basic humanity. Lane partners with fellow journalists to uncover Luthor's conspiracy with Boravian President Ghurkos, and turns the public opinion back in favor of Superman. She likewise partners with Mr. Terrific to risk her life to save Superman.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4c5c080a-ef51-4731-bbbc-9ca1565a71d0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The overall media ethos in the movie is that of demonstrating how, just like in real life, billionaire influenced and billionaire run media is devastating our society with misinformation, disinformation, and flat out propaganda. In that backdrop, Lane ultimately emerges as a principled journalist--not without her flaws--but with a bent towards social justice for working people.
**Bovarian President Vasil Ghurkos:** Played by Zlatko Burić, President of Boravia, Vasil Ghurkos appears as a brilliant fusion of Vladamir Putin and Benjamin Netanyahu. A man who is deeply corrupt while claiming to stand for democracy and rule of law, bent on imperialism while claiming to oppose tyranny and war, and full of bravado while sending a powerful military to massacre children from the comfort of his bunker.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/39b77bdf-96e5-4755-82c8-15fcad9faa16?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
And as he sends young people to massacre innocent civilians, he hides behind a camera and makes secret deals with billionaires. Ghurkos effortlessly weaves together everything detestable about politicians into one soulless person, and reminds viewers why Putin and Netanyahu are both wanted by the ICC for war crimes.
**The Justice Gang:** Featuring Mr. Terrific, the Green Lantern, and Hawk Girl, the Justice Gang's story arc directly rebukes the cowardice of bystanders. Early in the film they refuse to help save Superman for fear of being viewed as "political." Mr. Terrific, notably a Black superhero, is the first to break from this apathy and decides to help Lois Lane find and save Superman from his prison in the pocket universe.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3ada099d-02e7-4254-b38e-e6cdaf21df60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Towards the end of the film, the rest of the Justice Gang shows up to protect the Johanpurians from the impending annihilation they were about to face from the powerful U.S. backed Boravian military. The lesson is clear--when it comes to basic human rights and dignity, remaining silent is choosing to be complicit in genocide. That it is incumbent upon all of us to step up and support the marginalized, as that is how ultimately protect our humanity.
**Political Detainees:** The film depicted hundreds, if not thousands, of innocent people detained in cages inside Luthor's pocket universe, all for the 'crime' of criticizing Lex Luthor, posting unflattering commentary about him, or for opposing war and genocide. This was clearly a direct jab at the United States government denying due process of law and arbitrarily and cruelly detaining in cages innocent people like Kilmar Abrego Garcia, Mahmoud Khalil, Rumeyza Ozturk, and more. Indeed, experts estimate that of the 204,000 people that ICE has detained without due process of law, 93% have no criminal record or record of violence whatsoever.
The meta human who tortures Superman with kryptonite does so because Luthor holds his son hostage. It is only after Luthor executes Ali, that he realizes that Ali's life was no less valuable than his son's life, and chooses to help Superman. To me, this was a message that none of us are free until all of us are free, and we cannot trade lives for human dignity. This is also a call out to a beautiful teaching both in Judaism and Islam, where we are taught that to kill one life is to kill all humanity, and to save one life is to save all humanity.
**Additional Character Similarities:** Superman fights an army of fascists called The Raptors, who hide their faces and conceal their identities--a knock on anonymous ICE agents. A newspaper headline briefly asks if a billionaire is funding the next Metropolis mayoral candidate--perhaps a knock on what we're seeing in NYC with billionaires getting behind Cuomo and Adams to oppose Mamdani. But certainly a knock on unlimited money in politics corrupting our Republic. Superman's Earth parents are a childless couple that he has to keep anonymous, because their safety is in danger for harboring an 'illegal alien immigrant,'--a knock on the Trump regime raiding homes, schools, and hospitals to intimidate anyone who would give immigrants sanctuary. We see corporate media platform Lex Luthor to demonize Superman, without any fact checking or verifying of Luthor's claims. Perhaps the one unrealistic part was their retraction of supporting Luthor towards the end of the film, because I do not recall corporate media like the New York Times or Washington Post ever actually admitting they were wrong to platform and enable Trump and genocide.
**If you 're finding value in this review, join our community as** **a free or paid subscriber.**
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack..dyUYDtFRdMqmAqHTW1Quu1zHCY8zB9zgybwO81xEllA?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=168171423)
## The Movie Heroes vs Real Life Heroes
Finally, as I walked out of the film I began to ask myself--who are the real life superheroes of today? None of us can fly, or shoot laser beams out of our eyes, or exert super human strength. But the film answers this question too. In the penultimate moment of the film Superman implores to Lex Luthor that his strength is not because he is physically strong or because he can fly--but because he is ultimately as human as anyone on Earth, and persists on with courage and heart despite his many mistakes and flaws. His strength, Superman insists, is not in his muscle, but in his courage to continue putting one foot in front of the other and fight for what is right against all odds. Thus, we see clearly who are today's superheroes.
It's leader like Ms. Rachel, who speak up for Palestinian babies suffering genocide, despite vicious attacks against her character and safety. Film director Ava DuVernay, who continues to use her massive platform to call out the injustices in American foreign policy in Palestine. It's Senator Chris Van Hollen who uses his position of power and privilege as an elected official to oppose war, oppose concentration camps, and oppose denial of due process--even traveling to El Salvador to meet with Kilmar Abrego Garcia. It's US Veterans standing with Afghan refugees at their immigration hearings, to protect them from ICE fascists. It's Barbara Stone, a 71-year-old grandmother arrested by ICE for protecting immigrants at a San Diego immigration center. It's Mahmoud Khalil, Rumeysa Ozturk, and Mohsen Mahdawi for fearlessly speaking up for justice despite government tyranny targeting them to destroy their lives.
It's the people of Palestine and the people of Ukraine, standing up to tyrannical invasion and destruction from an imperialist corrupt politician who cares nought for human rights and international law. It is every person who refuses to sit silent as we watch an ongoing genocide broadcast in real time, on our phones, laptops, ipads, and television screens. It is the working class teacher leaning into the importance of diversity, despite Federal crackdowns. It is the business owner declaring that their business is open to all people, despite right wing targeting of inclusion. It is the person turning off corporate media funded by billionaires, and investing in independent scholarly voices and non-profit media to invest their dollars in a future committed to factual accuracy and human rights. It is every person for whom human rights are not a weather pattern to bear in some conditions but not others--but the ever shining brightness of Earth's yellow sun, one that gives power to Superman, yes, but also to all life on Earth to continue to put one step ahead of the other in the ongoing and unrelenting march towards justice.
## **Conclusion**
The brilliance of this Superman film isn't just in its visual spectacle or storytelling--it's in its insistence that heroism isn't defined by powers, but by principle. It reminds us that the true battle isn't between gods and monsters, but between courage and complicity. Between those who abuse power, and those who dare to challenge it.
By weaving real-world atrocities into a fictional frame, this film doesn't just entertain--it indicts. It indicts the billionaires who profit off war, the governments that abandon human rights for convenience, the corporate media that launders propaganda as journalism, and the bystanders who mistake neutrality for virtue. It dares us to see through the smokescreen--and act.
Superman may be fiction, and it certainly uses comedy as levity throughout the film, but the injustice he fights is all too real. And if he, an undocumented alien from another planet, can choose to stand up for truth and justice in a world stacked against him--then what excuse do we, who claim this Earth as our home, have to remain silent?
The real superheroes aren't caped crusaders flying through the sky. They are the teachers, parents, protestors, journalists, and everyday people who wake up each day and choose to fight for someone other than themselves. Who risk ridicule, arrest, or worse--not for glory, but because their conscience demands it.
This film holds up a mirror to the systems that oppress, and to each of us. What will we do with the time we've been given? Will we ignore suffering and align ourselves with villains, or will we become the very heroes we've been waiting for?
