The movie's parallels with today's human rights conditions are undeniable, and the lessons it presents are memorable | | | Jul 13 | | | | | | | | | | | | This weekend I watched the new Superman movie, direct by James Gunn, and it is a strong contender for the best Superman movie yet. The Superman franchise is not paying me to write this review, nor do I have any contact with anyone associated with the film. I'm writing this because I feel compelled to share a powerful work of art that speaks to the human rights issues I care so deeply about. Walking into the theatre I'd seen murmurings from MAGA pundits that the movie was "woke" (used as a pejorative of course) and even "anti-Israel." Not only are these claims unfounded, in reality, the film demonstrates the power of storytelling, the need for justice, and makes us wonder--who are the real superheroes in our world today? Let 's Address This. Let's Address This with Qasim Rashid is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. NOTE: Spoilers below. If you have not yet seen Superman, you can stop here and avoid spoilers. Otherwise, please proceed! | | ---|---|--- Is Superman 'Woke?' The above 1950s era comic answers this question. Not only is being Woke a good thing in terms of respecting human dignity and diversity, it is who Superman always was. This was Superman in Jim Crow America--an immigrant alien orphan raised by a childless couple and taught to accept all religions, races, and nationalities. Superman was the de facto anti-establishment anti-corruption anti-nationalist superhero. Those offended by that notion of inclusivity either never understood why Superman was created, or want to transform him into a right wing zealot to appease their own egos. Either way, with this bizarre alleged criticism of 'woke,' they reveal much about themselves. Now let's dive into the film. A Timely Commentary on Earth 2025 I assure you Let's Address This is not transforming into a movie review platform. Yet I feel compelled to write about Superman because its parallels with politics and human rights today cannot be ignored. Movies have often served as water carriers to glorify war and nationalism, e.g. American Sniper, Top Gun 1 and 2, and Air Force One, among many others. Superman does the opposite, and appeals to our inner and innate humanity to care for one another, to place public good above personal greed, and to recognize the need of heroes to speak up and step up for marginalized communities today. With that, let's dive into the plot. The Plot Superman focuses on a conflict between two fictional neighboring nations, Boravia and Jarhanpur. Boravia is an American ally for decades, a nation who has received $80 billion in military aid from the United States through defense contractor Lex Luthor. A nation with a history of grotesque human rights violations, ruled by the imperialist President Ghurkos (more on him later), yet gets away with it because it is, after all, an American ally. And a nation who wants to conquer Jarhanpur to 'free it from its tyranically dictator,'--an excuse we soon learn is largely western propaganda. Jarhanpur, by comparison, is a poorer and underdeveloped nation with no real army. It's residents prepare to fight Boravia's massive military with sticks, stones, and pitchforks. And as they face destruction, Superman intervenes and forces back the Boravian military. We quickly realize Boravia and Jarhanpur are metaphors for both Israel and Palestine as well as Russia and Ukraine. This is also why I find it fascinating that some claim the movie is "anti-Israel." In fact, the movie is anti-imperialism. And given the noted distinction that the Jarhanpurians are brown skinned and not physically dissimilar to Palestinians, the movie's resemblance to Israel's aggression against Palestine largely also applies to Russia's aggression against Ukraine. However, I've yet to hear anyone from the right claim the movie is "anti-Russia." Why? Because they recognize Russian aggression against Ukraine as unjust and illegal, yet refuse to apply the same rules to Israel's occupation of Palestinian lands and current invasion and occupation of Gaza. As the film progresses, the forces of evil seek to advance Boravia's war of aggression against Jarhanpur, all with US backing and support. Because, after all, horrific human rights violations notwithstanding, the United States stands by it's ally ~~Israel~~ Boravia. The Players Each of the main characters play a role that well corresponds with contemporary society. It isn't subtle, either. And that's what makes the film such an impactful experience. It doesn't offer whitewashed aspirations of right and wrong, but speaks truth to power unequivocally. Superman: Played by David Corenswet, the film makes no secret of the fact that Clark Kent is an undocumented illegal alien orphan, raised by an anonymous childless couple. Despite facing torture to reveal the identity of his adopted parents, Superman resists, knowing full well Luthor will kill them if he finds out. In fact, the one martyr is an apparently Muslim food truck owner named Malik Mali Ali, who had earlier attempted to help Superman. This act of solidarity enraged Lex Luthor, who kidnaps Ali to use him as leverage against Superman. Luthor tortures Superman to reveal his parents' identity, and when he does not, Luthor executes Ali with a bullet to his head. Right now, anti-Muslim hate crimes are at record highs--most of which corporate media ignores. Right now, by some estimates the genocide in Gaza has killed more than 300,000 Palestinians--all of whom the United States and the Israeli government deems expendable. Thus, the symbolism of a portraying a Muslim American martyr as an expendable casualty did not go unnoticed to this Muslim American writer. | | ---|---|--- As another stark similarity, a Department of Defense official makes clear to Superman that because he has no documented status, he therefore has no rights and due process of law in the United States. While in reality the United States Constitution affords due process to all people, documented or not, the film's commentary speaks to the fact that this due process is being unjustly denied. And thus, to further emphasize the point, just as the US Government is outsourcing immigrants to private contractors to lock into torture camps and gulags on foreign soil, in the movie the US Government outsources Superman's detention and torture to billionaire sociopath Lex Luthor, who traps Superman in a tiny torture chamber in a pocket universe and in a different dimension. Lex Luthor: Played by Nicholas Hoult, the film leans in to the modern tech bro billionaire brand of a man who is allegedly a genius, hopelessly corrupt, and openly envious of everything he doesn't have. Luthor is the lead villain, with several villains under his direction. He is a man who demands total control and compliance, has every tech gizmo imaginable at his disposal, virtually unlimited wealth (yet somehow wants more), and commands a team of insecure minions who hang on his every word (DOGE employees, anyone?). There's even a moment where we see Luthor's army of online social media influencers--literally monkeys locked in cages trained to sht post rage bait against the film's hero, Superman. An astute commentary on how mindless bots drive online outrage. | | ---|---|--- Luthor is a combination of Elon Musk and his reliance on government contracts and shady dealings with foreign governments, Peter Thiel and his eugenicist style obsession with using technology to 'perfect' humanity and make himself immortal, and Donald Trump and his obsession with power and wanting to be a king. It is revealed that Luthor is helping Boravian President Ghurkos invade Jahanpur in exchange for sovereign kingship over half of Jahanpur--again--a not so subtle jab to Jared Kushner boasting about "beachfront property" in Gaza or Trump's AI generated propaganda video of a Trump Gaza hotel. Lois Lane: Played by Rachel Brosnahan, Lois Lane's story arc represents the hope of what journalism is supposed to be. She begins by grilling Superman for his uninvited intervention that stopped Boravia from invading Jahanpur, focused on whether he had the proper approvals to stop a war, rather than on the principle that Superman saved the lives of innocent people who would have otherwise been murdered. She admits that her and Superman are opposites, because she inherently questions everything and everyone, and Superman inherently trusts everyone and sees the good in them. It is only when she sees the injustices imposed on Superman that she decides to take meaningful action to stop billionaires and the US Government from colluding to deny basic humanity. Lane partners with fellow journalists to uncover Luthor's conspiracy with Boravian President Ghurkos, and turns the public opinion back in favor of Superman. She likewise partners with Mr. Terrific to risk her life to save Superman. | | ---|---|--- The overall media ethos in the movie is that of demonstrating how, just like in real life, billionaire influenced and billionaire run media is devastating our society with misinformation, disinformation, and flat out propaganda. In that backdrop, Lane ultimately emerges as a principled journalist--not without her flaws--but with a bent towards social justice for working people. Bovarian President Vasil Ghurkos: Played by Zlatko Burić, President of Boravia, Vasil Ghurkos appears as a brilliant fusion of Vladamir Putin and Benjamin Netanyahu. A man who is deeply corrupt while claiming to stand for democracy and rule of law, bent on imperialism while claiming to oppose tyranny and war, and full of bravado while sending a powerful military to massacre children from the comfort of his bunker. | | ---|---|--- And as he sends young people to massacre innocent civilians, he hides behind a camera and makes secret deals with billionaires. Ghurkos effortlessly weaves together everything detestable about politicians into one soulless person, and reminds viewers why Putin and Netanyahu are both wanted by the ICC for war crimes. The Justice Gang: Featuring Mr. Terrific, the Green Lantern, and Hawk Girl, the Justice Gang's story arc directly rebukes the cowardice of bystanders. Early in the film they refuse to help save Superman for fear of being viewed as "political." Mr. Terrific, notably a Black superhero, is the first to break from this apathy and decides to help Lois Lane find and save Superman from his prison in the pocket universe. | | ---|---|--- Towards the end of the film, the rest of the Justice Gang shows up to protect the Johanpurians from the impending annihilation they were about to face from the powerful U.S. backed Boravian military. The lesson is clear--when it comes to basic human rights and dignity, remaining silent is choosing to be complicit in genocide. That it is incumbent upon all of us to step up and support the marginalized, as that is how ultimately protect our humanity. Political Detainees: The film depicted hundreds, if not thousands, of innocent people detained in cages inside Luthor's pocket universe, all for the 'crime' of criticizing Lex Luthor, posting unflattering commentary about him, or for opposing war and genocide. This was clearly a direct jab at the United States government denying due process of law and arbitrarily and cruelly detaining in cages innocent people like Kilmar Abrego Garcia, Mahmoud Khalil, Rumeyza Ozturk, and more. Indeed, experts estimate that of the 204,000 people that ICE has detained without due process of law, 93% have no criminal record or record of violence whatsoever. The meta human who tortures Superman with kryptonite does so because Luthor holds his son hostage. It is only after Luthor executes Ali, that he realizes that Ali's life was no less valuable than his son's life, and chooses to help Superman. To me, this was a message that none of us are free until all of us are free, and we cannot trade lives for human dignity. This is also a call out to a beautiful teaching both in Judaism and Islam, where we are taught that to kill one life is to kill all humanity, and to save one life is to save all humanity. Additional Character Similarities: Superman fights an army of fascists called The Raptors, who hide their faces and conceal their identities--a knock on anonymous ICE agents. A newspaper headline briefly asks if a billionaire is funding the next Metropolis mayoral candidate--perhaps a knock on what we're seeing in NYC with billionaires getting behind Cuomo and Adams to oppose Mamdani. But certainly a knock on unlimited money in politics corrupting our Republic. Superman's Earth parents are a childless couple that he has to keep anonymous, because their safety is in danger for harboring an 'illegal alien immigrant,'--a knock on the Trump regime raiding homes, schools, and hospitals to intimidate anyone who would give immigrants sanctuary. We see corporate media platform Lex Luthor to demonize Superman, without any fact checking or verifying of Luthor's claims. Perhaps the one unrealistic part was their retraction of supporting Luthor towards the end of the film, because I do not recall corporate media like the New York Times or Washington Post ever actually admitting they were wrong to platform and enable Trump and genocide. If you 're finding value in this review, join our community as a free or paid subscriber. The Movie Heroes vs Real Life Heroes Finally, as I walked out of the film I began to ask myself--who are the real life superheroes of today? None of us can fly, or shoot laser beams out of our eyes, or exert super human strength. But the film answers this question too. In the penultimate moment of the film Superman implores to Lex Luthor that his strength is not because he is physically strong or because he can fly--but because he is ultimately as human as anyone on Earth, and persists on with courage and heart despite his many mistakes and flaws. His strength, Superman insists, is not in his muscle, but in his courage to continue putting one foot in front of the other and fight for what is right against all odds. Thus, we see clearly who are today's superheroes. It's leader like Ms. Rachel, who speak up for Palestinian babies suffering genocide, despite vicious attacks against her character and safety. Film director Ava DuVernay, who continues to use her massive platform to call out the injustices in American foreign policy in Palestine. It's Senator Chris Van Hollen who uses his position of power and privilege as an elected official to oppose war, oppose concentration camps, and oppose denial of due process--even traveling to El Salvador to meet with Kilmar Abrego Garcia. It's US Veterans standing with Afghan refugees at their immigration hearings, to protect them from ICE fascists. It's Barbara Stone, a 71-year-old grandmother arrested by ICE for protecting immigrants at a San Diego immigration center. It's Mahmoud Khalil, Rumeysa Ozturk, and Mohsen Mahdawi for fearlessly speaking up for justice despite government tyranny targeting them to destroy their lives. It's the people of Palestine and the people of Ukraine, standing up to tyrannical invasion and destruction from an imperialist corrupt politician who cares nought for human rights and international law. It is every person who refuses to sit silent as we watch an ongoing genocide broadcast in real time, on our phones, laptops, ipads, and television screens. It is the working class teacher leaning into the importance of diversity, despite Federal crackdowns. It is the business owner declaring that their business is open to all people, despite right wing targeting of inclusion. It is the person turning off corporate media funded by billionaires, and investing in independent scholarly voices and non-profit media to invest their dollars in a future committed to factual accuracy and human rights. It is every person for whom human rights are not a weather pattern to bear in some conditions but not others--but the ever shining brightness of Earth's yellow sun, one that gives power to Superman, yes, but also to all life on Earth to continue to put one step ahead of the other in the ongoing and unrelenting march towards justice. Conclusion The brilliance of this Superman film isn't just in its visual spectacle or storytelling--it's in its insistence that heroism isn't defined by powers, but by principle. It reminds us that the true battle isn't between gods and monsters, but between courage and complicity. Between those who abuse power, and those who dare to challenge it. By weaving real-world atrocities into a fictional frame, this film doesn't just entertain--it indicts. It indicts the billionaires who profit off war, the governments that abandon human rights for convenience, the corporate media that launders propaganda as journalism, and the bystanders who mistake neutrality for virtue. It dares us to see through the smokescreen--and act. Superman may be fiction, and it certainly uses comedy as levity throughout the film, but the injustice he fights is all too real. And if he, an undocumented alien from another planet, can choose to stand up for truth and justice in a world stacked against him--then what excuse do we, who claim this Earth as our home, have to remain silent? The real superheroes aren't caped crusaders flying through the sky. They are the teachers, parents, protestors, journalists, and everyday people who wake up each day and choose to fight for someone other than themselves. Who risk ridicule, arrest, or worse--not for glory, but because their conscience demands it. This film holds up a mirror to the systems that oppress, and to each of us. What will we do with the time we've been given? Will we ignore suffering and align ourselves with villains, or will we become the very heroes we've been waiting for? clean_body: Trump needs his state-level allies. The most direct way to fight back is by defeating them at the ballot box and electing state Democrats who won’t bow to his tirade of authoritarianism. | | | | | ---|--- Several states are considering creating their own 'Alligator Alcatraz' facilities. And the Trump administration is urging state GOP leaders to follow suit. 'Alligator Alcatraz' is just the beginning of a horrific GOP plan. Officials in Georgia and Arkansas revealed that they already have their own version of these inhumane detention facilities in the works. This situation makes one thing clear: Trump’s regime is looking to the states to do its bidding and is depending on MAGA Republicans up and down the ballot to rubber-stamp their cruelty. We can’t sit back while these atrocities are being carried out. Knowing that Trump needs the support of his state-level allies, the most direct way we can fight back is by defeating them at the ballot box and electing state Democrats who won’t bow to his tirade of authoritarianism. Democratic power is the strongest tool we have to combat GOP extremism. subject: Republicans abandoned working families
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Wiley Nickel for North Carolina**
body:
We can’t let MAGA Republicans determine our path. |
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111425367/557183981/-1960556440?refcode=AU_GWN_FR_GEN_MMG_AC_20250713_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODMxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNTdmZWU0YS0yNDYwLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3inHIHLbGoMe_zfg4fBI22ad1qh4_NsT9WISa9jektg=)
---
Peter, working families in North Carolina deserve leaders who prioritize justice and ethical governance, not personal interests. Due to congressional Republican support of the Big Ugly Bill — _including every single NC U.S. House Republican_ — over 600,000 of our friends and neighbors in North Carolina stand to lose their healthcare. Folks in nursing homes will be kicked out and rural hospitals will be shut down across the state — all to fund tax breaks for billionaires. **We have to hold them accountable, and I’m running for U.S. Senate to do just that.** Throughout my career, I’ve been committed to fighting corruption and advocating for policies that empower North Carolinians. From safeguarding voting rights to ensuring private healthcare decisions remain between patients and providers, I’ve consistently stood up for transparency and justice. To take this open seat and flip the Senate blue, we need a grassroots movement that’s ready to demand ethical governance and justice for all. **[Will you support that movement by chipping in $5 before our mid-month fundraising deadline? Your support and donations are crucial to flipping North Carolina blue and reclaiming the Senate majority.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111425368/557183986/-782127407?amount=5&refcode=AU_GWN_FR_GEN_MMG_AC_20250713_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODMxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNTdmZWU0YS0yNDYwLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3inHIHLbGoMe_zfg4fBI22ad1qh4_NsT9WISa9jektg=)** _If you've saved payment information with ActBlue, your donation to Wiley Nickel will process immediately:_ **** | | **[DONATE $5 NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111425370/557183992/474294291?amount=5&express_lane=true&refcode=AU_GWN_FR_GEN_MMG_AC_20250713_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODMxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNTdmZWU0YS0yNDYwLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3inHIHLbGoMe_zfg4fBI22ad1qh4_NsT9WISa9jektg=)**

clean_body: We can’t let MAGA Republicans determine our path. | = Peter, working families in North Carolina deserve leaders who prioritize justice and ethical governance, not personal interests. Due to congressional Republican support of the Big Ugly Bill — including every single NC U.S. House Republican — over 600,000 of our friends and neighbors in North Carolina stand to lose their healthcare. Folks in nursing homes will be kicked out and rural hospitals will be shut down across the state — all to fund tax breaks for billionaires. We have to hold them accountable, and I’m running for U.S. Senate to do just that. Throughout my career, I’ve been committed to fighting corruption and advocating for policies that empower North Carolinians. From safeguarding voting rights to ensuring private healthcare decisions remain between patients and providers, I’ve consistently stood up for transparency and justice. To take this open seat and flip the Senate blue, we need a grassroots movement that’s ready to demand ethical governance and justice for all. = If you've saved payment information with ActBlue, your donation to Wiley Nickel will process immediately: ** | | = = | = = | = = We can’t let MAGA Republicans determine our path. body:
Gabe Vasquez won the most competitive Democratic seat in Congress in 2022. He's running to be a champion for working families and build an economy that benefits everyone. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/lNYIdkhPikOBrh6RGc-KWg2?unsubscribedata=jNw2GgjQecOmC%2BG%2B1NRNxPWEmKkrY6RAqD1kuMfO8WZ5SUjNICqZF%2FJ2T9CeJP4Glw3L%2BO0LjEvfet6%2BaCNSSDZT7AsK87%2FvqyK6d7nzWCwsnDEJ01rebSFuoSlPM2gQIJopI%2B12HAml60KRvl0a6Gu1JsA%2BAKI1cFuY3X05wcbCm2p0qnhn55p7cNOuE2KGm5P86%2BzNJ%2B536g6chNk0N0iuPki9qd%2F4yesJxEFnGfLtWuDqeummqmLD7xduFGoyZIIKMciB6oHnSW1sbMdA6ookpEHj1RNTXmE2PDE%2Fk44jnPg3bwrj%2BUrh2G7v1rvvWP8a4x9ptG97ml%2BKiqPtJNItuPM8MC%2BmiXb3htyZXBevhr7KAVgen5p45YHvRSi%2FEEshzaB7Z%2FqyuSJUTzNgXY0zbQMSIYa36hKqOXSAC9o%3D)

It’s Bill. I’ve been around politics a long time — and I’ve seen a lot. But I’ll tell you one thing: I’ve never seen anything as destructive as the bill Republicans just passed. Republicans just voted to rip away health care from working families, slash food assistance for kids, and they did it all with a big smile on their face — just to hand more tax breaks to the ultra-wealthy. I’ve seen budget fights and I’ve seen ugly politics, but I’ve never seen something this openly cruel. Gabe Vasquez is the opposite of that kind of politics. He’s a public servant in the truest sense — someone who listens to his community, fights for what’s right, and never forgets who he’s working for. We need leaders like him leading the fight **_right now._** [We need to send a message right now that we won’t forget what Republicans just did. clean_body: Gabe Vasquez won the most competitive Democratic seat in Congress in 2022. He's running to be a champion for working families and build an economy that benefits everyone. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | It’s Bill. I’ve been around politics a long time — and I’ve seen a lot. But I’ll tell you one thing: I’ve never seen anything as destructive as the bill Republicans just passed. Republicans just voted to rip away health care from working families, slash food assistance for kids, and they did it all with a big smile on their face — just to hand more tax breaks to the ultra-wealthy. I’ve seen budget fights and I’ve seen ugly politics, but I’ve never seen something this openly cruel. Gabe Vasquez is the opposite of that kind of politics. He’s a public servant in the truest sense — someone who listens to his community, fights for what’s right, and never forgets who he’s working for. We need leaders like him leading the fight right now. = | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now. | = | = | = | = | = | = Thanks for being in this fight with us, Bill McCamley Campaign Manager, Team Vasquez | | | ---|---|--- = Together, we flipped NM-02 in 2022. name: Mikie Sherrill
subject: 5 quick reasons
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24-sherril?refcode=250713em [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24-sherril?refcode=250713em]
It’s been a few weeks since I won the Democratic nomination for governor of New Jersey. But this race is already a rollercoaster — we’ve seen an outpouring of grassroots support and also faced a barrage of right-wing attacks.
Today, I need your help to stop MAGA Republicans from flipping New Jersey red, and I’m humbly asking you to split $5 between my team and Tobias Read to power our campaign to victory. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24-sherril?refcode=250713em] But if you’re not sure (which I understand!), here are five reasons I hope will convince you:
1) New Jersey is not a safe Democratic state anymore — far from it. The last governor’s election here? It was decided by 3%. New Jersey’s margin in the 2024 presidential election? 6%. The first polling in this race of the general election? It shows me leading my GOP opponent by only 3%.
2) Donald Trump is going to do everything he can to defeat me in this race. He’s already endorsed my opponent, Jack Ciattarelli, and led a rally for him — meaning MAGA money from all over the country is going to pour into Ciattarelli’s campaign.
3) Unlike my opponent, I won’t ever hesitate to stand up to Trump when he attacks New Jersey families. I’ve sworn an oath to the Constitution, and I’ve spent my career honoring it to the best of my ability every single day — before loyalty to any other politician or political party. Ciattarelli has already made clear that his number-one priority is staying in Trump’s good graces.
4) My top priority as governor will be making life more affordable for New Jersey families. I’ve put forward detailed plans for lowering the cost of everything from housing and energy to health care, child care, and groceries — and I’m going to hit the ground running on Day 1, making them a reality.
5) The stakes of this race are high not only for New Jersey but for the country. This is our chance to show the entire nation that the people reject the Trump administration’s incompetence and authoritarianism. And it’s our chance to get started on a better path forward — one where we prioritize doing what’s best for working families over what’s best for billionaires and corrupt political allies. There is no more important bellwether ahead of 2026.
If any of these reasons speak to you, I hope you will split a $5 donation between my team and Tobias Read to help me defeat my Trump-endorsed opponent in this incredibly competitive election to determine who leads our state for the next four years. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24-sherril?refcode=250713em]
DONATE NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24-sherril?refcode=250713em]
Thanks for reading, and have a great rest of your day.
-Mikie Sherrill
Tobias Read is Oregon's Secretary of State. To support him directly, please donate here. [https://www.tobiasread.com]
Mikie Sherrill is running for Governor of New Jersey. You can support her directly here. [https://www.mikiesherrill.com/]
https://www.facebook.com/TobiasReadOR [https://www.facebook.com/TobiasReadOR] https://twitter.com/TobiasRead [https://twitter.com/TobiasRead] https://instagram.com/tobiasreador [https://instagram.com/tobiasreador]

Friends of Tobias Read (5208)
PO Box 42307
Portland, OR 97242
United States
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/IMahE5FQZEyEjGesjn_XSA2?unsubscribedata=zP9Fwc7NMiiQFCOBgrk7fGj5F60JK9Nd4UNkF5h46Xsc%2FsRx9u9OSmvQrYA3dwQeZS732dN8KYlQ6EynuHvIG%2FZfE7NS20kuA0v6Xc1%2F8BjidBJwlxZ6WCUNDeiJQ1JGBm0Pk55b3tLnfiQSL53sS9LQpGX5J1WxVYXFkSDz1aWXiAJuK5Z1ZkoNXNOmRGN4uW8%2FsSEEKaXQIAItjCV0sj8Us3zj6mirkS6bERr%2BpRQzacaueiFqdenksI%2FxgWwLE1kfpr2kR9gJkKdrCS4h5aMSdvaSlm7W%2F8fGtgfUFXtHRadLrkRN3KuMkpI2l8Vm%2B5%2F5NI4cFjvnjR1L%2Byz1OziVnzD39TVzv6oDglADna1%2BJmSs%2FoE9aAZgjDjDxCq5gRbUxrYWEpSmbHAejfhAU%2ByZBbCwaujvDhayDJhXof3c3jIgMepjMb03JvlB1xeN .

Hi Peter, We’ve got our July mid-month fundraising deadline coming up in just a few days, and I want to take a moment to explain why we set these goals in the first place. It’s not about hitting some arbitrary number or checking a box. It’s about making sure we’ve got the resources to keep doing this work the right way: people-first, community-centered, and focused on real results. We’re building up this campaign with small-dollar donations from Oregonians who believe in what we’re fighting for. name: Team Jena
subject: Democracy is on the line
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jena for Colorado
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2507-q3-jg?refcode=250713-email [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2507-q3-jg?refcode=250713-email]
Peter –
Jena Griswold has never been afraid to stand up to Donald Trump — not when he spread the Big Lie after the 2020 election, and not when his extremist allies and followers tried to intimidate her for defending our democracy.
Each and every time, Jena has stood up for Coloradans and for our rights and freedoms.
Trump and his far-right allies are targeting our rights, our Constitution, and our democracy. As Attorney General, Jena will continue to defend the Colorado way of life against Trump’s unconstitutional actions and policies.
We need a strong grassroots movement to show that Jena’s campaign isn’t just about one race — it’s about protecting our values from a dangerous national agenda. Will you chip in right now to help Jena stand up to Trump and keep Colorado a beacon of justice and democracy? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2507-q3-jg?refcode=250713-email]
Thanks for being part of this fight.

clean_body: [] Peter – Jena Griswold has never been afraid to stand up to Donald Trump — not when he spread the Big Lie after the 2020 election, and not when his extremist allies and followers tried to intimidate her for defending our democracy. Each and every time, Jena has stood up for Coloradans and for our rights and freedoms. Trump and his far-right allies are targeting our rights, our Constitution, and our democracy. As Attorney General, Jena will continue to defend the Colorado way of life against Trump’s unconstitutional actions and policies. name: Mary Trump
subject: this fight is personal for me
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Seth Moulton for Congress
body:
| Friends, I never needed political pundits or psychologists to tell me that my uncle Donald shouldn’t be anywhere near the Oval Office. I spent much of my childhood in the very house where Donald grew up. The cruel, weak, and deeply insecure man who brought our democracy to the brink of disaster is the same man I have always known. _Seeing him sworn in for a second time was an absolute gut punch._ This fight is personal for me, and as more people lose their jobs or have their rights and freedoms trampled, it’s becoming personal for them, too. **I’m committed to fighting back, but I’m under no illusion about the uphill battle we face. We need everyone to speak up.[Will you split a $25 donation between my org Democracy Defense Fund and Seth Moulton to push back against my uncle and his dangerous, hateful agenda?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111426020/557185997/-204460493?refcode=250713_MaryTrump_DDF&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNGIzNWQxMi0yNzYwLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jEo0_NrjuZ9iNiw00uyKuRVvgk-5syk5PJzyH0k-9Dc=)** I launched the Democracy Defense Fund to build a grassroots movement to secure our electoral system from Donald and the authoritarian traitors who seek to destroy it. In order to finally defeat Donald once and for all, we must:
1. _**Flip the House.** The GOP’s majority is historically small and ripe for the taking, and if we retake the House, we can put a critical check on Donald’s power._

2. _**Take Back the Senate.** We need a Democratic majority in the Senate to fight back against judicial appointments and prevent Donald from securing a generation of MAGA rule._

3. _**Take the fight to the States.** We must continue to win critical gubernatorial elections and invest in flippable down-ballot races that have the potential to push back on Donald’s dangerous agenda through state legislatures._

4. _**Win in 2025.** Virginia, New Jersey, Louisiana, and Mississippi all have important legislative elections in 2025, and both New Jersey and Virginia will also hold gubernatorial elections._

**Winning big in these races would shift the narrative of GOP dominance and give Democrats serious momentum heading into a pivotal 2026 election cycle.** [**Can I count on you to help me build and grow the grassroots network we need to fight back against Donald, Elon Musk, and the GOP? If so, please split $25 between my org Democracy Defense Fund and Seth Moulton today!**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111426021/557185998/-204460493?refcode=250713_MaryTrump_DDF&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNGIzNWQxMi0yNzYwLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jEo0_NrjuZ9iNiw00uyKuRVvgk-5syk5PJzyH0k-9Dc=) In solidarity, Dr. Mary L. One year ago today, America nearly lost President Trump.

[](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/-cJfugo_RnDxoHxO9diF7_M_3PIwd41cqN2ifwLqFfCrhXBYFUJm7crqQSiZUt8G086C7_gIE891A88ruQC0C69HCD-pPS2pdIOQhHX6dnE8eseJD2Zu8drrczx3p40ULlqZMWvTCnbO9xKC7OEy4GockXddzWK54vYwyYGBCGAC10SzcMcPNsQ2qWHZ-WgruLX169R-GFFfcxquPCd-Kmw-JmYj3GkFjRVgSv-tMwn7vSa-HYshYcEl8vKamSB1cJxlxDzlgXGMLB7oZUee08qaKBz9JC0dQxxXPUGz5GFxh_AZb6J_mZQn6wYntExQFXlxwpN35jMyxdi2MH_OYELa7xM9IezWKsgFlTpRDDamZQO47gFHxvGq8DcpDVogb0WTtMVLN5SUPkyz4rT20qgOW33fsrDfKNbbvS0WmQltGNF9txi5TeyNcI9mYM7tvF_iWkCvwvxetbg--3_i1akMq1B2ErS1oqzxSx_IRMksrZcDqTzSJQgiH8PYgY6Jjr13jfjkLXAS29iKFPp9fRM9jHZIVZWbc3uiZJZGPycduKMaVSJ9JLjBviwZW0ZmMMmcOfs-QoD8dTrx4QZsyoLbyZaooVcM8SAL-BPA7XFNvoK7_DqYE2qGnzlUT9SD1JrBEDU0zPU5ZVPW7-dd41wz_737hd7ctavh63-sryJhzdcZ1azCMjLr_6EspfZNqhdo-ZqHOB8qA1dChR_xa0X4_Uakn_5L3Y1HE6cupYE1ZRGfR0J08TMBDOAEEqfD-91S/e2RbeQWpkCw3iCB8J1c1MQ3NXIpC4kHV/10)

**Peter** ,

Congressman Burlison here.

I don't write messages like this often. _**But today is not an ordinary day.

**_
Exactly one year ago--President Donald J. Trump **_was nearly assassinated_** in Butler, Pennsylvania.

A mere inch made the difference between life and death.

A mere inch could have changed the course of history.

The image of President Trump--his ear bloodied, Secret Service agents swarming, supporters screaming--will **_never_** leave my mind.

[](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/Vm1ALTkhvNmQKWVbdtH95PYI4O0EqX4G1DmGQdnz_avdeaWpLoJNLrQTbnsFFX8m_tUbpIbCmgmG2S-RX52DXFzg5qQ4m5oyxoqwnB6BIPoaj9TNFAcFBc7ij3o8LmbO30Y3mFXLL2P0pGonVpIuqRiyWMJsBp3Yo6ZE51yJvR0HjiavSknjEpU2VlzCdw6Ypqqb9sE_1Ny2GaQAmwoGf7_VPB2B76I-WZX8Wbp0jIO3U475bG3UcPR8jUenjauVjSHQESiMjVrcgu_Qdci5uwieMV35u37ndZ7sepS8YTz0iYvedlM5cHa-2J6qkQR20ft-A8Jh3Q2xooHHoo-OagM3xRqORZyZarQjJqjUO_bx2-gbqpYW1tPIqA4DyfcD-ot1x4KtPIBUx8_jTwhw5sh8MH2B_IufONWJt8kpivOXODM20vx6btR2IsfMbRuBhyGZBtM_dye9rPoq-1OKSUQy6NpGTqJEuPJIkd8NoheTWmLckGFyYn5JfsblOWR31XGC1M1wbe6WSTZKRumLvyYpwnfRD-oCjBVPA_rhtO4-qE5F51GgPQm4na4D50iGFP32jrzNGCk37GJ27fWeTyFg_6TtbXrMt74cu0Qfnyn-M5WlRghehgoTKZY3MQxjkacMyEtjKohQQPQy6kKtbUhr6bpVQIH3rUgBDnh53i0U5CSqx5DB96e9z1O_JoRG9flfuQetwwE-imJH7etz4Jj1lA34KcMNE_2cNtf5bj7xXpVX58vJJSY36k9oZrCzUElH/ukuAX5joItId3JJOLPCXNNP9nAf5nHhO/10)
And yet...

The media moved on.

The Left refused to condemn it.

And instead of cooling the temperature--Democrats doubled down, calling Trump a _**" threat to democracy"**_ and his supporters _**" extremists."

**_
Let me be clear:
👉 I stand with President Trump.
👉 I stand with the America First movement.
👉 And I will **NEVER** let the Radical Left silence our voice with violence.

But Patriot--this anniversary is a wake-up call.

Our majority in Congress is **hanging by a thread**.

The Radical Left is **bankrolling challengers** in swing seats.

And if they flip even a handful of districts--**it 's over.

**
We won't have the votes to secure the border.

We won't be able to protect your freedoms.

And we'll LOSE the firewall protecting President Trump from impeachment--_**again**.

_
That's why I need you now more than ever.

We have to fight like every seat matters--because it does.

If you stand with Trump, with me, and with the America First mission, chip in **ANY** amount today 👇

[RUSH A DONATION TO DEFEND OUR MAJORITY](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/nHm46wsT2NBFewsczyOB84w1EbqCE0OFAgj41-lEBfSQjwEdV3lEWoY2r5-Y-NspPxrH2A86ycqP2QX1xecnG3LBL-uDHMM5ZQGEvZVSjZosfHZ5W41kDtIO1n3eLE8_98nxjaiKrLtQjlBReI0MzFbTOl1JTzY0DjacsyuJaSTboTpWhKYFA8MhsX6quVll4L7Etb9rzuy1YYoO3kjpgOhG9-nSwq2udRi8SG1OMW3dMxGMd9Ah08WEnSFzEQve0oR4a9tXXmo2aPe7ikZZ6KNUCGwD-MqutfpWNo4SceqqVMixOQ6p4VAg2qQcWdV6yFDIsdp816Ilp2VxFFFZkHZfajackfnPR7zBFM57pZgUW-0zkT3khG0mu_J_FKb--gfKY4UFQd7PVteOibhPfc56XEx-446-9y1G-qztkeKt5t-TNcxOolnLD7kWIeVYbBGq0YiN5wfvsVify7JNKMC4jQfaKmcsOzvZUEdOLXE_qGay_x3tuaKNvrKR9RqJoS731-RUfIjzly0DUm1VyNdWbK4BTr-qah0BBaoKLZ9VB92Wxi7xarbbDwIwxzUwQuVfyTySYwjAQb3r6DfouUB_3jfLcCsRVuGzWRxNL4Faw8TESuQeFj7jfpt3CkIzBRebHQc9D0s6ftkG0lmq7b8UkwXEGbEIEc25PSQgGYsrj0dZq6MdBvhqxM5zC2V4tTU0k68HKION1RhsZlIEEAnou6fpGGTdovt7VvD2zOXBj5cZxPh684nBWomYz-2zfAFu/u0GUjELIyV_5oVAExGMoaRZFRSTX5SVq/10)

body:
About your local post office...
| **_Folks, your local USPS office might be Trump’s next target:_** [](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/save-usps/?source=VPP_20250713_EMPET_E3_T0_AC&t=6&akid=20560%2E878179%2E9_dHOS) | [**HELP US FIGHT BACK: SIGN YOUR NAME TO STAND WITH THE U.S. POSTAL SERVICE »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/save-usps/?source=VPP_20250713_EMPET_E3_T0_AC&t=7&akid=20560%2E878179%2E9_dHOS)
---
**FACT:** Donald Trump has been foaming at the mouth to PRIVATIZE and DESTROY the U.S. Postal Service as we know it,_and now he’s following through!_

**FACT:** The USPS recently confirmed that more than 10,000+ postal workers will be laid off — **_your local post office could be next!_**

**FACT:** Millions of Americans rely on the Postal Service for bills, Social Security checks, medication, and _more._ _We can’t let Trump get away with this!_
[**WE NEED A SURGE OF 15,000 DEMOCRATS TO STAND WITH USPS:
Add your name by MIDNIGHT and defend your local Post Office from MAGA destruction! »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/save-usps/?source=VPP_20250713_EMPET_E3_T0_AC&t=8&akid=20560%2E878179%2E9_dHOS)
[**I, A DEDICATED DEMOCRAT, STAND WITH THE POSTAL SERVICE! »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/save-usps/?source=VPP_20250713_EMPET_E3_T0_AC&t=9&akid=20560%2E878179%2E9_dHOS)
---
Thank you for taking action,
_The Voter Protection Project_
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=20560%2E878179%2E9_dHOS). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=20560%2E878179%2E9_dHOS).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=20560%2E878179%2E9_dHOS) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=10&akid=20560%2E878179%2E9_dHOS). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=20560%2E878179%2E9_dHOS)
---

Now, he's taking that work to the next level by serving as your Congressman, representing New York's 16th District. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | Peter, is there anything we can say to convince you to = | = What if we told you George and Congressional Democrats are doing everything they can to hold the line against the dangerous agenda being pushed by House Republicans and the Trump administration? Or what if we told you that Republicans are clinging onto the slimmest House majority in modern history, and our best bet to put a stop to this dangerous agenda is by flipping the House in 2026? And what if we told you that we’re falling short of our mid-month fundraising goal , and we need a surge of support from this grassroots team to catch up? With so much at stake, we can’t afford to fall short here. But here's the good news: This grassroots team has always stepped up, and with enough support, we can surge ahead and catch up in no time. = If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now. | = | = | = | = | = | = Team Latimer * | | ---|--- = For over three decades, George Latimer has been a champion for progressive policies and leaders, helping lead the effort to make Westchester, a formerly solid Republican County, into a blue firewall that has withstood multiple red waves in recent years. name: Deb Haaland
subject: This is an email about the lessons I learned as a community organizer in Indian country. I hope you’ll take a moment to read it and then chip in to support our campaign.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO
body:
Thank you in advance for your support as we keep traveling the state.
# [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/133?t=1&akid=3211%2E400544%2EBzF4xW)
From rural communities to the smallest Tribal Nations, **I believe every person in New Mexico should have a voice.**
That’s been one of my guiding principles as we build this campaign for governor, and it’s a lesson I learned long ago, when I first began my career as a community organizer.
Years before I was first elected to Congress or served as U.S. Secretary of the Interior, I was an organizer in Indian Country for the reelection of President Barack Obama. During that time, I traveled to every chapter of the Navajo Nation. I visited every Pueblo across New Mexico, big and small. The people I met on the campaign trail were eager to share their stories and their ideas. **But far too often, our leaders don’t make the effort to engage with underrepresented communities.**
**[ ](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250713_1_nobodyforgotten?amount=5&refcode=em20250713-3211&refcode2=3211_400544_BzF4xW&akid=3211%2E400544%2EBzF4xW) **
_We drove so many miles across the state in that little Honda, I thought the wheels might fall off._
Later on while I was the NM Democratic Party chair, I continued to travel to every inch of our great state. **I made it a point to go deep into Republican stronghold counties and rural towns to hear directly from people about their struggles.** And here’s the thing: we didn’t argue. We had powerful conversations that led to common ground. Those moments taught me that, once you overcome partisanship and division, most of us want the same things.
[ ](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250713_1_nobodyforgotten?amount=5&refcode=em20250713-3211&refcode2=3211_400544_BzF4xW&akid=3211%2E400544%2EBzF4xW)
_A campaign stop in Carlsbad, NM._
I believe we can’t only focus our energy on big cities while ignoring the people on the margins, and that we must be clear-eyed about the fact that Republicans have been gaining ground in all parts of New Mexico. **To win this race, we have to organize everywhere and leave no voice unheard. Because nobody wants to feel forgotten.**
That’s why our campaign has just launched the Lift Up New Mexico Tour. In the coming days and weeks, I’ll be traveling across our beautiful state once again to meet with more voters, listen to their stories, and understand how I can best serve the people of New Mexico as Governor.
But organizing statewide in a state as large as New Mexico is time-consuming and expensive work, which is why I’m counting on people like you.
[**Will you contribute your very first $5 to our campaign so we can build a powerful organizing team that can engage with voters in every county in New Mexico?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250713_1_nobodyforgotten?amount=5&refcode=em20250713-3211&refcode2=3211_400544_BzF4xW&akid=3211%2E400544%2EBzF4xW)
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250713_1_nobodyforgotten?amount=5&refcode=em20250713-3211&refcode2=3211_400544_BzF4xW&akid=3211%2E400544%2EBzF4xW)**
Thank you in advance for your support as we keep traveling the state. Showing up for each other is how we’ll win.
Be fierce,
Deb
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_footer?refcode=em20250713-1&t=2&refcode2=3211_400544_BzF4xW&akid=3211%2E400544%2EBzF4xW)**
* * *
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails-dh/?t=3&akid=3211%2E400544%2EBzF4xW)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/update-your-information-dh/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.debhaaland.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe-dh/?t=4&akid=3211%2E400544%2EBzF4xW)

Email us: [info@debhaaland.com](mailto:info@debhaaland.com)
PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO

name: Professor Randy Villegas
subject: Why I ran for for school board and what’s next:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Villegas for Congress [#]
body:
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP62/1/117525/images/Villegas%20Logo.png]
Hi there,

Before I ever ran for Congress, I was and still am a professor. I ran for school board because I was tired of hearing that the Central Valley was only known for the wrong kinds of statistics.

https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rvfc-em-q3-2025?refcode=em-7.13.25-all-a [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rvfc-em-q3-2025?refcode=em-7.13.25-all-a]

I know our students can thrive. I know our community can flourish. And I know we can reverse the outcomes that have held so many families back for far too long.

But that only happens if we have strong leadership that believes in our potential and fights for it like our lives depend on it.

I am where I am today because I had educators and leaders who believed in me, who believed in my potential.

Whether a student wants to go to a four-year university, the military, or straight into the workforce – I want them to have the schools, skills, and resources they need to succeed.

That’s exactly why I’m running for Congress.

Because I know the power of education. I know what happens when someone invests in our students and believes in their future. And I also know first-hand what’s at stake when Washington forgets about places like the Central Valley.

If you’re with me, chip in $3 today. Together, we can build a future where every student, every family, and every neighborhood has the opportunity to flourish and thrive. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rvfc-em-q3-2025?refcode=em-7.13.25-all-a]

DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rvfc-em-q3-2025?refcode=em-7.13.25-all-a]

Thank you,

Professor Randy Villegas
https://www.facebook.com/VillegasforCongress [https://www.facebook.com/VillegasforCongress] https://www.instagram.com/villegasforcongress/ [https://www.instagram.com/villegasforcongress/] https://x.com/Villegas_CA22 [https://x.com/Villegas_CA22][https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP62/1/117525/images/unnamed-2.png]With deep ties to our community and a lifelong commitment to justice and equity, Randy is running for Congress in California’s 22nd District to bring bold, compassionate leadership to Washington. To support Randy's campaign consider making a grassroots contribution here. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rvc-web-2025?refcode=em-footer]
We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/YVd1H8iXaku5biXQEwa1sQ2?unsubscribedata=Qc9z%2B2mWo7V%2BY%2Fbquo8HM5WPKr8VkVej%2F1yJnUAst457Va4Gn61mHNMNX0tPY%2Fj%2Buhzmj1yHdgSPJl%2F3u5aLf%2FU95vmvMoPtigJfZ96dCFtoLOLKkXVvJgSPMTeAYJyGssU7WkqxpSfu%2FYzPMxy64S5ql8r2kmmYneJW10NZNCvgch7qK8DZNNFLKtXatpLzlPaulB8owMS%2BRBsZ7GTenz%2FL%2FIA0DNJikAVXZL7Ky82EqDy62XAHnpfjduez0f1BjjZAi3AJE3SAMZlNhNXz%2BJGce4CY7yNCjwvfcRwilzHWXQiji6AHhzcwaTN0yf%2Bu2bCBzpuqMkVPFd7rIqQOaUbNsV%2FZ4ub9uZavO9Am5MS4rTT9jE7KNBhoGWMPAzCzNi6R4Jdyr79X%2FuviEx2odh0M2kaTSXT%2FA6SMDe1VVu%2F%2BjnHNqzVOc9h%2FvbcCmvXB .

Villegas for Congress

Visalia, CA 93279

This seems like something out of a twisted novel, folks, but **the new top voting lawyer at the Department of Justice was until recently an attorney and activist for a leading anti-voting legal group** that has worked for years to spread fear about unproven illegal voting and press election officials to implement stricter, suppressive voting rules. The lawyer, Maureen Riordan, worked side-by-side with Cleta Mitchell — the right-wing activist who played a key role in President Donald Trump’s failed bid to subvert the results of the 2020 election — following Mitchell’s pledge to “ _reclaim our election systems from the left._ ” This appointment, along with the posting of Trump campaign lawyer and election denier Harmeet Dhillon as head of the Civil Rights division, shows how DOJ has gone from a largely consistent defender of voting rights to instead working actively to undermine our fundamental rights. **Again, are we surprised? No.** But these moves from the administration highlight how badly **we need to fight for our fundamental rights as citizens** , from the outside of government. **[Please, chip in $25 or whatever you can today to help us defend the right to vote across the country, from state legislatures to courtrooms to the ballot box>>](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111426827/557188598/1723810624?am=25.00&contactdata=Q8KwsGZDpHZ2XFdJdOSqbIPYjS28bobqVLDu5GaihKooU/jbBWNQeWX6cZ+mv84N+yRDkveaRx3G2Ujj/bSJoSGIjBnCZX/9tu7O0G7qXjA=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5MjE1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjM0ZmFhZGNlLTJjNjAtZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=8QqoPSA-yijOdIXZgFX1AmcYdlJSr5aoq6z_c1jQcJE=&emci=0ab73288-0b60-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=34faadce-2c60-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=33603084)**

| | Riordan's first lawsuit at her new job? Filed recently against North Carolina, it cites Trump’s March anti-voter executive order and aims to strip legitimate voters from the rolls and force North Carolina to do more to collect unnecessary information that’s required by new, suppressive state laws, all from people newly registering to vote. This follows her work at the harmless-sounding Public Interest Legal Foundation (PILF), which is anything but. PILF specializes in pressuring states and counties — _by threatening and bringing expensive and frivolous lawsuits_ — to more aggressively remove voters from the rolls. As part of that effort, PILF has worked to stoke fear about the threat of non-citizen voting, which is extremely rare. Remember, the civil rights division of DOJ was created in 1957 -- often referred to as the “crown jewel” of the department -- has always been a cornerstone in the fight to protect our right to vote, not suing to restrict it or cut off access for voters seeking to exercise their rights. But Trump's nominees to DOJ have**sent out “mission statements” that made it clear the civil rights division was shifting its focus from protecting the civil rights of marginalized people to supporting Trump’s priorities and anti-voting conspiracy theories.** This administration is seemingly going to do everything in their power to attack our right to vote until they ensconce their control forever. We have to keep fighting back, no matter what the cost. **[folks, will you fuel our efforts to protect our right to vote and so much more with $25 or more right now?>>](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111426841/557188610/1723810624?am=25.00&contactdata=Q8KwsGZDpHZ2XFdJdOSqbIPYjS28bobqVLDu5GaihKooU/jbBWNQeWX6cZ+mv84N+yRDkveaRx3G2Ujj/bSJoSGIjBnCZX/9tu7O0G7qXjA=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5MjE1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjM0ZmFhZGNlLTJjNjAtZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=8QqoPSA-yijOdIXZgFX1AmcYdlJSr5aoq6z_c1jQcJE=&emci=0ab73288-0b60-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=34faadce-2c60-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=33603084)**

Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.

Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities. clean_body: DemDocket: DOJ’s New Top Voting Lawyer Worked for Leading Anti-Voting Law Firm| | | We can't wait for some hero to ride over the hill at full gallop to come save us, folks. It's up to us, our movement, and supporters like you to fight back with vote-suppressors taking over the Department of Justice's Civil Rights division. | | | This seems like something out of a twisted novel, folks, but the new top voting lawyer at the Department of Justice was until recently an attorney and activist for a leading anti-voting legal group that has worked for years to spread fear about unproven illegal voting and press election officials to implement stricter, suppressive voting rules. The lawyer, Maureen Riordan, worked side-by-side with Cleta Mitchell — the right-wing activist who played a key role in President Donald Trump’s failed bid to subvert the results of the 2020 election — following Mitchell’s pledge to “ reclaim our election systems from the left. ” This appointment, along with the posting of Trump campaign lawyer and election denier Harmeet Dhillon as head of the Civil Rights division, shows how DOJ has gone from a largely consistent defender of voting rights to instead working actively to undermine our fundamental rights. Again, are we surprised? No. But these moves from the administration highlight how badly we need to fight for our fundamental rights as citizens , from the outside of government. If you've saved your payment information with FastAction, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | | | | Riordan's first lawsuit at her new job? Filed recently against North Carolina, it cites Trump’s March anti-voter executive order and aims to strip legitimate voters from the rolls and force North Carolina to do more to collect unnecessary information that’s required by new, suppressive state laws, all from people newly registering to vote. This follows her work at the harmless-sounding Public Interest Legal Foundation (PILF), which is anything but. PILF specializes in pressuring states and counties — by threatening and bringing expensive and frivolous lawsuits — to more aggressively remove voters from the rolls. As part of that effort, PILF has worked to stoke fear about the threat of non-citizen voting, which is extremely rare. Remember, the civil rights division of DOJ was created in 1957 -- often referred to as the “crown jewel” of the department -- has always been a cornerstone in the fight to protect our right to vote, not suing to restrict it or cut off access for voters seeking to exercise their rights. But Trump's nominees to DOJ havesent out “mission statements” that made it clear the civil rights division was shifting its focus from protecting the civil rights of marginalized people to supporting Trump’s priorities and anti-voting conspiracy theories. This administration is seemingly going to do everything in their power to attack our right to vote until they ensconce their control forever. We have to keep fighting back, no matter what the cost. | | | | | | ---|---|---|--- Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country. Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities. Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code. A copy of Integrity First Voter Action Project's latest financial report may be obtained by mail at Integrity First Voter Action Project, c/o Mele Brengarth & Assoc, PO Box 15845, Washington, DC 20003.Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www.voteractionproject.org, and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.Integrity First Voter Action Project P.O. Box 19294 Portland, OR 97280 United States

subject: I voted no
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY DR. RAUL RUIZ FOR CONGRESS COMMITTEE
body:
I said no — now I need your help to fight back.

While Washington remains gridlocked, Dr. Raul Ruiz is committed to serving and helping constituents with the problems they have with the federal government and cutting red tape. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/dAfRehWDrkq5GdVTndjWgA2?unsubscribedata=15FrRuNoInJXUa4fF2L%2BsfXY2kACGFJg1pD3d0dlSkcKMjA%2BMrY9AfSmTJF9GfJ%2BP3Hh2ROuZ%2FQ6lH6Mi5RNteyNxlsJ%2FwrLxMnqJ4dJXKjH6%2F21Qn0EJitrLWiOVhBas2rKIoqW8PEwXQlPKvtOwuFMsrg53FU5sXloczIdcEKlMl8NH8fxcsD1Gjm7%2Fzg7VH7l4XYPG02V%2BytsEArcjzJq9w3%2B4%2Fl53J7gqRWdgn2cQdIKM7OKVNu5kN5qZT829Yk5ycbib%2FX9XgaTbIfDhDcEV9lqBduGCitLYnM%2BcswiHhCQgBTRreC1b1p6oDPCx3lT8IiNbE9LAa7zOCWU2O7CQftQSYi4mUEQoq4FPb5oNa9jMYRHOBrwWprBsF1tBuIlznEn%2F0BnG4xtfCeXqKFXzuYk8WNgTJcg2LLlcOBQQ0WgtTtmGkbKCeo9P6IC)

It's Raul. As a doctor, I’ve seen what happens when people fall through the cracks — when care is too far, too expensive, or simply out of reach. So when I saw what Donald Trump’s “Big Beautiful Bill” would do — to patients, to hospitals, to the next generation of doctors — there was no question. **I voted no.** I know what it means when a rural hospital shuts down. I know what it looks like when there aren’t enough doctors to go around. And now, because of this bill, students from working and middle-class families will be pushed out of medical school because they won't afford to pay. That’s not just bad policy — **it’s dangerous for communities like ours.** This bill will create chaos for communities across the country — all so billionaires can walk away with a massive tax break. CA-25 is critical to flipping the House in 2026 — and that’s what it’s going to take to stop Donald Trump from doing even more damage. [But we can only do it if we hold the line right now. We’re pushing to hit our $5,000 mid-month fundraising goal, and every single donation counts. The son of hardworking farmworkers, Dr. Ruiz grew up in Coachella, CA and learned at an early age that the key to attaining the American Dream is hard work and a great education. In the summer of 1990, Raul walked in the desert heat from business to business in the Coachella Valley asking them to invest in their community by contributing to his education. With each investment for college, he made a promise to come back home and serve his community as a physician. In 2012, he decided to take his service to the community to the next level by running for Congress. **Dr. Ruiz needs our help to continue fighting for us in Congress.**

clean_body: I said no — now I need your help to fight back. While Washington remains gridlocked, Dr. Raul Ruiz is committed to serving and helping constituents with the problems they have with the federal government and cutting red tape. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | It's Raul. As a doctor, I’ve seen what happens when people fall through the cracks — when care is too far, too expensive, or simply out of reach. So when I saw what Donald Trump’s “Big Beautiful Bill” would do — to patients, to hospitals, to the next generation of doctors — there was no question. I voted no. I know what it means when a rural hospital shuts down. I know what it looks like when there aren’t enough doctors to go around. And now, because of this bill, students from working and middle-class families will be pushed out of medical school because they won't afford to pay. That’s not just bad policy — it’s dangerous for communities like ours. This bill will create chaos for communities across the country — all so billionaires can walk away with a massive tax break. CA-25 is critical to flipping the House in 2026 — and that’s what it’s going to take to stop Donald Trump from doing even more damage. = If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately. | | = | = | = | = | = | = Thank you, Raul | | | ---|---|--- = The son of hardworking farmworkers, Dr. Ruiz grew up in Coachella, CA and learned at an early age that the key to attaining the American Dream is hard work and a great education. In the summer of 1990, Raul walked in the desert heat from business to business in the Coachella Valley asking them to invest in their community by contributing to his education. With each investment for college, he made a promise to come back home and serve his community as a physician. In 2012, he decided to take his service to the community to the next level by running for Congress. Dr. Ruiz needs our help to continue fighting for us in Congress.** | = = PAID FOR BY DR. name: Tim Kaine
subject: My strategy to combat Trump in the Senate:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025]
When Trump won the White House and the GOP secured a trifecta of power in Washington last November, one of the first things I did was ask my Senate staff to put together a list of all the procedural options I would have to counter a President I knew would be determined to push the bounds of his power.
They came up with a motley list of creative options, and in the past six months I’ve used pretty much every single one.
The challenging times we’re facing demand that we discover new ways to resist — and use them as often and as effectively as we can.
Expressing concerns is easy. But that’s often not enough when it comes to Donald Trump and his loyal followers.
If we have the power to stop something, we need to use it — even if that means taking a hard vote, or pushing our colleagues. If you agree, will you add your name to let me know? [https://secure.ngpvan.com/olYCM5sVtEqO-9l7zxYkAA2]
This year, I’ve triggered Senate votes on five privileged resolutions — meaning resolutions that the Senate is required to hold a vote on within a certain period of time. I’ve forced votes on repealing Trump’s fake “national energy emergency,” preventing Trump from taking further military action against Iran, and blocking Trump’s deeply unpopular global tariffs.
We haven’t won on everything — or even most things. But in April, we successfully voted to block President Trump’s ridiculous taxes on Canadian imports.
And even when we lose, these votes can serve a real purpose: They force every Senator to go on the record about Trump’s agenda. And they allow me — a Senator from the minority party — to control the Senate floor, taking up time that might otherwise be used to push through harmful legislation.
Introducing privileged resolutions isn’t a commonly-used tactic. But Donald Trump doesn’t employ commonly-used tactics either! Trump uses whatever tools and powers he can get his hands on to get the things done that he wants to get done — Democrats need to do the same.
I’m going to keep pushing in every way I can to combat this President’s dangerous agenda and protect the interests of Virginians and Americans — and I’m so glad to have you on this team as we continue to navigate these uniquely trying times.
Foundational values that we have taken for granted in the past — the rule of law, our democratic ideals — are being tested with this President and his acolytes in Congress and the Administration.
But we can and must continue to fight for what we believe in.
If you’re with me, I hope you’ll add your name today. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/olYCM5sVtEqO-9l7zxYkAA2]
Thanks for reading,
Tim Kaine
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025]
